flavorText: While grooming itself, it builds up fur inside its stomach. It sets the fur alight and spews fiery attacks, which change based on how it coughs.

name: Inkay
flavorText: Opponents who stare at the flashing of the light-emitting spots on its body become dazed and lose their will to fight.

name: Darkrai
rules: Enigma Berry: If Darkrai is damaged by an attack from your opponent's Fighting Pokémon, remove 4 damage counters at the end of that turn.

attacks.name: Spacial Rend
attacks.text: Search your deck for a Stadium card, show it to your opponent, and put it into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward. If there is any Stadium card in play, discard it.

attacks.name: Roar of Time
attacks.text: Search your discard pile for 3 Pokémon, show them to your opponent, and put them on top of your deck. Shuffle your deck afterward. (If you don't have 3 Pokémon in your discard pile, this attack does nothing.)

name: Fomantis

attacks.name: Sweet Scent
attacks.text: Heal 30 damage from 1 of your Pokémon.

flavorText: When the sun rises, Fomantis spreads its four leaves and bathes in the sunlight. The tip of its head has a pleasant aroma.

name: Team Aqua's Cacnea
rules: This Pokémon is both Grass Darkness type.

attacks.name: Paralyzing Gaze
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

attacks.name: Pin Missile
attacks.text: Flip 4 coins. This attack does 10 damage times the number of heads.

name: Simisear

attacks.name: Double Fire
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 80 damage times the number of heads.

flavorText: A flame burns inside its body. It scatters embers from its head and tail to sear its opponents.

attacks.name: Searing Flame
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Burned.

name: Litleo
flavorText: They set off on their own from their pride and live by themselves to become stronger. These hot-blooded Pokémon are quick to fight.

name: Simisear

attacks.name: Yawn
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Asleep.

attacks.name: Flamethrower
attacks.text: Discard a Fire Energy attached to this Pokémon.

flavorText: A flame burns inside its body. It scatters embers from its head and tail to sear its opponents.

name: Wailmer

attacks.name: Lullaby
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Asleep.

flavorText: On sunny days, it lands on beaches to bounce like a ball and play. It spouts water from its nose.

name: Team Magma's Groudon-EX
rules: Pokémon-EX rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

abilities.name: Power Saver
abilities.text: If there are 4 or fewer Team Magma Pokémon in play, this Pokémon can't attack.

attacks.name: Magma Quake
attacks.text: If your opponent's Active Pokémon already has any damage counters on it, this attack does 80 more damage.

name: Lapras

attacks.name: Aqua Bullet
attacks.text: This attack does 20 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

attacks.name: Hydro Pump
attacks.text: This attack does 10 more damage times the amount of Water Energy attached to this Pokémon.

flavorText: These Pokémon were once near extinction due to poaching. Following protective regulations, there is now an overabundance of them.

attacks.name: Super Singe
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Burned.

attacks.name: Lava Burn
attacks.text: Does 10 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

attacks.name: Sharp Claws
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 40 damage plus 10 more damage.

name: Smoochum

abilities.name: Sweet Sleeping Face
abilities.text: As long as Smoochum is Asleep, prevent all damage done to Smoochum by attacks.

attacks.name: Energy Antics
attacks.text: Move an Energy card attached to 1 of your opponent's Pokémon to another of your opponent's Pokémon. Smoochum is now Asleep.

flavorText: Its lips are the most sensitive part of its body. It always uses its lips first to examine things.

name: Machoke

attacks.name: Pummel
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 70 more damage.

flavorText: Its muscular body is so powerful, it must wear a power-save belt to be able to regulate its motions.

name: Rapidash

attacks.name: Overrun
attacks.text: This attack does 30 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: Very competitive, this Pokémon will chase anything that moves fast in the hopes of racing it.

name: Electrode

abilities.name: Buzzap
abilities.text: At any time during your turn (before your attack), you may Knock Out Electrode and attach it to 1 of your other Pokémon. If you do, choose a type of Energy. Electrode is now an Energy card of that type (instead of a Pokémon) that provides 2 energy. This power can't be used if Electrode is Asleep, Confused, or Paralyzed.

attacks.name: Electric Shock
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If tails, Electrode does 10 damage to itself.

flavorText: It stores electrical energy under very high pressure. It often explodes with little or no provocation.

abilities.name: Miracle Shift
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), discard a basic Energy card attached to 1 of your Pokémon. Then, choose a basic Energy card from your discard pile and attach it to that Pokémon. This power can't be used if Togetic is affected by a Special Condition.

attacks.name: Mini-Metronome
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, choose 1 of the Defending Pokémon's attacks. Mini-Metronome copies that attack except for its Energy cost. (You must still do anything else required in order to use that attack.) (No matter what type the Defending Pokémon is, Togetic is still Colorless.) Togetic performs that attack.

name: Omastar

attacks.name: Time Spiral
attacks.text: Choose 1 of your opponent's Evolved Pokémon. Remove the highest Stage Evolution card from that Pokémon and have your opponent shuffle that card into his or her deck.

attacks.name: Primal Tentacles
attacks.text: Does 30 damage plus 10 more damage for each Helix Fossil, Dome Fossil, and Old Amber in your discard pile.

flavorText: It is thought that this Pokémon became extinct because its spiral shell grew too large.

name: Mightyena δ
rules: This Pokémon is both Darkness Metal type.

attacks.name: Delta Fang
attacks.text: If the Defending Pokémon is Pokémon-ex, that Pokémon can't attack during your opponent's next turn.

attacks.name: Gang Up
attacks.text: Does 10 damage times the number of Darkness Pokémon and Metal Pokémon you have in play.

name: Arcanine

attacks.name: Flamethrower
attacks.text: Discard 1 Fire Energy card attached to Arcanine in order to use this attack.

attacks.name: Take Down
attacks.text: Arcanine does 30 damage to itself.

flavorText: A Pokémon that has been long admired for its beauty. It runs gracefully, as if on wings.

name: Tangrowth

attacks.name: Bind Down
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon can't retreat during your opponent's next turn.

attacks.name: Chlorowhip
attacks.text: If this Pokémon has at least 2 Grass Energy attached to it, heal 60 damage from this Pokémon.

flavorText: It ensnares prey by extending arms made of vines. Losing arms to predators does not trouble it.

name: Dark Charizard

attacks.name: Continuous Fireball
attacks.text: Flip a number of coins equal to the number of Fire Energy cards attached to Dark Charizard. This attack does 50 damage times the number of heads. Discard a number of Fire Energy cards attached to Dark Charizard equal to the number of heads.

flavorText: Seemingly possessed, it spews fire like a volcano, trying to burn all it sees.

name: Tirtouga
rules: Put this card onto your Bench only with the effect of Cover Fossil

abilities.name: Prehistoric Call
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), if this Pokémon is in your discard pile, you may put this Pokémon on the bottom of your deck.

attacks.name: Slam
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 30 damage times the number of heads.

flavorText: This Pokémon was restored from a fossil. It swam skillfully and dove to depths beyond half a mile.

name: Slurpuff

attacks.name: Cotton Guard
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, any damage done to this Pokémon by attacks is reduced by 30 (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

attacks.name: Sleepy Ball
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Asleep.

flavorText: It can distinguish the faintest of scents. It puts its sensitive sense of smell to use by helping pastry chefs in their work.

name: Lapras

attacks.name: Water Gun
attacks.text: Does 10 damage plus 10 more damage for each Water Energy attached to Lapras but not used to pay for this attack's Energy cost. You can't add more than 20 damage in this way.

attacks.name: Confuse Ray
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Confused.

flavorText: A Pokémon that has been overhunted almost to extinction. It can ferry people across water.

name: Alolan Ninetales-GX
rules: Pokémon-GX rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Ice Blade
attacks.text: This attack does 50 damage to 1 of your opponent's Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

attacks.name: Blizzard Edge
attacks.text: Discard 2 Energy from this Pokémon.

attacks.name: Ice Path-GX
attacks.text: Move all damage counters from this Pokémon to your opponent's Active Pokémon. (You can't use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)

attacks.name: Synchronize
attacks.text: This attack can't be used unless Sabrina's Abra and the Defending Pokémon have the same number of Energy cards attached to them.

name: Rillaboom VMAX
rules: VMAX rule: When your Pokémon VMAX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Max Beating
attacks.text: You may discard up to 3 Grass Energy from this Pokémon. If you do, this attack does 50 more damage for each card you discarded in this way.

name: Axew

attacks.name: Dragon Rage
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. If either of them is tails, this attack does nothing.

flavorText: They use their tusks to crush the berries they eat. Repeated regrowth makes their tusks strong and sharp.

name: Rotom

abilities.name: Mischievous Trick
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may switch 1 of your face-down Prize cards with the top card of your deck. This power can't be used if Rotom is affected by a Special Condition.

attacks.name: Plasma Arrow
attacks.text: Choose 1 of your opponent's Pokémon. This attack does 20 damage for each Energy attached to that Pokémon. This attack's damage isn't affected by Weakness or Resistance.

flavorText: Research continues on this Pokémon, which could be the power source of a unique motor.

name: Heatmor

attacks.name: Hot Lick
attacks.text: If the Defending Pokémon is Durant, this attack does 50 more damage.

attacks.name: Firebreathing
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 more damage.

flavorText: Using their very hot, flame-covered tongues, they burn through Durant's steel bodies and consume their insides.

abilities.name: Thick Fat
abilities.text: Any damage done to Grumpig by attacks from Fire Pokémon and Water Pokémon is reduced by 30 (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

attacks.name: Circular Steps
attacks.text: Does 10 damage times the number of Pokémon in play (both yours and your opponent's), excluding Grumpig.

name: Emboar-EX
rules: Pokémon-EX rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Spiral Punch
attacks.text: Flip a coin until you get tails. This attack does 20 more damage for each heads.

attacks.name: Strong Flare
attacks.text: Discard 2 Energy attached to this Pokémon.

name: Orbeetle V
rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Strafe
attacks.text: You may switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

attacks.name: Mysterious Wave
attacks.text: This attack does 30 more damage for each Energy attached to your opponent's Active Pokémon.

abilities.name: Pattern Distraction
abilities.text: As long as Spinda is your Active Pokémon, whenever your opponent's Basic Pokémon tries to attack, your opponent flips a coin. If tails, that attack does nothing. You can't use more than 1 Pattern Distraction Poké-Body each turn.

attacks.name: Whimsy Draw
attacks.text: Flip a coin until you get tails. For each heads, draw 2 cards.

attacks.name: Double-edge
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If tails, Spinda does 10 damage to itself.

name: Quilava

attacks.name: Mini Eruption
attacks.text: Discard the top card of your deck. If that card is an Energy card, this attack does 30 more damage.

flavorText: Be careful if it turns its back during battle. It means that it will attack with the fire on its back.

name: Meganium

attacks.name: Sleep Powder
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon is now Asleep.

attacks.name: Giant Bloom
attacks.text: Remove 2 damage counters from Meganium.

flavorText: Meganium's breath has the power to revive dead grass and plants. It can make them healthy again.

name: Blaziken

attacks.name: Clutch
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon can't retreat during your opponent's next turn.

attacks.name: Burning Shot
attacks.text: Discard 2 Energy attached to this Pokémon. This attack does 150 damage to 1 of your opponent's Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: It can clear a 30-story building in a leap. Its fiery punches scorch its foes.

name: Sawsbuck

attacks.name: Push Down
attacks.text: Your opponent switches the Defending Pokémon with 1 of his or her Benched Pokémon.

attacks.name: Take Down
attacks.text: This Pokémon does 20 damage to itself.

flavorText: The plants growing on its horns change according to the season. The leaders of the herd possess magnificent horns.

attacks.name: Bubble
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

attacks.name: Giant Claw
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.

name: Golduck

attacks.name: Encore
attacks.text: Choose 1 of the Defending Pokémon's attacks. That Pokémon can use only that attack during your opponent's next turn.

attacks.name: Break Beam
attacks.text: You may do 40 damage plus 20 more damage. If you do, Golduck is now Confused.

flavorText: A Pokémon that lives in lakes. It swims faster than any human swimming champion.

name: Pansear
flavorText: When it is angered, the temperature of its head tuft reaches 600° F. It uses its tuft to roast berries.

abilities.name: Baby Evolution
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may put Electabuzz from your hand onto Elekid (this counts as evolving Elekid) and remove all damage counters from Elekid.

attacks.name: Magnetic Trip
attacks.text: If Low Pressure System is in play, the Defending Pokémon is now Confused.

name: Alomomola

attacks.name: Super Deep Dive
attacks.text: Heal 30 damage from this Pokémon. Switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

flavorText: Floating in the open sea is how they live. When they find a wounded Pokémon, they embrace it and bring it to shore.

attacks.name: Needles
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed and Poisoned.

attacks.name: Sandstorm
attacks.text: If the Defending Pokémon tries to attack during your opponent's next turn, your opponent flips a coin. If tails, that attack does nothing.

name: Wobbuffet

attacks.name: Counter
attacks.text: If an attack damages Wobbuffet during your opponent's next turn (even if Wobbuffet is Knocked Out), flip a coin. If heads, Wobbuffet attacks the Defending Pokémon for an equal amount of damage.

flavorText: To keep its pitch-black tail hidden, it lives quietly in the darkness. It is never first to attack.

name: Drifblim

attacks.name: Wind Wave
attacks.text: Search your discard pile for up to 5 in any combination of Pokémon and Supporter cards. Show them to your opponent and shuffle them into your deck.

attacks.name: Explosive Smoke
attacks.text: Does 10 damage to each Benched Pokémon (both yours and your opponent's). (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: It's drowzy in daytime, but flies off in the evening in big groups. No one knows where they go.

name: Clefable

attacks.name: Metronome
attacks.text: Choose 1 of the Defending Pokémon's attacks. Metronome copies that attack except for its Energy costs and anything else required in order to use that attack, such as discarding Energy cards. (No matter what type the Defending Pokémon is, Clefable's type is still Colorless.)

attacks.name: Minimize
attacks.text: All damage done by attacks to Clefable during your opponent's next turn is reduced by 20 (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

flavorText: A timid Fairy Pokémon that is rarely seen. It will run and hide the moment it senses people.

name: Celebi ex
rules: When Pokémon-ex has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

abilities.name: Time Reversal
abilities.text: Once during your turn, when you put Celebi ex from your hand onto your Bench, you may search your discard pile for a card, show it to your opponent, and put it on top of your deck.

attacks.name: Psychic Shield
attacks.text: Prevent all effects of attacks, including damage, done to Celebi ex by your opponent's Pokémon-ex during your opponent's next turn.

name: Tyranitar

attacks.name: Payback
attacks.text: If your opponent has only 1 Prize card left, this attack does 40 damage plus 40 more damage and discard the top 3 cards from your opponent's deck.

attacks.name: Ground Burn
attacks.text: Each player discards the top card of his or her deck. This attack does 80 damage plus 20 more damage for each Energy card discarded in this way.

flavorText: If it rampages, it knocks down mountains and buries rivers. Maps must be redrawn afterward.

name: Mantine

attacks.name: Healing Wave
attacks.text: Discard as many cards as you like from your hand. Heal 10 damage from this Pokémon for each card you discarded in this way.

attacks.name: Dwindling Wave
attacks.text: This attack does 90 damage minus 10 damage for each damage counter on this Pokémon.

flavorText: While elegantly swimming in the sea, it ignores Remoraid that cling to its fins seeking food scraps.

name: Wimpod

attacks.name: Gnaw and Run
attacks.text: Switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

flavorText: It's nature's cleaner—it eats anything and everything, including garbage and rotten things. The ground near its nest is always clean.

abilities.name: Night Vision
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), if Sableye is your Active Pokémon, you may look at your opponent's hand. This power can't be used if Sableye is affected by a Special Condition.

attacks.name: Limitation
attacks.text: Your opponent can't play any Supporter Cards from his or her hand during your opponent's next turn.

attacks.name: Heat Tackle
attacks.text: Arcanine does 10 damage to itself.

abilities.name: Fire Immunity
abilities.text: You can't attach Fire Energy cards from your hand to Moltres.

attacks.name: Collect Fire
attacks.text: If there are any Fire Energy cards in your discard pile, flip a coin. If heads, attach 1 of them to Moltres.

attacks.name: Burning Tail
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If tails, discard a Fire Energy card attached to Moltres.

name: Quagsire

abilities.name: Laid-Back
abilities.text: Any damage done to this Pokémon by attacks is reduced by 20 (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

attacks.name: Mud Gun
attacks.text: If this Pokémon has any Fighting Energy attached to it, this attack does 30 more damage.

flavorText: It has an easygoing nature. It doesn't care if it bumps its head on boats and boulders while swimming.

name: Primeape

attacks.name: Bebop Punch
attacks.text: Choose 1 of your opponent's Pokémon. Flip a coin until you get tails. This attack does 50 damage times the number of heads to that Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: If approached while asleep, it may awaken and angrily give chase in a groggy state of semi-sleep.

name: Light Ledian

attacks.name: Flash Touch
attacks.text: If you have any Benched Pokémon, switch 1 of them with Light Ledian. As long as that Pokémon is your Active Pokémon, it can't become Asleep, Confused, Paralyzed, or Poisoned. (All other effects of attacks, Pokémon Powers, and Trainer cards still happen.)

attacks.name: Comet Punch
attacks.text: Flip 4 coins. This attack does 20 damage times the number of heads.

flavorText: The number of spots on its back increase or decrease depending on the number of stars visible in the night sky.

name: Regigigas

abilities.name: Slow Start
abilities.text: Regigigas can't attack until your opponent has 3 or less Prize cards left.

attacks.name: Crush Grip
attacks.text: If the Defending Pokémon already has any damage counters on it, this attack's base damage is 40 instead of 120.

flavorText: There is an enduring legend that states this Pokémon towed continents with ropes.

name: Kyurem-EX
rules: Pokémon-EX rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Glaciate
attacks.text: This attack does 30 damage to each of your opponent's Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

attacks.name: Icecalibur
attacks.text: Discard an Energy attached to this Pokémon. The Defending Pokémon can't attack during your opponent's next turn.

name: Ekans

attacks.name: Wrap
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, discard an Energy from your opponent's Active Pokémon.

flavorText: By dislocating its jaw, it can swallow prey larger than itself. After a meal, it curls up and rests.

name: Dark Dragonite

abilities.name: Summon Minions
abilities.text: When you play Dark Dragonite from your hand, search your deck for up to 2 Basic Pokémon and put them onto your Bench. Shuffle your deck afterward.

attacks.name: Giant Tail
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.

flavorText: Sometimes called "The God of Destruction." Its wings are able to support it in spite of its massive weight.

name: Grubbin

attacks.name: Electrical Signal
attacks.text: Search your deck for up to 2 Lightning Pokémon, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

flavorText: If you find its nest, you shouldn't stick your hand inside. You'll get bitten by an irritated Grubbin.

attacks.name: Aqua Wave
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 10 damage plus 20 more damage.

attacks.name: Dragonbreath
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

abilities.name: Stages of Evolution
abilities.text: As long as Hitmonchan is an Evolved Pokémon, Hitmonchan gets +30 HP.

attacks.name: Heavy Punch
attacks.text: Does 10 damage times the number of your opponent's Benched Pokémon.

attacks.name: Speedy Uppercut
attacks.text: This attack's damage isn't affected by Weakness, Resistance, Poké-Powers, Poké-Bodies, or any other effects on the Defending Pokémon.

name: Gogoat

attacks.name: Push Down
attacks.text: Your opponent switches his or her Active Pokémon with 1 of his or her Benched Pokémon.

attacks.name: Forest Press
attacks.text: Flip a coin for each Grass Energy attached to this Pokémon. This attack does 30 more damage for each heads.

flavorText: It can tell how its trainer is feeling by subtle shifts in the grip on its horns. This empathetic sense lets them run as if one being.

name: Cinderace V
rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

abilities.name: Field Runner
abilities.text: If a Stadium is in play, this Pokémon has no Retreat Cost.

abilities.name: Sinkhole
abilities.text: If your opponent's Active Pokémon retreats, put 2 damage counters on that Pokémon.

attacks.name: Push Down
attacks.text: Your opponent switches the Defending Pokémon with 1 of his or her Benched Pokémon.

attacks.name: Magnitude
attacks.text: Does 10 damage to each Benched Pokémon (both yours and your opponent's). (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

name: Electrode

attacks.name: Chain Lightning
attacks.text: If the Defending Pokémon isn't Colorless, this attack does 10 damage to each Benched Pokémon of the same type as the Defending Pokémon (including your own). (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: It stores electrical energy under very high pressure. It often explodes with little or no provocation.

attacks.name: Scorpion Grapple
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed. If tails, the Defending Pokémon can't retreat during your opponent's next turn.

attacks.name: Cross Poison
attacks.text: Flip 4 coins. This attack does 20 damage times the number of heads. If 2 or more of them are heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned.

attacks.name: Derail
attacks.text: Discard all Special Energy cards attached to the Defending Pokémon.

name: Pignite

attacks.name: Flame Charge
attacks.text: Search your deck for a Fire Energy card and attach it to this Pokémon. Shuffle your deck afterward.

flavorText: When its internal fire flares up, its movements grow sharper and faster. When in trouble, it emits smoke.

name: Larvesta
flavorText: This Pokémon was believed to have been born from the sun. When it evolves, its entire body is engulfed in flames.

name: Granbull

attacks.name: Timid Tackle
attacks.text: Granbull does 20 damage to itself. Switch Granbull with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

attacks.name: Chomp
attacks.text: Does 40 damage plus 10 more damage for each damage counter on Granbull.

flavorText: Because its fangs are too heavy, it always keeps its head tilted down. However, its bite is powerful.

name: Reshiram

attacks.name: Outrage
attacks.text: This attack does 10 more damage for each damage counter on this Pokémon.

attacks.name: Scorching Breath
attacks.text: This Pokémon can't attack during your next turn.

flavorText: This legendary Pokémon can scorch the world with fire. It helps those who want to build a world of truth.

name: Floatzel

attacks.name: Rescue
attacks.text: Shuffle 3 Pokémon from your discard pile into your deck.

attacks.name: Screw Tail
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, discard an Energy attached to your opponent's Active Pokémon.

flavorText: It floats using its well-developed flotation sac. It assists in the rescues of drowning people.

abilities.name: Energy Drain
abilities.text: If an opponent's attack does damage to Koga's Muk (even if Koga's Muk is Knocked Out), flip a coin. If heads and if it has any, choose 1 Energy card attached to the attacking Pokémon and discard it. This power can't be used if Koga's Muk is already Asleep, Confused, or Paralyzed when your opponent attacks.

name: Golem

attacks.name: Double Throw
attacks.text: Choose 2 of your opponent's Pokémon. This attack does 30 damage to each of them. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

attacks.name: Megaton Rock
attacks.text: You may do 40 damage instead of 80 to the Defending Pokémon. If you do, during your opponent's next turn, any damage done to Golem by attacks is reduced by 40 (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

flavorText: It sheds its hide once a year. Its boulderlike body is so tough, even dynamite can't harm it.

name: Pansear
flavorText: Very intelligent, it roasts berries before eating them. It likes to help people.

abilities.name: Backup
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), if you have less than 6 cards in your hand, you may draw cards until you have 6 cards in your hand. This power can't be used if Porygon2 is affected by a Special Condition.

attacks.name: Machine Burst
attacks.text: If Porygon2 has a Technical Machine card attached to it, the Defending Pokémon is now Asleep and Burned.

abilities.name: Dark Spell
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), if Misdreavus is your Active Pokémon, you may flip a coin. If heads, put 1 damage counter on 1 of your opponent's Pokémon. This power can't be used if Misdreavus is affected by a Special Condition or if your other Active Pokémon is not Misdreavus.

attacks.name: Hide in Shadows
attacks.text: Switch Misdreavus with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

name: Pyroar

abilities.name: Unnerve
abilities.text: Whenever your opponent plays an Item or Supporter card from their hand, prevent all effects of that card done to this Pokémon.

attacks.name: Dominating Fangs
attacks.text: If Lysandre Labs is in play, this attack does 60 more damage.

flavorText: With fiery breath of more than 10,000 degrees Fahrenheit, they viciously threaten any challenger. The females protect the pride's cubs.

name: Eldegoss V
rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

abilities.name: Happy Match
abilities.text: When you play this Pokémon from your hand onto your Bench during your turn, you may put a Supporter card from your discard pile into your hand.

attacks.name: Float Up
attacks.text: You may shuffle this Pokémon and all attached cards into your deck.

attacks.name: Super Singe
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Burned.

name: Wailord

attacks.name: Water Cannon
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 30 damage times the amount of Water Energy attached to this Pokémon.

attacks.name: Giant Wave
attacks.text: This Pokémon can't use Giant Wave during your next turn.

flavorText: The biggest of all Pokémon. It can dive to a depth of almost 10,000 feet on only one breath.

name: Spiritomb

abilities.name: Curse Breath
abilities.text: Once during your turn, when you put Spiritomb from your hand onto your Bench, you may put 1 damage counter on all Pokémon that already have any damage counters on them (both yours and your opponent's). You can't use more than 1 Curse Breath Poké-Power each turn.

attacks.name: Transfer Pain
attacks.text: Move 1 damage counter from 1 of your Pokémon to 1 of your opponent's Pokémon.

flavorText: A Pokémon that was formed by 108 spirits. It is bound to a fissure in an odd keystone.

name: Magikarp

abilities.name: Submerge
abilities.text: As long as this Pokémon is on your Bench, prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks (both yours and your opponent's).

attacks.name: Waterfall Evolution
attacks.text: Search your deck for a card that evolves from this Pokémon and put it onto this Pokémon to evolve it. Then, shuffle your deck.

flavorText: Although weak and helpless, this Pokémon is incredibly fertile. They exist in such multitudes, you'll soon grow tired of seeing them.

name: Pignite

attacks.name: Firebreathing
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 more damage.

flavorText: Whatever it eats becomes fuel for the flame in its stomach. When it is angered, the intensity of the flame increases.

name: Golisopod

attacks.name: Hard Times Slash
attacks.text: This attack does 50 more damage for each of your opponent's Pokémon V and Pokémon-GX in play.

attacks.name: Smash Turn
attacks.text: Switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

flavorText: It will do anything to win, taking advantage of every opening and finishing opponents off with the small claws on its front legs.

name: Ponyta

attacks.name: Agility
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, prevent all effects of attacks, including damage, done to this Pokémon during your opponent's next turn.

flavorText: As a newborn, it can barely stand. However, through galloping, its legs are made tougher and faster.

name: Voltorb

attacks.name: Thunder Wave
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

attacks.name: Big Explosion
attacks.text: This Pokémon does 60 damage to itself.

flavorText: Usually found in power plants. Easily mistaken for a Poké Ball, it has zapped many people.

abilities.name: Energy Grounding
abilities.text: Once during your opponent's turn, when any of your Pokémon is Knocked Out by your opponent's attacks, you may use this power. Choose a basic Energy card discarded from the Knocked Out Pokémon and attach it to Lanturn. You can't use more than 1 Energy Grounding Poké-Power each turn.

attacks.name: Lightning Strike
attacks.text: You may discard all Lightning Energy attached to Lanturn. If you do, this attack's base damage is 90 instead of 50.

attacks.name: Chime
attacks.text: Search your opponent's discard pile for a Supporter card and use the effect of that card as the effect of this attack. (The Supporter card remains in your opponent's discard pile.)

attacks.name: Psychic Boom
attacks.text: Does 10 damage times the amount of Energy attached to the Defending Pokémon.

name: Fennekin
flavorText: Eating a twig fills it with energy, and its roomy ears give vent to air hotter than 390 degrees Fahrenheit.

name: Heatmor

attacks.name: Singe
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon is now Burned.

attacks.name: Incinerate
attacks.text: Before doing damage, discard a Pokémon Tool card attached to the Defending Pokémon.

flavorText: Using their very hot, flame-covered tongues, they burn through Durant's steel bodies and consume their insides.

attacks.name: Battle Blast
attacks.text: Does 40 damage plus 10 more damage for each Fighting Energy attached to Breloom.

name: Simisear

attacks.name: Flame Burst
attacks.text: Does 20 damage to 2 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

attacks.name: Fury Swipes
attacks.text: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 40 damage times the number of heads.

flavorText: It loves sweets because they become energy for the fire burning inside its body.

abilities.name: Hover Lift
abilities.text: You pay Colorless less to retreat your Jigglypuff, Wigglytuff, Wigglytuff ex, and Igglybuff.

abilities.name: Baby Evolution
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may put Jigglypuff from your hand onto Igglybuff (this counts as evolving Igglybuff) and remove all damage counters from Igglybuff.

name: Cacturne

abilities.name: Poison Payback
abilities.text: If this Pokémon is your Active Pokémon and is damaged by an opponent's attack (even if this Pokémon is Knocked Out), the Attacking Pokémon is now Poisoned.

attacks.name: Feint Attack
attacks.text: This attack does 50 damage to 1 of your opponent's Pokémon. This damage isn't affected by Weakness, Resistance, or any other effects on that Pokémon.

flavorText: Packs of them follow travelers through the desert until the travelers can no longer move.

name: Bronzong

abilities.name: Cursed Alloy
abilities.text: As long as Bronzong is your Active Pokémon, put 1 damage counter on each of your opponent's Pokémon that has any Poké-Powers between turns.

attacks.name: Pain Amplifier
attacks.text: Put 1 damage counter on each of your opponent's Pokémon that already has damage counters on it.

attacks.name: Coating
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, any damage done to Bronzong by attacks is reduced by 20 (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

flavorText: One caused a news sensation when it was dug up at a construction site after a 2,000-year sleep.

name: Charmeleon

attacks.name: Flamethrower
attacks.text: Discard 1 Fire Energy card attached to Charmeleon in order to use this attack.

flavorText: When it swings its burning tail, it raises the temperature to unbearably high levels.

name: Tentacool

attacks.name: Sludge Shock
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon can't be healed during your opponent's next turn.

flavorText: They can be found lying dehydrated on beaches, but they are often still alive. When soaked in water, they will revive.

name: Flareon

abilities.name: Power Cheer
abilities.text: The attacks of your Pokémon-GX in play that evolve from Eevee do 30 more damage to your opponent's Active Pokémon (before applying Weakness and Resistance). You can't apply more than 1 Power Cheer Ability at a time.

attacks.name: Flamethrower
attacks.text: Discard an Energy from this Pokémon.

flavorText: If it inhales deeply, that's a sign it's about to attack. Prepare to be hit by flames of over 3,000 degrees Fahrenheit!

name: Roserade

abilities.name: Energy Signal
abilities.text: When you attach a Grass Energy card or Psychic Energy card from your hand to Roserade during your turn, you may use this power. If you attach a Grass Energy card, the Defending Pokémon is now Confused. If you attach a Psychic Energy card, the Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned. This power can't be used if Roserade is affected by a Special Condition.

attacks.name: Power Blow
attacks.text: Does 20 damage times the amount of Energy attached to Roserade.

flavorText: Its sweet aroma attracts prey. Then it spews poison. The more toxic it is, the sweeter its aroma.

name: Magcargo

ancientTrait.name: Ω Barrier
ancientTrait.text: Whenever your opponent plays a Trainer card (excluding Pokémon Tools and Stadium cards), prevent all effects of that card done to this Pokémon.

attacks.name: Indomitable Blaze
attacks.text: If your opponent's Active Pokémon is a Pokémon-EX, this attack does 60 more damage.

flavorText: Its body is as hot as lava and is always billowing. Flames will occasionally burst from its shell.

name: Pelipper

attacks.name: Water Pulse
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon is now Asleep.

attacks.name: Continuous Crush
attacks.text: Flip a coin until you get tails. For each heads, discard an Energy card attached to the Defending Pokémon.

flavorText: It is a messenger of the skies, carrying small Pokémon and eggs to safety in its bill.

name: Sceptile-GX
rules: Pokémon-GX rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Mach Cut
attacks.text: Discard a Special Energy from your opponent's Active Pokémon.

attacks.name: Leaf Cyclone
attacks.text: Move a Grass Energy from this Pokémon to 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

attacks.name: Jungle Heal-GX
attacks.text: Heal all damage from each of your Pokémon that has any Grass Energy attached to it. (You can't use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)

name: Shuppet
flavorText: It loves vengeful emotions and hangs in rows under the eaves of houses where vengeful people live.

name: Tropical Wind
rules: Flip a coin. If heads, remove 2 damage counters from each Active Pokémon (remove 1 damage counter if a Pokémon has only 1). If tails, each Active Pokémon is now Asleep.

name: Torracat

attacks.name: Fury Swipes
attacks.text: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 20 damage for each heads.

attacks.name: Flamethrower
attacks.text: Discard an Energy from this Pokémon.

flavorText: At its throat, it bears a bell of fire. The bell rings brightly whenever this Pokémon spits fire.

name: Kingler

attacks.name: Scissor Blow
attacks.text: If the Defending Pokémon would be be Knocked Out by this attack, Kingler does 30 damage to itself.

flavorText: The larger pincer has 10,000-horsepower strength. However, it is so heavy, it is difficult to aim.

name: Carracosta

attacks.name: Fossil Clutch
attacks.text: You may discard an Item card that has Fossil in its name from your hand. If you do, this attack does 50 more damage.

flavorText: It could knock out a foe with a slap from one of its developed front appendages and chew it up, shell or bones and all.

attacks.name: Sing
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon is now Asleep.

attacks.name: Egg Surprise
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, Chansey does 50 damage to the Defending Pokémon. If tails, remove 5 damage counters from Chansey. (All if there are less than 5.)

name: Lapras

attacks.name: Ferry
attacks.text: Search your discard pile for a Supporter card, show it to your opponent, and put it into your hand.

flavorText: It ferries people across the sea on its back. It may sing an enchanting cry if it is in a good mood.

attacks.name: Alert
attacks.text: Draw a card. Then, switch Brock's Zubat with 1 of your Benched Pokémon. You can't use this attack if your Bench is empty.

name: Heatmor

attacks.name: Luring Flame
attacks.text: Switch 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon with the Defending Pokémon. The new Defending Pokémon is now Burned.

attacks.name: Fiery Licks
attacks.text: Discard the top 4 cards of your deck. This attack does 50 damage times the number of Fire Energy cards discarded.

flavorText: It draws in air through its tail, transforms it into fire, and uses it like a tongue. It melts Durant and eats them.

attacks.name: Smash Turn
attacks.text: After your attack, you may switch Dodrio with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

name: Lurantis

attacks.name: Petal Blizzard
attacks.text: This attack does 10 damage to each of your opponent's Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

attacks.name: Sol Slice
attacks.text: If this Pokémon has any Fire Energy attached to it, this attack does 50 more damage.

flavorText: For self-protection, it pretends to be a bug Pokémon. Both of its arms bear keen-edged petals.

abilities.name: Sticky Hold
abilities.text: If Gastrodon East Sea is switched or retreats to your Bench, move as many Energy cards attached to Gastrodon East Sea as you like to the new Active Pokémon.

attacks.name: Calling Wave
attacks.text: Search your deck for up to 2 Gastrodon and put them onto your Bench as Basic Pokémon. Put 2 damage counters on each of them. Shuffle your deck afterward.

name: Team Aqua's Poochyena

attacks.name: Roar
attacks.text: Your opponent switches his or her Active Pokémon with 1 of his or her Benched Pokémon.

flavorText: Poochyena can easily detect invading foes with its acute sense of smell. It's an excellent lookout!

name: Pawniard
flavorText: Blades comprise this Pokémon's entire body. If battling dulls the blades, it sharpens them on stones by the river.

name: Claydol

attacks.name: Antique Magic
attacks.text: Put 3 damage counters on any Pokémon (both yours and your opponent's) in any way you like.

attacks.name: Synchro Attack
attacks.text: If the Defending Pokémon has the same remaining HP as Claydol, this attack's base damage is 90 instead of 30.

flavorText: An ancient clay figurine that came to life as a Pokémon from exposure to a mysterious ray of light.

name: Lapras

attacks.name: Water Arrow
attacks.text: This attack does 20 damage to 1 of your opponent's Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: It loves crossing the sea with people and Pokémon on its back. It understands human speech.

name: Glaceon

abilities.name: Freeze Zone
abilities.text: The Retreat Cost of each of your Team Plasma Pokémon in play is ColorlessColorless less.

attacks.name: Icy Wind
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon is now Asleep.

flavorText: It lowers its body heat to freeze its fur. The hairs then become like needles it can fire.

abilities.name: Evolution Helper
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), if Nidoqueen is on your Bench, you may search your deck for a card that evolves from your Active Pokémon and attach it to your Active Pokémon. (This counts as evolving that Pokémon.) Shuffle your deck afterward.

attacks.name: Double Claw
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 30 damage plus 20 more damage for each heads

name: Psyduck

attacks.name: Headache
attacks.text: Your opponent can't play Trainer cards during his or her next turn.

attacks.name: Fury Swipes
attacks.text: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 10 damage times the number of heads.

flavorText: While lulling its enemies with its vacant look, this wily Pokémon will use psychokinetic powers.

attacks.name: Triple Breach
attacks.text: Choose 3 of your opponent's Pokémon. This attack does 10 damage to each of those Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

attacks.name: Rend
attacks.text: If the Defending Pokémon already has any damage counters on it, this attack does 30 damage plus 20 more damage.

name: Manectric-EX
rules: Pokémon-EX rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Overrun
attacks.text: This attack does 20 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

attacks.name: Assault Laser
attacks.text: If your opponent's Active Pokémon has a Pokémon Tool card attached to it, this attack does 60 more damage.

name: Totodile

attacks.name: Leer
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

attacks.name: Fury Swipes
attacks.text: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 10 damage for each heads.

flavorText: Its powerful, well-developed jaws are capable of crushing anything. Even its Trainer must be careful.

name: Typhlosion

abilities.name: Fire Boost
abilities.text: When you play Typhlosion from your hand, you may flip a coin. If heads, search your deck for up to 4 Fire Energy cards and attach them to Typhlosion. Shuffle your deck afterward.

attacks.name: Flame Wheel
attacks.text: Discard 3 Fire Energy cards attached to Typhlosion in order to use this attack. Do 20 damage to each Benched Pokémon (yours and your opponent's). (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: It has a secret, devastating move. It rubs its blazing fur together to cause huge explosions.

name: Persian

attacks.name: Sharpen Claws
attacks.text: Flip 3 coins. For each heads, discard a card from your opponent's hand without looking.

attacks.name: Sneaky Attack
attacks.text: If Persian has any Darkness Energy attached to it, this attack does 30 damage plus 30 more damage.

flavorText: Its lithe muscles allow it to walk without making a sound. It attacks in an instant.

name: Typhlosion

attacks.name: Massive Eruption
attacks.text: Discard the top 5 cards of your deck. This attack does 80 damage times the number of Energy cards you discarded.

attacks.name: Flare Destroy
attacks.text: Discard an Energy attached to this Pokémon. Then, discard an Energy attached to your opponent's Active Pokémon.

flavorText: It has a secret, devastating move. It rubs its blazing fur together to cause huge explosions.

attacks.name: Burning Venom
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon is now Burned and Poisoned.

attacks.name: Strangle
attacks.text: If the Defending Pokémon has δ on its card, this attack does 50 damage plus 30 more damage.

name: Torkoal V
rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Combustion Pillar
attacks.text: Discard the top card of your deck. If that card is a Fire Energy card, this attack does 90 more damage.

attacks.name: Steam Crush
attacks.text: Discard 2 Energy from your opponent's Active Pokémon.

name: Blastoise

abilities.name: Powerful Squall
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may look at the top 6 cards of your deck and attach any number of Water Energy cards you find there to your Pokémon in any way you like. Shuffle the other cards back into your deck.

attacks.name: Hydro Tackle
attacks.text: This Pokémon does 30 damage to itself.

flavorText: It crushes its foe under its heavy body to cause fainting. In a pinch, it will withdraw inside its shell.

name: Rotom

attacks.name: Energy Extract
attacks.text: Search your deck for a basic Energy card and attach it to this Pokémon. Shuffle your deck afterward.

attacks.name: Plasmagic
attacks.text: Move 2 damage counters from each of your Pokémon to your opponent's Active Pokémon.

flavorText: Its body is composed of plasma. It is known to infiltrate electronic devices and wreak havoc.

name: Poliwag

attacks.name: Spiral Current
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Confused. That Pokémon can't retreat during your opponent's next turn.

flavorText: Its slick black skin is thin and damp. A part of its internal organs can be seen through the skin as a spiral pattern.

name: Articuno

attacks.name: Ice Beam
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: A legendary bird Pokémon. It can create blizzards by freezing moisture in the air.

name: Shuckle

abilities.name: Fermenting Liquid
abilities.text: Whenever you attach an Energy card from your hand to Shuckle, draw a card.

attacks.name: Shell Stunner
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, prevent all damage done to Shuckle by attacks during your opponent's next turn.

flavorText: It stores berries inside its shell. To avoid attacks, it hides beneath rocks and remains completely still.

name: Braixen

attacks.name: Clairvoyant Eye
attacks.text: Look at the top 3 cards of your deck and put them back on top of your deck in any order.

attacks.name: Fire Tail Slap
attacks.text: Discard a Fire Energy attached to this Pokémon.

flavorText: It has a twig stuck in its tail. With friction from its tail fur, it sets the twig on fire and launches into battle.

name: Dustox

attacks.name: Stun Spore
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

attacks.name: Toxic Dust
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned. Put 2 damage counters instead of 1 on the Defending Pokémon between turns.

flavorText: A nocturnal Pokémon. Drawn by streetlights, they messily eat the leaves of trees lining boulevards.

attacks.name: Delivery
attacks.text: Put any 1 card from your discard pile into your hand.

attacks.name: Lifting
attacks.text: Search your deck for up to 2 Basic Pokémon and put them onto your Bench. For each Basic Pokémon you put onto your Bench, you may search your deck for a basic Energy card and attach it to that Pokémon. Shuffle your deck afterward.

attacks.name: Ominous Wind
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Confused and can't retreat during your opponent's next turn.

name: Uxie

abilities.name: Memory Out
abilities.text: Once during your opponent's turn, if Uxie is damaged by an opponent's attack (even if Uxie is Knocked Out), you may use this power. The Attacking Pokémon can't use that attack during your opponent's next turn.

attacks.name: Mind Off
attacks.text: If you have Mesprit and Azelf in play, this attack does 10 damage plus 20 more damage and the Defending Pokémon is now Asleep.

flavorText: Known as "The Being of Knowledge." It is said that it can wipe out the memory of those who see its eyes.

name: Steelix

attacks.name: Guard Press
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, any damage done to Steelix by attacks is reduced by 20 (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

attacks.name: Steel Swing
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 80 damage times the number of heads.

flavorText: Its body has been compressed deep under the ground. As a result, it is even harder than a diamond.

abilities.name: Dark Lift
abilities.text: If Team Aqua's Carvanha has any Darkness Energy attached to it, the Retreat Cost for Team Aqua's Carvanha is 0.

attacks.name: Slow-Acting Poison
attacks.text: At the end of your opponent's next turn, the Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned.

name: Skarmory

attacks.name: Steel Coat
attacks.text: Search your deck for a Metal Energy card and attach it to 1 of your Pokémon. Shuffle your deck afterward.

flavorText: After nesting in bramble bushes, the wings of its chicks grow hard from scratches by thorns.

abilities.name: Stages of Evolution
abilities.text: As long as Hitmonlee is an Evolved Pokémon, Hitmonlee's attacks do 20 more damage to your opponent's Pokémon (before applying Weakness and Resistance).

attacks.name: Stretch Kick
attacks.text: Choose 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. This attack does 10 damage to that Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

name: Yanmega

attacks.name: Sonic Wave
attacks.text: Does 10 damage to each of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.) Your opponent switches the Defending Pokémon with 1 of his or her Benched Pokémon.

attacks.name: Pursue and Turn
attacks.text: If the Defending Pokémon already has 2 or more damage counters on it, this attack does 60 damage plus 20 more damage and switch Yanmega with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

flavorText: By churning its wings, it creates shock waves that inflict critical internal injuries to foes.

name: M Manectric-EX
rules: Mega Evolution rule: When 1 of your Pokémon becomes a Mega Evolution Pokémon, your turn ends.
Pokémon-EX rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Turbo Bolt
attacks.text: Attach 2 basic Energy cards from your discard pile to 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

name: Magneton

attacks.name: Sonicboom
attacks.text: Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for this attack. (Any other effects that would happen after applying Weakness and Resistance still happen.)

attacks.name: Selfdestruct
attacks.text: Does 20 damage to each Pokémon on each player's Bench. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.) Magneton does 100 damage to itself.

flavorText: Formed by several Magnemites linked together. They frequently appear when sunspots flare up.

attacks.name: Cry for Help
attacks.text: Search your deck for a Fire Pokémon (excluding Pokémon-ex), show it to your opponent, and put it into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.

attacks.name: Fireworks
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If tails, discard a Fire Energy attached to Torkoal.

abilities.name: Fan Action
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may switch 1 of the Defending Pokémon with 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. Your opponent chooses the Benched Pokémon to switch. This power can't be used if Shiftry is affected by a Special Condition.

attacks.name: Stadium Power
attacks.text: If there is any Stadium card in play, this attack does 50 damage plus 20 more damage.

name: Pichu

abilities.name: Baby Evolution
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may put Pikachu from your hand onto Pichu (this counts as evolving Pichu) and remove all damage counters from Pichu.

attacks.name: Baby Steps
attacks.text: Look at the top 5 cards of your deck, choose 1 of them, and put it into your hand. Shuffle the other cards back into your deck.

flavorText: The electric sacs in its cheeks are small. If even a little electricity leaks, it becomes shocked.

attacks.name: Moonwatching
attacks.text: Search your deck for a basic Energy card. Show that card to your opponent, then put it into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.

attacks.name: Comet Slap
attacks.text: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 10 damage times the number of heads.

attacks.name: Rend
attacks.text: If the Defending Pokémon has any damage counters on it, this attack does 20 damage plus 20 more damage.

attacks.name: Double Spin
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 60 damage times the number of heads.

attacks.name: Confuse Ray
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Confused.

attacks.name: Energy Crush
attacks.text: Does 20 more damage plus 10 more damage for each Energy attached to all of your opponent's Pokémon.

attacks.name: Smash Turn
attacks.text: You may switch Crawdaunt G with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

attacks.name: Hyper Beam
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, discard an Energy card attached to the Defending Pokémon.

name: Kartana

attacks.name: Big Cut
attacks.text: If you have exactly 4 Prize cards remaining, this attack does 120 more damage.

attacks.name: False Swipe
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, put damage counters on your opponent's Active Pokémon until its remaining HP is 10.

flavorText: This Ultra Beast's body, which is as thin as paper, is like a sharpened sword.

name: Lunatone

attacks.name: Selfdestruct
attacks.text: Does 10 damage to each Benched Pokémon (both yours and your opponent's). (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.) Lunatone does 60 damage to itself.

flavorText: It was discovered at the site of a meteor strike 40 years ago. Its stare can lull its foes to sleep.

name: Raikou

abilities.name: Legendary Body
abilities.text: As long as Raikou is your Active Pokémon, Raikou and Energy cards attached to it aren't affected by effects from Trainer cards other than Stadium cards. As long as this Power is active, discard any Trainer cards attached to Raikou. (This power works even if Raikou is Asleep, Confused, or Paralyzed.)

attacks.name: Lightning Spark
attacks.text: If your opponent has any Benched Pokémon, flip a coin. If heads, choose 1 of them and this attack does 20 damage to it. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: A Pokémon that races across the land while barking a cry that sounds like crashing thunder.

attacks.name: Thundershock
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

name: Ducklett

attacks.name: Splatter
attacks.text: This attack does 20 damage to 1 of your opponent's Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: They are better at swimming than flying, and they happily eat their favorite food, peat moss, as they dive underwater.

name: Psyduck

attacks.name: Headache
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent can't play any Trainer cards from their hand during their next turn.

flavorText: Using psychokinesis gives it a headache, so it normally passes the time spacing out and doing as little as possible.

name: Larvesta

attacks.name: Ember
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If tails, discard an Energy attached to this Pokémon.

flavorText: This Pokémon was believed to have been born from the sun. When it evolves, its entire body is engulfed in flames.

name: Dewgong

attacks.name: Ice Beam
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: Stores thermal energy in its body. Swims at a steady 8 knots even in intensely cold waters.

name: Shellos
flavorText: Its shape and coloration vary, depending on its habitat.

name: Litten
flavorText: While grooming itself, it builds up fur inside its stomach. It sets the fur alight and spews fiery attacks, which change based on how it coughs.

name: Kangaskhan

attacks.name: Fetch
attacks.text: Draw a card.

attacks.name: Comet Punch
attacks.text: Flip 4 coins. This attack does 20 damage times the number of heads.

flavorText: The infant rarely ventures out of its mother's protective pouch until it is three years old.

name: Emboar

abilities.name: Inferno Fandango
abilities.text: As often as you like during your turn (before your attack), you may attach a Fire Energy card from your hand to 1 of your Pokémon.

flavorText: It can throw a fire punch by setting its fists on fire with its fiery chin. It cares deeply about its friends.

name: Wurmple
flavorText: It lives among the tall grass and in forests. It repels attacks by raising up the spikes on its rear.

name: Feebas

attacks.name: Add-a-Dash
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. For each heads, draw a card.

flavorText: It is a shabby and ugly Pokémon. However, it is very hardy and can survive on little water.

name: Inkay

attacks.name: Hypnosis
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Asleep.

flavorText: It flashes the light-emitting spots on its body, which drains its opponent's will to fight. It takes the opportunity to scuttle away and hide.

name: Ekans
flavorText: By dislocating its jaw, it can swallow prey larger than itself. After a meal, it curls up and rests.

name: Exeggutor

attacks.name: Teleport
attacks.text: Switch Exeggutor with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

attacks.name: Big Eggsplosion
attacks.text: Flip a number of coins equal to the number of Energy attached to Exeggutor. This attack does 20 damage times the number of heads.

flavorText: Legend has it that on rare occasions, one of its heads will drop off and continue on as an Exeggcute.

name: Light Machamp

abilities.name: Tag Team
abilities.text: When you play Light Machamp from your hand, if it is on your Bench, remove 3 damage counters from your Active Pokémon. If it has fewer damage counters than that, remove all of them. Then, switch Light Machamp with your Active Pokémon.

attacks.name: Beatdown
attacks.text: If the Defending Pokémon has Dark in its name or is a Darkness Pokémon, flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 100 damage instead of 50.

flavorText: It uses its four arms in combat to unleash a ceaseless flurry of punches.

name: Slugma

attacks.name: Singe
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Burned.

flavorText: A common sight in volcanic areas, it slowly slithers around in a constant search for warm places.

name: Emboar

attacks.name: Firebreathing
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 30 more damage.

attacks.name: Fire Blast
attacks.text: Discard an Energy attached to this Pokémon.

flavorText: It has mastered fast and powerful fighting moves. It grows a beard of fire.

name: Pichu

abilities.name: Sweet Sleeping Face
abilities.text: As long as Pichu is Asleep, prevent all damage done to Pichu by attacks.

attacks.name: Playground
attacks.text: Each player may search his or her deck for as many Basic Pokémon as he or she likes, put them onto his or her Bench, and shuffle his or her deck afterward. (You put your Pokémon on the Bench first.) Pichu is now Asleep.

flavorText: Despite its small size, it can zap even adult humans. However, if it does so, it also surprises itself.

attacks.name: Freeze Dry
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

name: Arcanine

abilities.name: Burning Road
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), if this Pokémon was on the Bench and became your Active Pokémon this turn, you may move any number of Fire Energy attached to your Pokémon to this Pokémon.

attacks.name: Scorching Breath
attacks.text: This Pokémon can't attack during your next turn.

flavorText: A Pokémon that is described in Chinese legends. It is said to race at an unbelievable speed.

name: Empoleon

attacks.name: Dual Splash
attacks.text: Choose 2 of your opponent's Pokémon. This attack does 30 damage to each of them. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

attacks.name: Surf Together
attacks.text: Does 50 damage plus 10 more damage for each of your Benched Pokémon. Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does 10 damage to each of your Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: It swims as fast as a jet boat. The edges of its wings are sharp and can slice apart drifting ice.

name: Salazzle

attacks.name: Panic Poison
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Burned, Confused, and Poisoned.

attacks.name: Hunter's Nails
attacks.text: If your opponent's Active Pokémon is affected by a Special Condition, this attack does 60 more damage.

flavorText: Filled with pheromones, its poisonous gas can be diluted to use in the production of luscious perfumes.

abilities.name: Terraforming
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may look at the top 4 cards of your deck and rearrange them as you like. This power can't be used if Machamp is affected by a Special Condition.

attacks.name: Iron Fist
attacks.text: Count the number of Pokémon you have in play with damage counters on them. Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 50 damage plus 10 more damage for each of those Pokémon.

name: Yanma

attacks.name: Shockwave
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 10 damage to each of your opponent's Pokémon. Don't apply Weakness and Resistance. Then, if your opponent has any Benched Pokémon, he or she chooses 1 of them and switches it with the Defending Pokémon.

attacks.name: Swift
attacks.text: This attack's damage isn't affected by Weakness, Resistance, Pokémon Powers, or any other effects on the Defending Pokémon.

flavorText: If it flaps its wings really fast, it can generate shock waves that will shatter windows in the area.

name: Jolteon

abilities.name: Electric Effect
abilities.text: Each of your Stage 1 Pokémon in play is now a Lightning Pokémon in addition to its existing types.

attacks.name: Thunder Blast
attacks.text: Discard an Energy attached to this Pokémon.

flavorText: It accumulates negative ions in the atmosphere to blast out 10,000-volt lightning bolts.

name: Charizard V
rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Fire Spin
attacks.text: Discard 2 Energy from this Pokémon.

name: Electrode

attacks.name: Chain Lightning
attacks.text: If the Defending Pokémon isn't Colorless, this attack does 10 damage to each Benched Pokémon of the same type as the Defending Pokémon (including your own).

flavorText: It stores electrical energy under very high pressure. It often explodes with little or no provocation.

name: Orbeetle VMAX
rules: VMAX rule: When your Pokémon VMAX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

abilities.name: Eerie Beam
abilities.text: Once during your turn, if this Pokémon is in the Active Spot, you may put 1 damage counter on each of your opponent's Pokémon.

attacks.name: G-Max Wave
attacks.text: This attack does 50 more damage for each Energy attached to your opponent's Active Pokémon.

name: Electrode

attacks.name: Continuous Tumble
attacks.text: Flip a coin until you get tails. This attack does 20 damage times the number of heads.

attacks.name: Energy Bomb
attacks.text: You may move all Energy from this Pokémon to your Benched Pokémon in any way you like.

flavorText: It stores an overflowing amount of electric energy inside its body. Even a small shock makes it explode.

attacks.name: Stun Needle
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

attacks.name: Mystic Water
attacks.text: Does 20 damage plus 10 more damage for each Psychic Energy in play.

name: Tropius

attacks.name: Find a Friend
attacks.text: Search your deck for up to 2 Pokémon, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

flavorText: The bunch of fruit around its neck ripens twice a year and is delicious. It's a highly favored tropical snack.

name: Squirtle
flavorText: It shelters itself in its shell, then strikes back with spouts of water at every opportunity.

name: Dialga

attacks.name: Time Bellow
attacks.text: Draw a card.

attacks.name: Flash Cannon
attacks.text: You may return all Energy cards attached to Dialga to your hand. If you do, remove the highest Stage Evolution card from the Defending Pokémon and shuffle that card into your opponent's deck.

flavorText: It has the power to control time. It appears in Sinnoh-region myths as an ancient deity.

name: Darumaka

attacks.name: Firebreathing
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 10 more damage.

flavorText: Darumaka's droppings are hot, so people used to put them in their clothes to keep themselves warm.

name: Rapidash

attacks.name: Agility
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, prevent all effects of attacks, including damage, done to this Pokémon during your opponent's next turn.

attacks.name: Overrun
attacks.text: This attack does 20 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: Very competitive, this Pokémon will chase anything that moves fast in the hopes of racing it.

abilities.name: Camouflage Pattern
abilities.text: Prevent all effects of attacks, including damage, done to Dustox by your opponent's Pokémon that is affected by 2 or more Special Conditions.

attacks.name: Smogscreen
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned. If the Defending Pokémon tries to attack during your opponent's next turn, your opponent flips a coin. If tails, that attack does nothing.

attacks.name: Chemical Scale
attacks.text: If the Defending Pokémon has any Poké-Powers or Poké-Bodies, the Defending Pokémon is now Burned and Confused.

name: Croconaw

attacks.name: Sweep Away
attacks.text: Discard the top 3 cards of your deck.

flavorText: Once it bites down, it won't let go until it loses its fangs. New fangs quickly grow into place.

name: Houndoom-EX
rules: Pokémon-EX rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Melting Horn
attacks.text: Discard the top 2 cards of your opponent's deck.

attacks.name: Grand Flame
attacks.text: Attach a Fire Energy card from your discard pile to 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

name: Torchic

attacks.name: Ember
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If tails, discard a Fire Energy attached to this Pokémon.

flavorText: A fire burns inside, so it feels very warm to hug. It launches fireballs of 1,800 degrees Fahrenheit.

name: Magcargo

attacks.name: Stomp Off
attacks.text: Discard the top 2 cards of your opponent's deck.

attacks.name: Flamethrower
attacks.text: Discard an Energy from this Pokémon.

flavorText: Its brittle shell occasionally spouts intense flames that circulate throughout its body.

name: Tympole

attacks.name: Surprise Attack
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.

flavorText: By vibrating its cheeks, it emits sound waves imperceptible to humans and warns others of danger.

name: M Houndoom-EX
rules: Mega Evolution rule: When 1 of your Pokémon becomes a Mega Evolution Pokémon, your turn ends.
Pokémon-EX rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Inferno Fang
attacks.text: You may discard all Fire Energy attached to this Pokémon. If you do, this attack does 80 more damage.

attacks.name: Sporadic Sponging
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, remove 1 damage counter from Treecko.

name: Reshiram & Charizard-GX
rules: TAG TEAM rule: When your TAG TEAM is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Outrage
attacks.text: This attack does 10 more damage for each damage counter on this Pokémon.

attacks.name: Flare Strike
attacks.text: This Pokémon can't use Flare Strike during your next turn.

attacks.name: Double Blaze-GX
attacks.text: If this Pokémon has at least 3 extra Fire Energy attached to it (in addition to this attack's cost), this attack does 100 more damage, and this attack's damage isn't affected by any effects on your opponent's Active Pokémon. (You can't use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)

name: Delphox

abilities.name: Mystical Fire
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may draw cards until you have 6 cards in your hand.

attacks.name: Blaze Ball
attacks.text: This attack does 20 more damage for each Fire Energy attached to this Pokémon.

flavorText: It gazes into the flame at the tip of its branch to achieve a focused state, which allows it to see into the future.

name: Alakazam-EX
rules: Pokémon-EX rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

abilities.name: Kinesis
abilities.text: When you play M Alakazam-EX from your hand to evolve this Pokémon, before it evolves, you may put 2 damage counters on your opponent's Active Pokémon and 3 damage counters on 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon.

attacks.name: Suppression
attacks.text: Put 3 damage counters on each of your opponent's Pokémon that has any Energy attached to it.

name: Applin

attacks.name: Continuous Tumble
attacks.text: Flip a coin until you get tails. This attack does 30 more damage for each heads.

flavorText: It spends its entire life inside an apple. It hides from its natural enemies, bird Pokémon, by pretending it's just an apple and nothing more.

name: Inteleon V
rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Snipe Shot
attacks.text: This attack does 40 damage to 1 of your opponent's Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

attacks.name: Aqua Report
attacks.text: Your opponent reveals their hand.

abilities.name: Stages of Evolution
abilities.text: As long as Wobbuffet is an Evolved Pokémon, your opponent pays Colorless more to retreat his or her Active Pokémon.

attacks.name: Grind
attacks.text: Does 10 damage times the amount of Energy attached to Wobbuffet.

attacks.name: Shadow Tag
attacks.text: Put 7 damage counters on the Defending Pokémon at the end of your opponent's next turn.

name: Kingler δ
rules: This Pokémon is both Fire Metal type.

attacks.name: Body Slam
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

attacks.name: Prop-up Pinchers
attacks.text: If Kingler has a Pokémon Tool card attached to it, this attack does 40 damage plus 40 more damage.

name: Dewott

attacks.name: Razor Shell
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 more damage.

flavorText: Scalchop techniques differ from one Dewott to another. It never neglects maintaining its scalchops.

attacks.name: Dark Splash
attacks.text: Does 40 damage plus 10 more damage for each Darkness Pokémon in play.

name: Moltres

attacks.name: Hyper Flame
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, discard 1 Fire Energy card attached to Moltres. If tails, discard all Energy cards attached to Moltres. If you can't discard Energy cards, this attack does nothing.

flavorText: The flames on this legendary Pokémon's wings burn so brightly that they can make night seem like day.

abilities.name: Dowsing Code
abilities.text: Once during your turn, when you put Porygon-Z G from your hand onto your Bench, you may search your discard pile for up to 2 Pokémon Tool cards, show them to your opponent, and shuffle them into your deck.

attacks.name: Ambush
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 damage plus 40 more damage.

name: Charizard-EX
rules: Pokémon-EX rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Mega Ascension
attacks.text: Search your deck for M Charizard-EX, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.

attacks.name: Brave Fire
attacks.text: This Pokémon does 30 damage to itself.

abilities.name: Saturation
abilities.text: When you attach a Water Energy card from your hand to Quagsire, remove all Special Conditions and 2 damage counters from Quagsire.

attacks.name: Hyper Pump
attacks.text: Does 20 damage plus 20 more damage for each basic Energy card attached to Quagsire but not used to pay for this attack's Energy cost. You can't add more than 60 damage in this way.

name: Gyarados-GX
rules: Pokémon-GX rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Draconic Disaster
attacks.text: If there is any Stadium card in play, this attack does 100 more damage. Then, discard that Stadium card.

attacks.name: Dread Storm-GX
attacks.text: Discard an Energy from each of your opponent's Pokémon. (You can't use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)

abilities.name: Exoskeleton
abilities.text: Any damage done to Cloyster by attacks is reduced by 20 (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

attacks.name: Double Bubble
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 10 damage times the number of heads. If either of the coins is heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

attacks.name: Freezing Breath
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed. If tails, the Defending Pokémon is now Asleep.

attacks.name: Trample
attacks.text: For each Benched Pokémon (yours and your opponent's), flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 damage to that Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

name: Torkoal

abilities.name: Hot Snort
abilities.text: Once during your turn, when you put Torkoal from your hand onto your Bench, you may flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Burned.

flavorText: You find abandoned coal mines full of them. They dig tirelessly in search of coal.

name: Machamp

attacks.name: Vital Throw
attacks.text: You may do 40 damage plus 20 more damage. If you do, Machamp does 20 damage to itself.

attacks.name: Hundred Furious Punches
attacks.text: Does 60 damage plus 10 more damage for each Fighting Energy attached to Machamp.

flavorText: It quickly swings its four arms to rock its opponents with ceaseless punches and chops from all angles.

attacks.name: Feint Attack
attacks.text: Choose 1 of your opponent's Pokémon. This attack does 10 damage to that Pokémon. This attack's damage isn't affected by Weakness, Resistance, Poké-Powers, Poké-Bodies, or any other effects on that Pokémon.

attacks.name: Surprise
attacks.text: Choose 1 card from your opponent's hand without looking. Look at the card you chose, then have your opponent shuffle that card into his or her deck.

name: Larvesta

attacks.name: Take Down
attacks.text: This Pokémon does 10 damage to itself.

flavorText: The base of volcanoes is where they make their homes. They shoot fire from their five horns to repel attacking enemies.

name: Virizion

attacks.name: Giga Drain
attacks.text: Heal from this Pokémon the same amount of damage you did to the Defending Pokémon.

attacks.name: Sacred Sword
attacks.text: This Pokémon can't use Sacred Sword during your next turn.

flavorText: This Pokémon fought humans in order to protect its friends. Legends about it continue to be passed down.

abilities.name: Magnetic Field
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), if you have basic Energy cards in your discard pile, you may discard any 1 card from your hand. Then search for up to 2 basic Energy cards from your discard pile, show them to your opponent, and put them into your hand. You can't return the card you first discarded to your hand in this way. This power can't be used if Magneton is affected by a Special Condition.

attacks.name: Magnetic Force
attacks.text: Does 10 damage times the amount of Energy attached to all of your Pokémon (including Magneton).

abilities.name: Chaos Flash
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), if Golduck is your Active Pokémon, you may flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon (choose 1 if there are 2) is now Confused. This power can't be used if Golduck is affected by a Special Condition.

attacks.name: Special Blow
attacks.text: If the Defending Pokémon has any Special Energy cards attached to it, this attack does 30 damage plus 40 more damage.

name: Incineroar

attacks.name: Fire Fang
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Burned.

attacks.name: Darkest Lariat
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 100 damage for each heads.

flavorText: This Pokémon has a violent, selfish disposition. If it's not in the mood to listen, it will ignore its Trainer's orders with complete nonchalance.

name: Mr. Mime

abilities.name: Invisible Wall
abilities.text: Whenever an attack (including your own) does 30 or more damage to Mr. Mime (after applying Weakness and Resistance), prevent that damage. (Any other effects of attacks still happen.) This power stops working while Mr. Mime is Asleep, Confused, or Paralyzed.

attacks.name: Meditate
attacks.text: Does 10 damage plus 10 more damage for each damage counter on the Defending Pokémon.

flavorText: If interrupted while miming, it will slap around the enemy with its broad hands.

name: Bisharp

attacks.name: Wicked Jab
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: This pitiless Pokémon commands a group of Pawniard to hound prey into immobility. It then moves in to finish the prey off.

abilities.name: Temperamental Weather
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may search your deck for Castform, Sunny Castform, or Snow-cloud Castform and switch it with Rain Castform.
