attacks.text: If this Pokémon has fewer remaining HP than the Defending Pokémon, this attack does 60 more damage.

flavorText: If anyone were to hurt its pride, it would slash them with wings that can cleave through an ice floe.

name: Bronzong

attacks.name: Wide Laser
attacks.text: This attack does 10 damage to each of your opponent's Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

attacks.name: Hyper Beam
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, discard an Energy card attached to the Defending Pokémon.

flavorText: It brought rains by opening portals to another world. It was revered as a bringer of plentiful harvests.

attacks.name: Thunder Jolt
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If tails, Charmeleon does 10 damage to itself.

name: Dark Slowbro

abilities.name: Reel In
abilities.text: When you play Dark Slowbro from your hand, choose up to 3 Basic Pokémon and/or Evolution cards from your discard pile and put them into your hand.

attacks.name: Fickle Attack
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.

flavorText: It loves nothing better than to be left alone, and so sometimes ends up being manipulated by unscrupulous people.

abilities.name: Leaf Ride
abilities.text: If Scyther has any Energy attached to it, Scyther's Retreat Cost is 0.

attacks.name: Fury Cutter
attacks.text: Flip 3 coins. If 1 of them is heads, this attack does 10 damage plus 10 more damage. If 2 of them are heads, this attack does 10 damage plus 20 more damage. If all of them are heads, this attack does 10 damage plus 40 more damage.

name: Haunter

attacks.name: Sneaky Placement
attacks.text: Put 2 damage counters on 1 of your opponent's Pokémon.

attacks.name: Sleep Poison
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon is now Asleep and Poisoned.

flavorText: Its tongue is made of gas. If licked, its victim starts shaking constantly until death eventually comes.

attacks.name: Clay Curse
attacks.text: Put 2 damage counters on your opponent's Pokémon in any way you like.

attacks.name: Clay Pulse
attacks.text: Does 10 damage to each of your opponent's Benched Pokémon that has any damage counters on it. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

name: Litleo
flavorText: When they're young, they live with a pride. Once they're able to hunt prey on their own, they're kicked out and have to make their own way.

name: Mewtwo

attacks.name: Energy Control
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, choose a basic Energy card attached to 1 of your opponent's Pokémon and attach it to another of your opponent's Pokémon of your choice.

attacks.name: Telekinesis
attacks.text: Choose 1 of your opponent's Pokémon. This attack does 30 damage to that Pokémon. Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for this attack. (Any other effects that would happen after applying Weakness and Resistance still happen.)

flavorText: A scientist created this Pokémon after years of horrific gene-splicing and DNA engineering experiments.

name: Pinsir

attacks.name: Irongrip
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: If it fails to crush the victim in its pincers, it will swing its victim around and toss it hard.

name: Arbok
flavorText: After stunning its opponents with the pattern on its stomach, it quickly wraps them up in its body and waits for them to stop moving.

name: Ducklett

attacks.name: Roost
attacks.text: Heal 40 damage from this Pokémon. This Pokémon can't retreat during your next turn.

flavorText: When attacked, it uses its feathers to splash water, escaping under cover of the spray.

abilities.name: Restless Sleep
abilities.text: If your opponent's attack does damage to Rocket's Snorlax and Rocket's Snorlax is already Asleep (even if it's Knocked Out), this power does 20 damage to the attacking Pokémon.

attacks.name: Collapse
attacks.text: Rocket's Snorlax is now Asleep (after doing damage).

name: Gyarados

abilities.name: Dragon DNA
abilities.text: Gyarados can use any attack from its Basic Pokémon. (You still have to pay for that attack's Energy cost.) If Gyarados uses any attack from its Basic Pokémon, that attack does 30 more damage to the Defending Pokémon (before applying Weakness and Resistance).

attacks.name: Enrage
attacks.text: Flip a coin until you get tails. For each heads, choose 1 card from your opponent's hand without looking and discard it. If the first coin is tails, Gyarados is now Confused.

flavorText: Once it appears, its rage never settles until it has razed the fields and mountains around it.

attacks.name: Licking-Licking Heal
attacks.text: Attach a Basic Energy card from your hand to 1 of your Pokémon. Then, remove 2 damage counters from that Pokémon.

attacks.name: Return
attacks.text: Draw cards until you have 6 cards in your hand.

name: Vaporeon

abilities.name: Torrential Awakening
abilities.text: If this Pokémon has a Memory Capsule attached, Fire Pokémon in play (both yours and your opponent's) have no Abilities.

flavorText: When Vaporeon's fins begin to vibrate, it is a sign that rain will come within a few hours.

name: Beartic

attacks.name: Daunt
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, any damage done by attack from the Defending Pokémon is reduced by 20 (before applying Weakness and Resistance).

attacks.name: Ambush
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 30 more damage.

flavorText: It can make its breath freeze at will. Very able in the water, it swims around in northern seas and catches prey.

name: Litten

attacks.name: Caturday
attacks.text: Draw a card. If you do, this Pokémon is now Asleep.

attacks.name: Big Bite
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon can't retreat during your opponent's next turn.

flavorText: If you try too hard to get close to it, it won't open up to you. Even if you do grow close, giving it too much affection is still a no-no.

name: Coalossal

abilities.name: Tar Generator
abilities.text: Once during your turn, you may attach a Fire Energy card, a Fighting Energy card, or 1 of each from your discard pile to your Pokémon in any way you like.

flavorText: It's usually peaceful, but the vandalism of mines enrages it. Offenders will be incinerated with flames that reach 2,700 degrees Fahrenheit.

name: Dark Croconaw

attacks.name: Clamping Jaw
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon can't retreat during your opponent's next turn. If the Defending Pokémon tries to attack during your opponent's next turn, your opponent flips a coin. If tails, that attack does nothing. (Benching either Pokémon ends this effect.)

flavorText: It has 49 teeth in its mouth that are constantly replacing themselves. Pull one out, and a new one grows in.

name: Trapinch

attacks.name: Mini Earthquake
attacks.text: This attack does 10 damage to each of your Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: As it digs through the sand, its giant jaws crush any rocks that obstruct its path. It builds a funnel-shaped nest.

name: Golduck

attacks.name: Psyshock
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

attacks.name: Hyper Beam
attacks.text: If the Defending Pokémon has any Energy cards attached to it, choose 1 of them and discard it.

flavorText: Often seen swimming elegantly by lake shores. It is often mistaken for the Japanese monster, Kappa.

name: Alolan Geodude

attacks.name: Charge
attacks.text: Search your deck for up to 2 Lightning Energy cards and attach them to this Pokémon. Then, shuffle your deck.

attacks.name: Smash Bomb
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.

flavorText: If you mistake it for a rock and step on it, it will headbutt you in anger. In addition to the pain, it will also zap you with a shock.

name: Lurantis

abilities.name: Sunny Day
abilities.text: The attacks of your Grass Pokémon and Fire Pokémon do 20 more damage to your opponent's Active Pokémon (before applying Weakness and Resistance).

flavorText: It requires a lot of effort to maintain Lurantis's vivid coloring, but some collectors enjoy this work and treat it as their hobby.

name: Victini

attacks.name: Victory Sign
attacks.text: Search your deck for up to 2 basic Energy cards of different types and attach them to your Pokémon in any way you like. Then, shuffle your deck.

flavorText: This Pokémon brings victory. It is said that Trainers with Victini always win, regardless of the type of encounter.

name: Heliolisk

attacks.name: Parabolic Spark
attacks.text: Discard as many Lightning Energy attached to your Pokémon as you like. This attack does 30 damage times the number of Energy cards you discarded.

flavorText: They flare their frills and generate energy. A single Heliolisk can generate sufficient electricity to power a skyscraper.

name: Golduck

attacks.name: Amnesia
attacks.text: Choose 1 of the Defending Pokémon's attacks. The Pokémon can't use that attack during your opponent's next turn.

attacks.name: Aquafall
attacks.text: Discard all Energy attached to this Pokémon.

flavorText: When its forehead shines mysteriously, Golduck can use the full extent of its power.

abilities.name: Plunge
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), if Poliwrath is on your Bench, you may flip a coin. If heads, take all Energy cards attached to your Active Pokémon, if any, and attach them to Poliwrath. Then switch Poliwrath with your Active Pokémon.

attacks.name: Water Punch
attacks.text: Flip a number of coins equal to the amount of Water Energy attached to Poliwrath. This attack does 40 damage plus 10 more damage for each heads.

name: Vanillite

attacks.name: Triple Spin
attacks.text: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 10 damage for each heads.

flavorText: Born of an icicle, this Pokémon uses its frosty breath to make ice crystals, causing snow to fall.

name: Palkia

attacks.name: Spiral Drain
attacks.text: Heal 30 damage from this Pokémon.

flavorText: It is said to live in a gap in the spatial dimension parallel to ours. It appears in mythology.

name: Greninja

abilities.name: Mist Concealment
abilities.text: When you play this Pokémon from your hand to evolve 1 of your Pokémon, you may prevent all effects of attacks, including damage, done to this Pokémon by your opponent's Pokémon during your opponent's next turn.

attacks.name: Shadow Bullet
attacks.text: This attack does 20 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness or Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: It creates throwing stars out of compressed water. When it spins them and throws them at high speed, these stars can split metal in two.

abilities.name: Cheerful Voice
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may use this power. If you do, your turn ends. During your next turn, each of Ludicolo's attacks does 60 more damage to the Defending Pokémon (before applying Weakness and Resistance). This power can't be used if Ludicolo is affected by a Special Condition.

attacks.name: Mad Dance
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon is now Confused.

attacks.name: Best Dance
attacks.text: After doing damage, remove from Ludicolo the number of damage counters equal to the damage you did to the Defending Pokémon. Ludicolo can't use Best Dance during your next turn.

name: Guzzlord

attacks.name: Lord's Valley
attacks.text: If you have exactly 2, 4, or 6 Prize cards remaining, discard the top 10 cards of your deck.

flavorText: A dangerous Ultra Beast, it appears to be eating constantly, but for some reason its droppings have never been found.

name: Carvanha
flavorText: They swarm any foe that invades their territory. Their sharp fangs can tear out boat hulls.

name: Azelf

attacks.name: Trading Places
attacks.text: Switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

attacks.name: Psyjamming
attacks.text: Move as many Special Energy attached to your opponent's Pokémon to your opponent's other Pokémon in any way you like.

flavorText: When Azelf flew, people gained the determination to do things. It was the birth of willpower.

name: Kakuna

attacks.name: Stiffen
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, prevent all damage done to Kakuna during your opponent's next turn. (Any other effects of attacks still happen.)

attacks.name: Poisonpowder
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned.

flavorText: Almost incapable of moving, this Pokémon can only harden its shell to protect itself from predators.

name: Butterfree

attacks.name: Whirlwind
attacks.text: If your opponent has any Benched Pokémon, he or she chooses 1 of them and switches it with the Defending Pokémon. (Do the damage before switching the Pokémon.)

attacks.name: Mega Drain
attacks.text: Remove a number of damage counters from Butterfree equal to half the damage done to the Defending Pokémon (after applying Weakness and Resistance) (rounded up to the nearest 10). If Butterfree has fewer damage counters than that, remove all of them.

flavorText: In battle, it flaps its wings at high speed to release highly toxic dust into the air.

name: Growlithe

attacks.name: Odor Sleuth
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, put a card from your discard pile into your hand.

flavorText: It has a brave and trustworthy nature. It fearlessly stands up to bigger and stronger foes.

name: Raboot
flavorText: Its thick and fluffy fur protects it from the cold and enables it to use hotter fire moves.

name: Magma Pointer
rules: Attach a Pokémon Tool to 1 of your Pokémon that doesn't already have a Pokémon Tool attached to it.
The Team Magma Pokémon this card is attached to can also use the attack on this card. (You still need the necessary Energy to use this attack.)
You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

attacks.name: Magma Pointer
attacks.text: This attack does 20 damage to 1 of your opponent's Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

abilities.name: Safeguard
abilities.text: Prevent all effects of attacks, including damage, done to Wobbuffet by your opponent's Pokémon-ex.

attacks.name: Flip Over
attacks.text: Wobbuffet does 10 damage to itself, and don't apply Weakness and Resistance to this damage.

name: Krabby
flavorText: If it senses danger approaching, it cloaks itself with bubbles from its mouth so it will look bigger.

attacks.name: Double Tackle
attacks.text: Does 20 damage to each Defending Pokémon.

attacks.name: Crushing Blow
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, discard an Energy card attached to the Defending Pokémon.

name: Mudkip
flavorText: To alert it, the fin on its head senses the flow of water. It has the strength to heft boulders.

name: M Slowbro-EX
rules: Mega Evolution rule: When 1 of your Pokémon becomes a Mega Evolution Pokémon, your turn ends.
Pokémon-EX rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Loll Roll Spin
attacks.text: This Pokémon is now Confused. During your next turn, this Pokémon's Loll Roll Spin attack does 100 more damage (before applying Weakness and Resistance).

name: Horsea

attacks.name: Water Arrow
attacks.text: This attack does 10 damage to 1 of your opponent's Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: Known to shoot down flying bugs with precision blasts of ink from the surface of the water.

name: Prinplup

attacks.name: Bubble Beam
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: It lives a solitary life. Its wings deliver wicked blows that can snap even the thickest of trees.

name: Staryu

attacks.name: Reckless Charge
attacks.text: This Pokémon does 10 damage to itself.

flavorText: Even if its body is torn, it can regenerate as long as the glowing central core remains intact.

name: Poliwrath

attacks.name: Split Spiral Punch
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Confused.

attacks.name: Wake-Up Slap
attacks.text: If your opponent's Active Pokémon is affected by a Special Condition, this attack does 80 more damage. Then, remove all Special Conditions from that Pokémon.

flavorText: Its percentage of body fat is nearly zero. Its body is entirely muscle, which makes it heavy and forces its swimming prowess to develop.

abilities.name: Dark Gaze
abilities.text: As long as Umbreon is your Active Pokémon, Benched Pokémon (yours and your opponent's) can't use Poké-Powers.

attacks.name: Sharp Claws
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 10 damage plus 30 more damage.

attacks.name: Paralyzing Gaze
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

attacks.name: Knock Off
attacks.text: Choose 1 card from your opponent's hand without looking and discard it.

name: Piplup

attacks.name: Fury Attack
attacks.text: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 10 damage times the number of heads.

flavorText: A poor walker, it often falls down. However, its strong pride makes it puff up its chest without a care.

name: Tangrowth

attacks.name: Grind
attacks.text: Does 20 damage times the number of Energy attached to Tangrowth.

attacks.name: Plow Over
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed. If tails, put 1 Energy card attached to the Defending Pokémon in the Lost Zone.

flavorText: Its vines grow so profusely that, in the warm season, you can't even see its eyes.

name: Aurorus

attacks.name: Frost Wall
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks from Evolution Pokémon.

attacks.name: Blizzard Burn
attacks.text: This Pokémon can't attack during your next turn.

flavorText: Using its diamond-shaped crystals, it can instantly create a wall of ice to block an opponent's attack.

abilities.name: Flower Supplement
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may flip a coin. If heads, attach 1 basic Energy card from your hand to 1 of your Benched Pokémon. This power can't be used if Bellossom is affected by a Special Condition.

attacks.name: Knife Leaf
attacks.text: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 30 damage times the number of heads.

name: Graveler

attacks.name: Mudslide
attacks.text: Discard 2 Fighting Energy from this Pokémon. This attack does 80 damage to 1 of your opponent's Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: It climbs up cliffs as it heads toward the peak of a mountain. As soon as it reaches the summit, it rolls back down the way it came.

attacks.name: Screechy Voice
attacks.text: If the Defending Pokémon is an Evolved Pokémon, the Defending Pokémon is now Confused.

attacks.name: Push Away
attacks.text: Look at your opponent's hand, choose a Trainer card you find there, and discard it.

name: Articuno-GX
rules: Pokémon-GX rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

abilities.name: Legendary Ascent
abilities.text: When you play this Pokémon from your hand onto your Bench during your turn, you may switch it with your Active Pokémon. If you do, move any number of Water Energy from your other Pokémon to this Pokémon.

attacks.name: Cold Crush-GX
attacks.text: Discard all Energy from both Active Pokémon. (You can't use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)

attacks.name: Searing Flame
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon is now Burned.

attacks.name: Firebreathing
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 30 damage plus 20 more damage.

abilities.name: Thick Skin
abilities.text: Zangoose can't be affected by any Special Conditions.

attacks.name: Call for Family
attacks.text: Search your deck for up to 2 Basic Pokémon and put them onto your Bench. Shuffle your deck afterward.

attacks.name: Sharp Claws
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 10 damage plus 20 more damage.

attacks.name: Resonate
attacks.text: Your opponent switches each Defending Pokémon with his or her Benched Pokémon. If your opponent has only 1 Benched Pokémon, you choose the Defending Pokémon to switch.

attacks.name: Healing Sound
attacks.text: Remove 3 damage counters from each of your Active Pokémon. Each Defending Pokémon is now Asleep.

abilities.name: Temperamental Weather
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may search your deck for Castform, Rain Castform, or Snow-cloud Castform and switch it with Sunny Castform. (Any cards attached to Sunny Castform, damage counters, Special Conditions, and effects on it are now on the new Pokémon.) Shuffle Sunny Castform back into your deck. You can't use more than 1 Temperamental Weather Poké-Power each turn.

attacks.name: Sunshine
attacks.text: Search your discard pile for a Stadium card, show it to your opponent, and put it into your hand.

attacks.name: Sunburn
attacks.text: If High Pressure System is in play, the Defending Pokémon is now Burned.

name: Noctowl

attacks.name: See Beyond
attacks.text: Choose a card from your hand and put it as a Prize card face up. Then, choose 1 of your face-down Prize cards without looking and put it into your hand. This attack does nothing if all of your Prize cards are face up.

attacks.name: Extrasensory
attacks.text: If you have the same number of cards in your hand as your opponent, this attack does 30 damage plus 50 more damage.

flavorText: Its eyes are special. They can pick out objects as long as there is the tiniest amount of light.

name: Klang

attacks.name: Metal Sound
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon is now Confused.

attacks.name: Guard Press
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, any damage done to this Pokémon by attacks is reduced by 20 (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

flavorText: Spinning minigears are rotated at high speed and repeatedly fired away. It is dangerous if the gears don't return.

name: Vespiquen

attacks.name: Commanding Queen
attacks.text: If you have 4 or fewer Grass Pokémon on your Bench, this attack does nothing.

flavorText: Its abdomen is a honeycomb for grubs. It raises its grubs on honey collected by Combee.

name: Claydol

attacks.name: Rewind
attacks.text: Devolve each of your opponent's evolved Pokémon and put the highest Stage Evolution card on it into your opponent's hand.

attacks.name: Hyper Beam
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, discard an Energy attached to your opponent's Active Pokémon.

flavorText: It is said that it originates from clay dolls made by an ancient civilization.

attacks.name: Body Slam
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

name: Deoxys Speed Forme

abilities.name: Form Change
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may search your deck for any Deoxys and switch it with Deoxys Speed Forme. (Any cards attached to Deoxys Speed Forme, damage counters, Special Conditions, and effects on it are now on the new Pokémon.) If you do, put Deoxys Speed Forme on top of your deck. Shuffle your deck afterward. You can't use more than 1 Form Change Poké-Power each turn.

attacks.name: Speed Shot
attacks.text: Choose 1 of your opponent's Pokémon. This attack does 30 damage to that Pokémon. This attack's damage isn't affected by Weakness, Resistance, Poké-Powers, Poké-Bodies, or any other effects on that Pokémon.

flavorText: An alien virus that fell to earth on a meteor underwent a DNA mutation to become this Pokémon.

name: Gothita

attacks.name: Psybeam
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Confused.

flavorText: They intently observe both Trainers and Pokémon. Apparently, they are looking at something only Gothita can see.

name: Banette

abilities.name: Tool Concealment
abilities.text: Each Pokémon Tool card in play has no effect.

flavorText: Strong feelings of hatred turned a puppet into a Pokémon. If it opens its mouth, its cursed energy escapes.

attacks.name: Dragon Crush
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 10 damage to each Defending Pokémon. Discard an Energy card from each Defending Pokémon.

attacks.name: Aqua Sonic
attacks.text: This attack's damage is not affected by Resistance.

name: Crawdaunt

attacks.name: Double Claws
attacks.text: Discard 2 Energy from your opponent's Active Pokémon.

flavorText: It is a ruffian that uses its pincers to pick up and toss out other Pokémon from its pond.

name: Slowking

abilities.name: Trump Card
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), if any of your Pokémon were Knocked Out during your opponent's last turn, search your deck for any 1 card and put it into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward. This power can't be used if Slowking is affected by a Special Condition.

attacks.name: Psych Up
attacks.text: During your next turn, Slowking's Psych Up attack's base damage is 60.

flavorText: Being bitten by Shellder gave it intelligence comparable to that of award-winning scientists.

name: Corsola

attacks.name: Recover
attacks.text: Discard a Water Energy attached to Corsola and remove all damage counters from Corsola.

attacks.name: Hyper Cannon
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. If both of them are heads, this attack does 20 damage plus 50 more damage.

flavorText: It continuously sheds and grows. The tip of its head is prized as a treasure because of its beauty.

name: Clawitzer BREAK
rules: BREAK Evolution Rule: Clawitzer BREAK retains the attacks, Abilities, Weakness, Resistance, and Retreat Cost of its previous Evolution.

attacks.name: Lock-On
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon can't retreat during your opponent's next turn. During your next turn, any damage done to that Pokémon by attacks is increased by 120 (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

name: Bronzor

attacks.name: Extrasensory
attacks.text: If you have the same number of cards in your hand as your opponent, this attack does 30 damage plus 30 more damage.

flavorText: There are researchers who believe this Pokémon reflected like a mirror in the distant past.

name: Grotle

attacks.name: Sleep Powder
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Asleep.

flavorText: A Grotle that lives in the forest is said to have its own secret springwater.

name: Snorlax

attacks.name: Rock Smash
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 30 more damage.

flavorText: It is not satisfied unless it eats over 880 pounds of food every day. When it is done eating, it goes promptly to sleep.

name: Samurott

abilities.name: Shell Armor
abilities.text: Any damage done to this Pokémon by attacks is reduced by 20 (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

attacks.name: Hydro Pump
attacks.text: Does 10 more damage for each Water Energy attached to this Pokémon.

flavorText: Part of the armor on its anterior legs becomes a giant sword. Its cry alone is enough to intimidate most enemies.

attacks.name: Steep Dive
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, prevent all effects of an attack, including damage, done to Swellow during your opponent's next turn. If tails, during your next turn, Swellow's Glide attack's base damage is 100.

abilities.name: Battle Rush
abilities.text: If your opponent has any Pokémon in play that has maximum HP of 120 or more, ignore all Colorless Energy necessary to use Salamence's attacks.

attacks.name: Steam Twister
attacks.text: Discard a Fire Energy and a Water Energy attached to Salamence.

attacks.name: Time Spiral
attacks.text: If your opponent has any Evolved Pokémon in play, choose 1 of them and flip a coin. If heads, remove the highest Stage Evolution card on that Pokémon and have your opponent shuffle it into his or her deck.

attacks.name: Haunt
attacks.text: Put 2 damage counters on the Defending Pokémon.

abilities.name: Tropical Heal
abilities.text: Once during your turn, when you put Tropius from your hand onto your Bench, you may remove all Special Conditions, Imprison markers, and Shock-wave markers from your Pokémon.

attacks.name: Grind
attacks.text: Does 10 damage times the amount of Energy attached to Tropius.

name: Sharpedo

abilities.name: Rough Skin
abilities.text: If Sharpedo is your Active Pokémon and is damaged by an opponent's attack (even if Sharpedo is Knocked Out), put 2 damage counters on the Attacking Pokémon.

attacks.name: Strike Wound
attacks.text: If the Defending Pokémon has 2 or more damage counters on it, this attack does 60 damage plus 20 more damage. This attack damage isn't affected by Weakness, Resistance, Poké-Powers, Poké-Bodies, or any other effects on that Pokémon.

flavorText: Its fangs rip through sheet iron. It swims at 75 mph and is known as "The Bully of the Sea."

abilities.name: Natural Cure
abilities.text: When you attach a Water Energy card from your hand to Lombre, remove all Special Conditions from Lombre.

attacks.name: Blot
attacks.text: Remove 2 damage counters from Lombre.

name: Kabutops

attacks.name: Hydrocutter
attacks.text: Flip a number of coins equal to the number of Energy cards attached to Kabutops. This attack does 40 damage times the number of heads. You can't flip more than 3 coins in this way.

flavorText: In the water, it tucks its limbs to become more compact, then it wiggles its shell to swim fast.

name: Ariados

attacks.name: Spider Web
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon can't retreat. (Benching or evolving that Pokémon ends this effect.)

attacks.name: Poison Bite
attacks.text: If this attack damages the Defending Pokémon, the Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned and you remove a number of damage counters from Ariados equal to half that damage (rounded up to the nearest 10). If Ariados has fewer damage counters than that, remove all of them.

flavorText: It spins string not only from its rear but also from its mouth. It's hard to tell which end is which.

attacks.name: Double Stab
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 20 damage times the number of heads.

name: Galarian Darmanitan

attacks.name: Frozen Heat
attacks.text: You may discard all Water Energy from this Pokémon. If you do, this attack does 60 more damage.

flavorText: Anger has reignited its atrophied flame sac. This Pokémon spews fire everywhere as it rampages indiscriminately.

abilities.name: Territoriality
abilities.text: If your Active Pokémon is damaged by an opponent's attack (even if that Pokémon is Knocked Out), put 2 damage counters on the Attacking Pokémon. You can't put more than 2 damage counters in this way.

attacks.name: Fling Away
attacks.text: If your opponent has any Benched Pokémon, this attack's base damage is 30 instead of 60 and this attack does 30 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

attacks.name: Giga Horn
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. If both of them are tails, this attack does nothing.

name: Omastar

abilities.name: Primal Swirl
abilities.text: Once during your turn, when you play Omastar from your hand to evolve 1 of your Pokémon, you may remove the highest Stage Evolution card from each of your opponent's Benched Evolved Pokémon and put those cards back into his or her hand. You can't use more than 1 Primal Swirl Poké-Power each turn.

attacks.name: Send Back
attacks.text: Your opponent switches the Defending Pokémon with 1 of his or her Benched Pokémon.

flavorText: It is thought that this Pokémon became extinct because its spiral shell grew too large.

name: Dark Vileplume

abilities.name: Hay Fever
abilities.text: No Trainer cards can be played. This power stops working while Dark Vileplume is Asleep, Confused, or Paralyzed.

attacks.name: Petal Whirlwind
attacks.text: Flip a coins. This attack does 30 damage times the number of heads. If you get 2 or more heads, Dark Vileplume is now Confused (after doing damage).

flavorText: Perhaps because of their strong-smelling pollen, other Pokémon do not approach areas where Vileplumes live.

name: Cloyster

attacks.name: Sudden Grip
attacks.text: If this Pokémon evolved from Shellder during this turn, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: Its shell is extremely hard. It cannot be shattered, even with a bomb. The shell opens only when it is attacking.

name: Vanillish

attacks.name: Ice Beam
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: It conceals itself from enemy eyes by creating many small ice particles and hiding among them.

name: Seismitoad

attacks.name: Echoed Voice
attacks.text: During your next turn, this Pokémon's Echoed Voice attack does 50 more damage (before applying Weakness and Resistance).

attacks.name: Drain Punch
attacks.text: Heal 20 damage from this Pokémon.

flavorText: They shoot paralyzing liquid from their head bumps. They use vibration to hurt their opponents.

name: Samurott

attacks.name: Pike
attacks.text: Does 30 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: Part of the armor on its anterior legs becomes a giant sword. Its cry alone is enough to intimidate most enemies.

name: Phione

attacks.name: Pull Out
attacks.text: Put a card from your discard pile on top of your deck.

attacks.name: Aqua Boomerang
attacks.text: Return this Pokémon and all cards attached to it to your hand.

flavorText: It drifts in warm seas. It always returns to where it was born, no matter how far it may have drifted.

name: Dusknoir

abilities.name: Dark Hide
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may flip a coin. If heads, look at your opponent's hand, choose a Pokémon you find there, and put it on the bottom of his or her deck. This power can't be used if Dusknoir is affected by a Special Condition.

attacks.name: Reaper Pulse
attacks.text: Move up to 2 damage counters from Dusknoir to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon.

flavorText: The antenna on its head captures radio waves from the world of spirits that command it to take people there.

name: Porygon2

abilities.name: Shortcut
abilities.text: The Retreat Cost for each Porygon, Porygon2, and Porygon-Z you have in play is Colorless less.

attacks.name: Reckless Charge
attacks.text: Porygon2 does 10 damage to itself.

flavorText: This upgraded version of Porygon is designed for space exploration. It can't fly, though.

name: Duskull

attacks.name: Revival
attacks.text: Put a Basic Pokémon from your opponent's discard pile onto his or her Bench.

attacks.name: Sneaky Placement
attacks.text: Put 1 damage counter on your opponent's Active Pokémon.

flavorText: It doggedly pursues its prey wherever it goes. However, the chase is abandoned at sunrise.

name: Metang

attacks.name: Double Smash
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 50 damage times the number of heads.

flavorText: When two Beldum fuse together, a magnetic nervous system places their brains in union.

name: Clauncher

attacks.name: Bubble
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: They knock down flying prey by firing compressed water from their massive claws like shooting a pistol.

name: Kyurem

attacks.name: Frost Spear
attacks.text: Does 30 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

attacks.name: Blizzard Burn
attacks.text: This Pokémon can't attack during your next turn.

flavorText: This legendary ice Pokémon waits for a hero to fill in the missing parts of its body with truth or ideals.

name: Marshtomp

attacks.name: Endeavor
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 20 more damage for each heads.

flavorText: Living on muddy ground that provides poor footing has made its legs sturdy.

name: Kricketune

attacks.name: Sore Performance
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon is now Asleep.

attacks.name: Concerto
attacks.text: Does 40 damage plus 10 more damage for each Kricketot and each Kricketune you have in play.

flavorText: It crosses its knifelike arms in front of its chest when it cries. It can compose melodies ad lib.

name: Phione

attacks.name: Charm
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, any damage done by attacks from the Defending Pokémon is reduced by 20 (before applying Weakness and Resistance).

attacks.name: Whirlpool
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, discard an Energy card attached to the Defending Pokémon.

flavorText: A Pokémon that lives in warm seas. It inflates the flotation sac on its head to drift and search for food.

name: Tsareena

abilities.name: Queenly Majesty
abilities.text: When you play this Pokémon from your hand to evolve 1 of your Pokémon during your turn, you may have your opponent reveal their hand. Then, discard a card from it.

attacks.name: Trop Kick
attacks.text: Heal 20 damage and remove all Special Conditions from this Pokémon.

flavorText: Its long, striking legs aren't just for show but to be used to kick with skill. In victory, it shows off by kicking the defeated, laughing boisterously.

name: Golbat

attacks.name: Screech
attacks.text: Until the end of your next turn, if an attack damages the Defending Pokémon (after applying Weakness and Resistance), that attack does 20 more damage to the Defending Pokémon.

attacks.name: Poison Bite
attacks.text: If this attack damages the Defending Pokémon, the Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned and you remove a number of damage counters from Golbat equal to half that damage (rounded up to the nearest 10). If Golbat has fewer damage counters than that, remove all of them. Either way, the Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned.

flavorText: It can drink more than 10 ounces of blood at once. If it has too much, it gets heavy and flies clumsily.

name: Torracat

attacks.name: Fire Fang
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Burned.

flavorText: It can act spoiled if it grows close to its Trainer. A powerful Pokémon, its sharp claws can leave its Trainer's whole body covered in scratches.

name: Charmeleon

attacks.name: Flamethrower
attacks.text: Discard 1 Fire Energy card attached to Charmeleon in order to use this attack.

flavorText: When it swings its burning tail, it raises the temperature to unbearably high levels.

abilities.name: Happy Healing
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), choose 1 of your Benched Pokémon and flip a coin. If heads, count the number of Energy attached to Blissey and then remove that many damage counters from the chosen Benched Pokémon. This power can't be used if Blissey is affected by a Special Condition.

attacks.name: Smash Bomber
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.

name: Shellder
flavorText: The hardness of its shell surpasses the hardness of a diamond. In days gone by, people used the shells to make shields.

name: Wailmer

attacks.name: Hydro Pump
attacks.text: This attack does 20 more damage for each Water Energy attached to this Pokémon.

flavorText: It shows off by spraying jets of seawater from the nostrils above its eyes. It eats a solid ton of Wishiwashi every day.

attacks.name: Target Scorch
attacks.text: If the Defending Pokémon has Team Aqua in its name, the Defending Pokémon is now Burned.

attacks.name: Damage Burn
attacks.text: If the Defending Pokémon already has any damage counters on it, this attack does 40 damage plus 20 more damage.

name: Bayleef

attacks.name: Poisonpowder
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned.

attacks.name: Pollen Shield
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, Bayleef can't become Asleep, Confused, Paralyzed, or Poisoned. (All other effects of attacks, Pokémon Powers and Trainer cards still happen.)

flavorText: A spicy aroma emanates from around its neck. The aroma acts as a stimulant to restore health.

attacks.name: Surprise
attacks.text: Choose 1 card from your opponent's hand without looking. Look at the card you chose, then have your opponent shuffle that card into his or her deck.

name: Scyther

attacks.name: Swords Dance
attacks.text: During your next turn, Scyther's Slash attack's base damage is 60 instead of 30.

flavorText: With ninja-like agility and speed, it can create the illusion that there is more than one of it.

name: Kakuna

attacks.name: Speed Evolution
attacks.text: Search your deck for a card that evolves from Kakuna and put it onto Kakuna. (This counts as evolving Kakuna.) Shuffle your deck afterward.

attacks.name: Poison Sting
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned.

flavorText: Although it is a cocoon, it can move a little. It can extend its poison barb if it is attacked.

name: Magikarp

attacks.name: Epic Splash
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. If either of them is tails, this attack does nothing.

flavorText: In the distant past, it was somewhat stronger than the horribly weak descendants that exist today.

attacks.name: Swift
attacks.text: This attack's damage isn't affected by Weakness, Resistance, Pokémon Powers, or any other effects on the Defending Pokémon.

attacks.name: Needle Ball
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned.

name: Golem

attacks.name: Rock Slide
attacks.text: This attack does 20 damage to 3 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: When Golem grow old, they stop shedding their shells. Those that have lived a long, long time have shells green with moss.

name: Vanillish

attacks.name: Ice Shard
attacks.text: If your opponent's Active Pokémon is a Fighting Pokémon, this attack does 30 more damage.

flavorText: Hot days cause its body to melt. It can be restored by refreezing it, but the process leaves its body slightly warped.

name: Voltorb
flavorText: It looks just like a Poké Ball. It is dangerous because it may electrocute or explode on touch.

name: Unown [H]

abilities.name: HEAL
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), if you have Unown H, Unown E, Unown A, and Unown L on your Bench, you may remove all Special Conditions from 1 of your Active Pokémon.

attacks.name: Hidden Power
attacks.text: Discard a card from your hand. (If you can't discard a card from your hand, this attack does nothing.)

flavorText: Shaped like ancient writing, it is a huge mystery whether language or UNOWN came first.

name: Sharpedo

abilities.name: Rough Skin
abilities.text: If this Pokémon is your Active Pokémon and is damaged by an opponent's attack (even if this Pokémon is Knocked Out), put 2 damage counters on the Attacking Pokémon.

attacks.name: Hard Bite
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 more damage.

flavorText: Its fangs rip through sheet iron. It swims at 75 mph and is known as

name: Staryu
flavorText: It appears in large numbers by seashores. At night, its central core flashes with a red light.

name: Luvdisc

attacks.name: Love Call
attacks.text: Choose 1 of your opponent's Pokémon. Search your deck for a Pokémon that is the same type as the Pokémon you chose, show it to your opponent, and put it into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.

attacks.name: Sweet Kiss
attacks.text: Your opponent may draw a card.

flavorText: It lives in warm seas. It is said that a couple finding this Pokémon will be blessed with eternal love.

name: Typhlosion

attacks.name: Fire Spin
attacks.text: Discard 2 Energy attached to Typhlosion.

flavorText: It has a secret, devastating move. It rubs its blazing fur together to cause huge explosions.

attacks.name: Sand Pit
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon can't retreat until the end of your opponent's next turn.

attacks.name: Dragonbreath
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed. If tails, this attack does nothing.

name: Croconaw

attacks.name: Big Bite
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon can't retreat during your opponent's next turn.

flavorText: If it loses a fang, a new one grows back in its place. There are always 48 fangs lining its mouth.

name: Krookodile-EX
rules: Pokémon-EX rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Second Bite
attacks.text: This attack does 10 more damage for each damage counter on your opponent's Active Pokémon.

attacks.name: Megaton Fang
attacks.text: Discard a card from your hand. If you can't discard a card, this attack does nothing.

attacks.name: Body Slam
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

attacks.name: Double-edge
attacks.text: Loudred does 10 damage to itself.

name: Zubat

attacks.name: Skill Dive
attacks.text: This attack does 10 damage to 1 of your opponent's Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: Even though it has no eyes, it can sense obstacles using ultrasonic waves it emits from it mouth.

name: Garbodor

abilities.name: Poisonous Puddle
abilities.text: Once during your turn, if a Stadium is in play, you may make your opponent's Active Pokémon Poisoned.

flavorText: This Pokémon eats trash, which turns into poison inside its body. The main component of the poison depends on what sort of trash was eaten.

attacks.name: Mid-air Strike
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 30 damage plus 30 more damage.

name: Starmie

attacks.name: Recover
attacks.text: Discard an Energy attached to this Pokémon and heal all damage from it.

attacks.name: Core Splash
attacks.text: If this Pokémon has any Psychic Energy attached to it, this attack does 30 more damage.

flavorText: Its central core glows with the seven colors of the rainbow. Some people value the core as a gem.

name: Feebas

attacks.name: Splashing Dodge
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, prevent all effects of attacks, including damage, done to this Pokémon during your opponent's next turn.

flavorText: A tough Pokémon that is perfectly fine even in dirty water. However, due to its ragged, shabby appearance, it isn't popular.

name: Marill
flavorText: The oil-filled tail functions as a buoy, so it's fine even in rivers with strong currents.

name: Sunflora

abilities.name: Grass Whistle
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may remove 1 damage counter from each of your Grass Pokémon. You can't use more than 1 Grass Whistle Poké-Power each turn. This power can't be used if Sunflora is affected by a Special Condition.

attacks.name: Petal Dance
attacks.text: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 30 damage times the number of heads. If you get 2 or more heads, Sunflora is now Confused.

flavorText: It gets energy from warm sunlight and is known for its habit of moving in pursuit of it.

name: Banette

ancientTrait.name: Δ Evolution
ancientTrait.text: You may play this card from your hand to evolve a Pokémon during your first turn or the turn you play that Pokémon.

attacks.name: Evolution Jammer
attacks.text: Your opponent can't play any Pokémon from his or her hand to evolve his or her Pokémon during his or her next turn.

attacks.name: Curse Deeply
attacks.text: Put 5 damage counters on your opponent's Active Pokémon.

flavorText: Strong feelings of hatred turned a puppet into a Pokémon. If it opens its mouth, its cursed energy escapes.

name: Swanna

attacks.name: Wing Dance
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, prevent all effects of attacks, including damage, done to this Pokémon during your opponent's next turn.

attacks.name: Air Slash
attacks.text: Discard an Energy attached to this Pokémon.

flavorText: It administers sharp, powerful pecks with its bill. It whips its long neck to deliver forceful repeated strikes.

abilities.name: Ancient Protection
abilities.text: Each of your Omanyte, Omastar, Kabuto, Kabutops, and Kabutops ex has no Weakness.

attacks.name: Granite Head
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, any damage done to Kabuto by attacks is reduced by 10 (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

name: Alomomola

attacks.name: Mysterious Beam
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, discard an Energy attached to the Defending Pokémon.

attacks.name: Double Slap
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 50 damage times the number of heads.

flavorText: The special membrane enveloping Alomomola has the ability to heal wounds.

name: Charmeleon

attacks.name: Slam
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 30 damage times the number of heads.

flavorText: In the rocky mountains where Charmeleon live, their fiery tails shine at night like stars.

name: Arcanine

abilities.name: Warming Up
abilities.text: If this Pokémon has a Burning Scarf attached, it gets +100 HP.

flavorText: The sight of it running over 6,200 miles in a single day and night has captivated many people.

name: Ditto

abilities.name: Ditto DNA
abilities.text: As long as Ditto is your Active Pokémon, its maximum HP is the same as your opponent's Active Pokémon. Ditto can use the attacks of that Pokémon as its own. (You still need the necessary Energy to use each attack.) If that Pokémon is no longer your opponent's Active Pokémon, choose 1 of your opponent's Active Pokémon for Ditto to copy.

flavorText: It has the ability to reconstitute its entire cellular structure to transform into whatever it sees.

attacks.name: Vine Tease
attacks.text: Look at your Prize cards without showing your opponent. Choose 1 of the Prize cards and switch it with the top card of your deck without looking at the top card of your deck. If you have no cards in your deck, this attack does nothing.

attacks.name: Wiggle
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Confused. If tails, the Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned.

name: Magnemite

attacks.name: Searching Magnet
attacks.text: Search your deck for up to 3 Metal Energy cards, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

flavorText: It sends out electromagnetic waves, which let it float through the air. Touching it while it's eating electricity will give you a full-body shock.

attacks.name: Fast Evolution
attacks.text: Search your deck for an Evolution card, show it to your opponent, and put it into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.

name: Hydreigon

abilities.name: Weed Out
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may choose 3 of your Benched Pokémon. Then, discard your other Benched Pokémon.

attacks.name: Dark Destruction
attacks.text: You may discard an Energy from this Pokémon. If you do, discard an Energy from your opponent's Active Pokémon.

flavorText: The heads on their arms do not have brains. They use all three heads to consume and destroy everything.

name: Oshawott

attacks.name: Razor Shell
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 more damage.

flavorText: The scalchop on its stomach is made from the same elements as claws. It detaches the scalchop for use as a blade.

name: Kangaskhan

attacks.name: Split Spiral Punch
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon is now Confused.

attacks.name: Dizzy Punch
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 50 damage times the number of heads.

flavorText: If it is safe, the young gets out of the belly pouch to play. The adult keeps a close eye on the youngster.

attacks.name: Fruition
attacks.text: Move 1 Energy card attached to Tropius to another of your Pokémon and remove 4 damage counters from that Pokémon (all if there are less than 4).

name: Manaphy

abilities.name: Blessings of the Deep
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may heal 20 damage from 1 of your Pokémon that has any Water Energy attached to it.

flavorText: It is born with a wondrous power that lets it bond with any kind of Pokémon.

name: Miltank

attacks.name: Sitdown Splash
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 more damage.

flavorText: If it is around babies, it milk it produces contains much more nutrition than usual.

name: Larvesta

attacks.name: Singe
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Burned.

flavorText: It protects itself with flame. Long years ago, people believed Larvesta had a nest on the sun.

name: Kyurem-EX
rules: Pokémon-EX rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Frozen Wings
attacks.text: Discard a Special Energy attached to the Defending Pokémon.

attacks.name: Hail Blizzard
attacks.text: This Pokémon can't use Hail Blizzard during your next turn.

name: Munna

attacks.name: Focused Wish
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 more damage.

flavorText: This Pokémon appears before people and Pokémon who are having nightmares and eats those dreams.

abilities.name: Psychic Wing
abilities.text: If Vibrava has any Psychic Energy attached to it, the Retreat Cost for Vibrava is 0.

attacks.name: Quick Blow
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 damage plus 20 more damage.

name: Solosis

attacks.name: Mini Link
attacks.text: If Solosis is on your Bench, this attack does 30 more damage.

flavorText: They drive away attackers by unleashing psychic power. They can use telepathy to talk with others.

name: Bergmite

attacks.name: Break Open
attacks.text: If your opponent has a Stadium card in play, discard it. If you do, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: Using air of -150 degrees Fahrenheit, they freeze opponents solid. They live in herds above the snow line on mountains.

name: Dusclops

attacks.name: Confuse Ray
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Confused.

attacks.name: Cursed Drop
attacks.text: Put 4 damage counters on your opponent's Pokémon in any way you like.

flavorText: Anyone who dares peer into its body to see its spectral ball of fire will have their spirit stolen away.

name: Golett

attacks.name: Smash Punch
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.

flavorText: Ancient science fashioned this Pokémon from clay. It's been active for thousands of years.

name: Samurott

abilities.name: Shell Armor
abilities.text: Any damage done to this Pokémon by attacks is reduced by 20 (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

attacks.name: Hydro Pump
attacks.text: Does 10 more damage for each Water Energy attached to this Pokémon.

flavorText: Part of the armor on its anterior legs becomes a giant sword. Its cry alone is enough to intimidate most enemies.

abilities.name: Body Odor
abilities.text: As long as Weezing is the Active Pokémon, put 1 damage counter on each of your opponent's Pokémon that has any Poké-Bodies between turns.

attacks.name: Mist Attack
attacks.text: Does 10 damage to each of your opponent's Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

name: Golem

attacks.name: Selfdestruct
attacks.text: Does 20 damage to each Pokémon on each player's Bench. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.) Golem does 100 damage to itself.

flavorText: Its boulder-like body is extremely hard. It can easily withstand dynamite blasts without damage.

name: Team Aqua Admin
rules: Attach a basic Energy card from your discard pile to your Active Team Aqua Pokémon.
You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn (before your attack).

name: Pyroar

attacks.name: Swirling Inferno
attacks.text: Discard all Pokémon Tool cards and Special Energy from each of your opponent's Pokémon.

flavorText: The males are usually lazy, but when attacked by a strong foe, a male will protect its friends with no regard for its own safety.

name: Shaymin

abilities.name: Floral Heal
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may heal 20 damage from your Active Grass Pokémon.

flavorText: The blooming of Gracidea flowers confers the power of flight upon it. Feelings of gratitude are the message it delivers.

name: Bergmite

attacks.name: Ice Block
attacks.text: Heal 30 damage from this Pokémon.

flavorText: Using air of -150 degrees Fahrenheit, they freeze opponents solid. They live in herds above the snow line on mountains.

name: Dark Exeggutor

attacks.name: Triple Headbutt
attacks.text: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 10 damage times the number of heads.

attacks.name: MAX Burst
attacks.text: Flip a number of coins equal to the number of Energy cards attached to your opponent's Pokémon. This attack does 20 damage times the number of heads.

flavorText: If one of its heads falls off, it turns into an Exeggcute, which begins to look for other Exeggcutes using a special form of telepathy.

name: Machoke

attacks.name: Karate Chop
attacks.text: Does 50 damage minus 10 damage for each damage counter on Machoke.

attacks.name: Submission
attacks.text: Machoke does 20 damage to itself.

flavorText: Its muscular body is so powerful that it must wear a power-save belt to help regulate its motions.

abilities.name: Solid Shell
abilities.text: Any damage done to Anorith by attacks is reduced by 10 (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

attacks.name: Fury Cutter
attacks.text: Flip 3 coins. If 1 of them is heads, this attack does 10 damage plus 10 more damage. If 2 of them are heads, this attack does 10 damage plus 30 more damage. If all of them are heads, this attack does 10 damage plus 50 more damage.

name: Seadra

attacks.name: Water Arrow
attacks.text: This attack does 30 damage to 1 of your opponent's Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: Its body bristles with sharp spikes. Carelessly trying to touch it could cause fainting from the spikes.

attacks.name: Plasma
attacks.text: If there are any Lightning Energy cards in your discard pile, flip a coin. If heads, attach 1 of them to Raichu.

attacks.name: Shock Bolt
attacks.text: Discard all Lightning Energy cards attached to Raichu or this attack does nothing.

name: Alolan Graveler
flavorText: When two Graveler fight each other, it fills the surroundings with flashes of light and sound. People call it the "fireworks of the earth."

name: Dark Weezing

attacks.name: Mass Explosion
attacks.text: Does 20 damage times the total number of Koffings, Weezings, and Dark Weezings in play (Apply Weakness and Resistance.). Then, this attack does 20 damage to each Koffing, Weezing, and Dark Weezing (even your own). Don't apply Weakness and Resistance.

attacks.name: Stun Gas
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned; if tails, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: Researchers have observed these Pokémon reproducing in industrial waste sites.

attacks.name: Retaliate
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 10 damage times the number of damage counters on Horsea.

attacks.name: Smokescreen
attacks.text: If the Defending Pokémon tries to attack during your opponent's next turn, your opponent flips a coin. If tails, that attack does nothing.

name: Vanilluxe

attacks.name: Double Freeze
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 40 damage times the number of heads. If either of them is heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: If both heads get angry simultaneously, this Pokémon expels a blizzard, burying everything in snow.

name: Muk

attacks.name: Sludge Drag
attacks.text: Switch the Defending Pokémon with 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. The new Defending Pokémon is now Confused and Poisoned.

attacks.name: Pester
attacks.text: If the Defending Pokémon is affected by a Special Condition, this attack does 50 damage plus 30 more damage.

flavorText: Its body is made of a powerful poison. Touching it accidentally will cause a fever that requires bed rest.

abilities.name: Maternal Comfort
abilities.text: At any times between turns, remove 1 damage counter from each of your Pokémon. You can't use more than 1 Maternal Comfort Poké-Body between turns.

attacks.name: Ruthless Tail
attacks.text: Does 50 damage plus 10 more damage for each of your opponent's Benched Pokémon.

name: Empoleon

attacks.name: Total Command
attacks.text: This attack does 20 damage for each Benched Pokémon (both yours and your opponent's).

attacks.name: Whirlpool
attacks.text: Discard an Energy from your opponent's Active Pokémon.

flavorText: The three horns that extend from its beak attest to its power. The leader has the biggest horns.

name: Axew

attacks.name: Dual Chop
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 10 damage times the number of heads.

flavorText: They use their tusks to crush the berries they eat. Repeated regrowth makes their tusks strong and sharp.

name: Galarian Zigzagoon

attacks.name: Pin Missile
attacks.text: Flip 4 coins. This attack does 10 damage for each heads.

flavorText: Its restlessness has it constantly running around. If it sees another Pokémon, it will purposely run into them in order to start a fight.

name: Litwick

attacks.name: Find a Friend
attacks.text: Search your deck for a Fire Pokémon, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

flavorText: While shining a light and pretending to be a guide, it leeches off the life force of any who follow it.

name: Drifblim

attacks.name: Wind Wave
attacks.text: Search your discard pile for up to 5 in any combination of Pokémon and Supporter cards. Show them to your opponent and shuffle them into your deck.

attacks.name: Explosive Smoke
attacks.text: Does 10 damage to each Benched Pokémon (both yours and your opponent's). (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: It's drowzy in daytime, but flies off in the evening in big groups. No one knows where they go.

name: Clawitzer

attacks.name: Reverse Thrust
attacks.text: Switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

attacks.name: Splash Cannon
attacks.text: This attack does 20 more damage for each Water Energy attached to this Pokémon.

flavorText: Their enormous claws launch cannonballs of water powerful enough to pierce tanker hulls.

name: Pachirisu

attacks.name: Minor Errand-Running
attacks.text: Search your deck for a basic Energy card, show it to your opponent, and put it into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.

attacks.name: Thunder Jolt
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If tails, Pachirisu does 10 damage to itself.

flavorText: It makes fur balls that crackle with static electricity. It stores them with berries in tree holes.

abilities.name: Self-healing
abilities.text: Whenever you attach a Water Energy card from your hand to Vaporeon, remove all Special Conditions affecting Vaporeon.

attacks.name: Hypnosplash
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon is now Asleep.

attacks.name: Aqua Trick
attacks.text: If the Defending Pokémon has any Energy cards attached to it, flip a coin. If heads, choose 1 of those Energy cards and move it to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. If your opponent has no Benched Pokémon, ignore this effect.

abilities.name: Baby Evolution
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may put Hitmonlee, Hitmonchan, or Hitmontop from your hand onto Tyrogue (this counts as evolving Tyrogue) and remove all damage counters from Tyrogue.

attacks.name: Desperate Punch
attacks.text: Does 10 damage times the number of Pokémon in play your opponent has more than you.

attacks.name: Detect
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, prevent all effects of an attack, including damage, done to Grovyle during your opponent's next turn.

name: Reshiram

attacks.name: Outrage
attacks.text: Does 10 more damage for each damage counter on this Pokémon.

attacks.name: Blue Flare
attacks.text: Discard 2 Fire Energy attached to this Pokémon.

flavorText: This Pokémon appears in legends. It sends flames into the air from its tail, burning up everything around it.

abilities.name: Hard Cocoon
abilities.text: During your opponent's turn, if Cascoon would be damaged by an opponent's attack (after applying Weakness and Resistance), flip a coin. If heads, reduce that damage by 30.

attacks.name: Poison Thread
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned.

name: Staryu

attacks.name: Continuous Spin
attacks.text: Flip a coin until you get tails. This attack does 10 damage times the number of heads.

flavorText: It appears in large numbers by seashores. At night, its central core flashes with a red light.

attacks.name: Healing Pollen
attacks.text: Flip 3 coins. For each heads, remove 1 damage counter from each of your Pokémon. If a Pokémon has fewer damage counters than the number of heads, remove all of them.

attacks.name: Sonic Distortion
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. If 1 or both of them are heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Confused.

name: Cinderace

abilities.name: Libero
abilities.text: Once during your turn, when this Pokémon moves from your Bench to the Active Spot, you may attach up to 2 Fire Energy cards from your discard pile to it.

attacks.name: Flare Striker
attacks.text: Discard 2 Energy from this Pokémon.

flavorText: It's skilled at both offense and defense, and it gets pumped up when cheered on. But if it starts showboating, it could put itself in a tough spot.

attacks.name: Poison Fang
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned.

name: Mudkip

attacks.name: Water Reserve
attacks.text: Search your deck for up to 3 Water Energy cards, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

flavorText: To alert it, the fin on its head senses the flow of water. It has the strength to heft boulders.

name: Togepi

attacks.name: Snivel
attacks.text: If the Defending Pokémon attacks Togepi during your opponent's next turn, any damage done to Togepi is reduced by 20 (before applying Weakness and Resistance). (Benching either Pokémon ends this effect.)

attacks.name: Mini-Metronome
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, choose 1 of the Defending Pokémon's attacks. Mini-Metronome copies that attack except for its Energy cost. (You must still do anything else in order to use that attack.) (No matter what type the Defending Pokémon is, Togepi's type is still Colorless.) Togepi performs that attack.

flavorText: Still only a hatchling, it uses poison to chase off its enemies when threatened.

name: Hitmonchan

attacks.name: Detect
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, prevent all effects of attacks, including damage, done to Hitmonchan during your opponent's next turn.

attacks.name: Sky Uppercut
attacks.text: This attack's damage isn't affected by Resistance.

flavorText: Its punches slice the air. However, it seems to need a short break after fighting for three minutes.

abilities.name: Honeycomb Defender
abilities.text: If Scizor has 6 or more damage counters on it, any damage done to Scizor by attacks is reduced by 40 (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

attacks.name: Accelerate
attacks.text: If the Defending Pokémon is Knocked Out by this attack, prevent all effects of an attack, including damage, done to Scizor during your opponent's next turn.

attacks.name: Pound Down
attacks.text: If you don't have any Pokémon with any Poké-Powers in play, this attack does 40 damage plus 30 more damage.

name: Magnemite

attacks.name: Lock-on
attacks.text: During your next turn, treat any tails flipped when using Magnemite's Electric Bolt attack as if they were heads. (Benching or evolving either Pokémon ends this effect.)

attacks.name: Electric Bolt
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. If both are heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed. If either of them is tails, this attack does nothing (not even damage).

flavorText: These Pokémon are attracted to electrical emissions and will often follow people using PokéGear.

name: Golbat

abilities.name: Sneaky Bite
abilities.text: When you play this Pokémon from your hand to evolve 1 of your Pokémon, you may put 2 damage counters on 1 of your opponent's Pokémon.

attacks.name: Swoop Across
attacks.text: This attack does 10 damage to each of your opponent's Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: Once it bites, it will not stop draining energy from the victim even if it gets too heavy to fly.

name: Dewott

attacks.name: Aqua Tail
attacks.text: Flip a coin for each Water Energy attached to this Pokémon. This attack does 10 more damage for each heads.

flavorText: Scalchop techniques differ from one Dewott to another. It never neglects maintaining its scalchops.

name: Golurk

ancientTrait.name: θ Stop
ancientTrait.text: Prevent all effects of your opponent's Pokémon's Abilities done to this Pokémon.

abilities.name: Double Type
abilities.text: As long as this Pokémon is in play, it is Psychic and Fighting type.

attacks.name: Superpower
attacks.text: You may do 40 more damage. If you do, this Pokémon does 20 damage to itself.

flavorText: It flies across the sky at Mach speeds. Removing the seal on its chest makes its internal energy go out of control.

name: Gyarados-EX
rules: Pokémon-EX rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Stormy Seas
attacks.text: Flip a coin until you get tails. For each heads, search your deck for a Water Energy card and attach it to this Pokémon. Shuffle your deck afterward.

attacks.name: Splash Burn
attacks.text: This attack does 10 damage to each of your Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

name: Cleffa
rules: If this Baby Pokémon is your Active Pokémon and your opponent announces an attack, your opponent flips a coin (before doing anything else). If tails, your opponent's turn ends.

attacks.name: Eek
attacks.text: Draw 2 cards.

flavorText: Because of its unusual, star-like silhouette, people believe that it came here on a meteor.

attacks.name: Double Scratch
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 40 damage times the number of heads.

name: Prinplup

attacks.name: Fury Attack
attacks.text: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 30 damage times the number of heads.

flavorText: Because every Prinplup considers itself to be the most important, they can never form a group.

name: Graveler

attacks.name: Harden
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, whenever 30 or less damage is done to Graveler (after applying Weakness and Resistance), prevent that damage. (Any other effects of attacks still happen.)

flavorText: Rolls down slopes to move. It rolls over any obstacle without slowing or changing its direction.

abilities.name: Evolutionary Gas
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), when you play Skuntank from your hand to evolve 1 of your Active Pokémon, you may choose 1 of the Defending Pokémon. If that Pokémon tries to attack during your opponent's next turn, that attack does nothing.

attacks.name: Poison Claws
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned.

attacks.name: Plunder
attacks.text: Before doing damage, discard all Trainer cards attached to the Defending Pokémon.

name: Frillish
flavorText: With its thin, veil-like arms wrapped around the body of its opponent, it sinks to the ocean floor.

name: Volcarona

attacks.name: Cauterize
attacks.text: If your opponent's Active Pokémon has no damage counters on it before this attack does damage, this attack does nothing.

flavorText: According to legends, it was hatched from a flaming cocoon to save people and Pokémon that were suffering from the cold.

name: Galarian Linoone

attacks.name: Double-Edge
attacks.text: This Pokémon also does 20 damage to itself.

flavorText: It uses its long tongue to taunt opponents. Once the opposition is enraged, this Pokémon hurls itself at the opponent, tackling them forcefully.

abilities.name: Levitate
abilities.text: If Beldum has any Energy attached to it, Beldum's Retreat Cost is 0.

name: Kingdra

attacks.name: Brine
attacks.text: This attack does 90 damage to 1 of your opponent's Pokémon that has any damage counters on it. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

attacks.name: Tornado Shot
attacks.text: Discard a Water Energy from this Pokémon. This attack does 30 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: It is said that it usually hides in underwater caves. It can create whirlpools by yawning.

name: Wailord

abilities.name: Water Veil
abilities.text: Whenever you attach an Energy card from your hand to this Pokémon, remove all Special Conditions from it.

attacks.name: Hydro Pump
attacks.text: This attack does 40 more damage for each Water Energy attached to this Pokémon.

flavorText: It can sometimes knock out opponents with the shock created by breaching and crashing its big body onto the water.

name: Metang

attacks.name: Energy Crane
attacks.text: Search your discard pile for up to 2 Psychic Energy cards and attach them to your Pokémon in any way you like.

flavorText: When two Beldum fuse together, a magnetic nervous system places their brains in union.

name: Delibird

attacks.name: Snowy Present
attacks.text: Draw a card for each Water Energy attached to all of your Pokémon.

attacks.name: Hail
attacks.text: This attack does 10 damage to each of your opponent's Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: It nests at the edge of sharp cliffs. It spends all day carrying food to its awaiting chicks.

name: Cloyster

attacks.name: Headlock
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 30 more damage. If tails, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

attacks.name: Guard Press
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, this Pokémon takes 20 less damage from attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

flavorText: Its hard shell cannot be shattered—not even by a bomb. The contents of the shell remain unknown.

attacks.name: Damage Burn
attacks.text: If the Defending Pokémon already has any damage counters on it, this attack does 40 damage plus 20 more damage.

name: Turtonator

attacks.name: Flame Cloak
attacks.text: Attach a Fire Energy card from your discard pile to this Pokémon.

flavorText: The shell on its back is chemically unstable and explodes violently if struck. The hole in its stomach is its weak point.

name: Lairon

attacks.name: Take Down
attacks.text: Lairon does 20 damage to itself.

flavorText: It loves iron ore. Groups of them fight for territory by bashing one another with their steel bodies.

name: Skitty

attacks.name: Jump On
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 more damage.

flavorText: It can't stop itself from chasing moving things, and it runs in a circle, chasing its own tail.

name: Dusknoir

abilities.name: Shadow Void
abilities.text: As often as you like during your turn (before your attack), you may move 1 damage counter from 1 of your Pokémon to this Pokémon.

attacks.name: Pain Pellets
attacks.text: Put damage counters on 1 of your opponent's Pokémon equal to the number of damage counters on this Pokémon.

flavorText: It is said to take lost spirits into its pliant body and guide them home.

name: Manectric

attacks.name: Lightning Twister
attacks.text: Does 20 damage times the number of basic Energy cards attached to Manectric.

attacks.name: Chain Lightning
attacks.text: Does 20 damage to each of your opponent's Benched Pokémon that is the same type as the Defending Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: It discharges electricity from its mane. It creates a thundercloud overhead to drop lightning bolts.

name: Cubchoo

attacks.name: Powder Snow
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Asleep.

attacks.name: Rest
attacks.text: Heal 60 damage from this Pokémon. This Pokémon is now Asleep.

flavorText: When it is not feeling well, its mucus gets watery and the power of its Ice-type moves decreases.

name: Swampert

attacks.name: Water Arrow
attacks.text: This attack does 60 damage to 1 of your opponent's Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: It can swim while towing a large ship. It bashes down foes with a swing of its thick arms.

name: Tropical Wind
rules: Flip a coin. If heads, remove 2 damage counters from each Active Pokémon (remove 1 damage counter if a Pokémon has only 1). If tails, each Active Pokémon is now Asleep.

name: Moltres

attacks.name: Wildfire
attacks.text: You may discard any number of Fire Energy cards attached to Moltres when you use this attack. If you do, discard that many cards from the top of your opponent's deck.

attacks.name: Dive Bomb
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.

flavorText: Known as the legendary bird of fire. Every flap of its wings creates a dazzling flash of flames.

name: Dratini

attacks.name: Wrap
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: After a 10-hour struggle, a fisherman was able to pull one up and confirm its existence.

name: Forretress

abilities.name: Iron Shell
abilities.text: Whenever you attach a basic Energy card from your hand to Forretress (excluding effects of attacks), you may flip a coin. If tails, put 2 damage counters on each Pokémon (both yours and your opponent's) (excluding any Forretress).

attacks.name: Explosion
attacks.text: Forretress does 40 damage to itself.

flavorText: It is encased in a steel shell. Its peering eyes are all that can be seen of its mysterious innards.

name: Wailord

abilities.name: Sleeping Pulse
abilities.text: As long as Wailord remains Asleep between turns, remove 1 damage counter from Wailord.

attacks.name: Sink Deep
attacks.text: If Wailord has any damage counters on it, Wailord is now Asleep.

flavorText: The biggest of all Pokémon. It can dive to a depth of almost 10,000 feet on only one breath.

name: Mantine

attacks.name: Water Reserve
attacks.text: Search your deck for up to 3 Water Energy cards, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

flavorText: If it builds up enough speed swimming, it can jump out above the waves and glide for over 300 feet.

abilities.name: Synchronicity
abilities.text: You may attach any Technical Machine to Xatu.

attacks.name: Warp Hole
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, choose a card from your discard pile and put it on top of your deck.

name: Magneton

attacks.name: Zap Cannon
attacks.text: This Pokémon can't use Zap Cannon during your next turn.

flavorText: It has about three times the electrical power of Magnemite. For some reason, outbreaks of this Pokémon happen when lots of sunspots appear.

name: Pikachu

attacks.name: Thundershock
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: It occasionally uses an electric shock to recharge a fellow Pikachu that is in a weakened state.

name: Magmar

attacks.name: Heat Breath
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 30 more damage.

flavorText: Its entire body is burning. When it breathes, the temperature rises. When it sneezes, flames shoot out!

name: Onix-GX
rules: Pokémon-GX rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Bind
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

attacks.name: Rocky Avalanche-GX
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, this Pokémon takes 100 less damage from attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance). (You can't use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)

abilities.name: Install
abilities.text: As often as you like during your turn (before your attack), move a Technical Machine card attached to 1 of your Pokémon to another of your Pokémon. This power can't be used if Porygon-Z is affected by a Special Condition.

attacks.name: Learning
attacks.text: Search your deck for a Pokémon LV.X that levels up from 1 of your Pokémon, and put it onto that Pokémon. (This counts as leveling up that Pokémon.) Shuffle your deck afterward.

attacks.name: Overload
attacks.text: Does 40 damage plus 20 more damage for each Technical Machine card attached to Porygon-Z.

name: Cinderace

attacks.name: Pyro Ball
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Burned.

attacks.name: Burning Kick
attacks.text: Discard all Energy from this Pokémon.

flavorText: It juggles a pebble with its feet, turning it into a burning soccer ball. Its shots strike opponents hard and leave them scorched.
