name: Litwick

attacks.name: Find a Friend
attacks.text: Search your deck for a Fire Pokémon, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

flavorText: While shining a light and pretending to be a guide, it leeches off the life force of any who follow it.

name: Zekrom

attacks.name: Outrage
attacks.text: Does 10 more damage for each damage counter on this Pokémon.

attacks.name: Bolt Strike
attacks.text: This Pokémon does 40 damage to itself.

flavorText: This Pokémon appears in legends. In its tail, it has a giant generator that creates electricity.

name: Manaphy

abilities.name: Final Wish
abilities.text: When this Pokémon is Knocked Out by damage from an opponent's attack, search your deck for a card and put it into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.

attacks.name: Seafaring
attacks.text: Flip 3 coins. For each heads, attach a Water Energy card from your discard pile to your Benched Pokémon in any way you like.

flavorText: It is born with a wondrous power that lets it bond with any kind of Pokémon.

attacks.name: Hypnotic Gaze
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon is now Asleep.

attacks.name: Fire Ring
attacks.text: If your opponent has any Benched Pokémon, choose 1 of them and this attack does 10 damage to it. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

name: Milotic

attacks.name: TLC
attacks.text: Shuffle 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon that has any damage counters on it and all cards attached to it into their deck.

attacks.name: Ocean Cyclone
attacks.text: This attack does 10 damage to each of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: Milotic has provided inspiration to many artists. It has even been referred to as the most beautiful Pokémon of all.

name: Duosion

attacks.name: Double Link
attacks.text: If Solosis is on your Bench, this attack does 30 more damage. If Duosion is on your Bench, this attack does 60 more damage.

flavorText: When their two divided brains think the same thoughts, their psychic power is maximized.

attacks.name: Overrun
attacks.text: If your opponent has any Benched Pokémon, flip a coin. If heads, choose 1 of them and this attack does 10 damage to it. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

name: Dark Flaaffy

attacks.name: High Voltage
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent can't play Trainer cards during his or her next turn.

attacks.name: Stun Wave
attacks.text: If the Defending Pokémon has a Pokémon Power, that power stops working until the end of your next turn.

flavorText: As a result of storing massive amounts of electricity there, it can no longer grow wool on certain areas of its body.

name: Seel

attacks.name: Growl
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, any damage done by attacks from the Defending Pokémon is reduced by 20 (before applying Weakness and Resistance).

flavorText: Covered with light blue fur, its hide is thick and tough. It is active in bitter cold of -40 degrees Fahrenheit.

name: Pyroar

attacks.name: Crunch
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, discard an Energy attached to your opponent's Active Pokémon.

attacks.name: Royal Flare
attacks.text: Discard 2 Energy attached to this Pokémon.

flavorText: The male with the largest mane of fire is the leader of the pride.

name: Lunatone

abilities.name: Marvel Eyes
abilities.text: If you have Solrock in play, prevent all effects of attacks, including damage, done to any of your Lunatone or Solrock by your opponent's Pokémon LV.X.

attacks.name: Gravity Wave
attacks.text: Does 30 damage to each of your opponent's Benched Pokémon that doesn't have a Retreat Cost. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: Because it turns active on nights of the full moon, it is said to have some link to the lunar phases.

name: Bayleef

attacks.name: Sweet Scent
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads and if any of your Pokémon have any damage counters on them, then remove 2 damage counters from 1 of them (or 1 if it only has 1). If tails and if any of your opponent's Pokémon have any damage counters on them, choose 1 of them and remove 2 damage counters from it (or 1 if it only has 1).

attacks.name: Double Razor Leaf
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 40 damage times the number of heads.

flavorText: The scent of spices comes from around its neck. Somehow, sniffing it makes you want to fight.

name: Gastly

attacks.name: Lick
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

attacks.name: Night Shade
attacks.text: Does 10 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: Born from gases, anyone would faint if engulfed by its gaseous body, which contains poison.

name: Deoxys

attacks.name: Close Encounter
attacks.text: If you go first, you can use this attack on your first turn. Draw 2 cards.

attacks.name: Overdrive Smash
attacks.text: During your next turn, this Pokémon's Overdrive Smash attack does 60 more damage (before applying Weakness and Resistance).

flavorText: DNA from a space virus mutated and became a Pokémon. It appears where auroras are seen.

name: Dewgong

attacks.name: Ice Beam
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: Stores thermal energy in its body. Swims at a steady 8 knots even in intensely cold waters.

name: Dark Charmeleon

attacks.name: Fireball
attacks.text: Use this attack only if there are any Fire Energy cards attached to Dark Charmeleon. Flip a coin. If heads, discard 1 of those Energy cards. If tails, this attack does nothing (not even damage).

flavorText: Its tail is incredibly strong, able to lift up to 5 adult men.

name: Dark Flaaffy
rules: This Pokémon is both Lightning Darkness type.

attacks.name: Thunder Slash
attacks.text: If the Defending Pokémon is a Basic Pokémon, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed. Dark Flaaffy can't use Thunder Slash during your next turn.

attacks.name: Shake
attacks.text: If your opponent has any Benched Pokémon, he or she chooses 1 of them and switches it with the Defending Pokémon. (Do the damage before switching the Pokémon.)

attacks.name: Fire Tackle
attacks.text: Blaine's Growlithe does 10 damage to itself.

name: Plusle

attacks.name: Call for Family
attacks.text: Search your deck for a Basic Pokémon and put it onto your Bench. Shuffle your deck afterward.

attacks.name: – Boost
attacks.text: If Minun is on your Bench, this attack does 20 damage plus 20 more damage.

flavorText: It cheers on friends with pom-poms made of sparks. It drains power from telephone poles.

abilities.name: Extra Feather
abilities.text: Each of your Stage 2 Pokémon-ex does 10 more damage to the Defending Pokémon (before applying Weakness and Resistance).

attacks.name: Confuse Ray
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Confused.

name: Keldeo

attacks.name: Bail Out
attacks.text: Put a Water Pokémon from your discard pile into your hand.

attacks.name: Resolute Blade
attacks.text: This attack does 20 more damage for each of your opponent's Benched Pokémon.

flavorText: It crosses the world, running over the surfaces of oceans and rivers. It appears at scenic waterfronts.

attacks.name: Earthquake
attacks.text: Does 10 damage to each of your own Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

attacks.name: Double Stab
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 40 damage times the number of heads.

name: Mareep

attacks.name: Cotton Guard
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, any damage done to this Pokémon by attacks is reduced by 20 (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

attacks.name: Thunder Jolt
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If tails, this Pokémon does 10 damage to itself.

flavorText: Its fluffy coat swells to double when static electricity builds up. Touching it can be shocking.

name: Mudkip
flavorText: To alert it, the fin on its head senses the flow of water. It has the strength to heft boulders.

name: Magikarp

attacks.name: Flail
attacks.text: Does 10 damage times the number of damage counters on Magikarp.

flavorText: In the distant past, it was stronger than its horribly weak descendants that exist today.

abilities.name: Poison Structure
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), if Sidney's Stadium is in play, you may choose 1 of the Defending Pokémon. That Pokémon is now Poisoned. This power can't be used if Cacturne is affected by a Special Condition.

attacks.name: Pin Missile
attacks.text: Flip 4 coins. This attack does 20 damage times the number of heads.

name: Virizion-GX
rules: Pokémon-GX rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Double Draw
attacks.text: Draw 2 cards.

attacks.name: Sensitive Blade
attacks.text: If you played a Supporter card from your hand during this turn, this attack does 80 more damage.

attacks.name: Breeze Away-GX
attacks.text: Put any number of your Pokémon in play and all cards attached to them into your hand. (You can't use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)

attacks.name: Thunder Throw
attacks.text: Choose 2 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. This attack does 10 damage to each of them. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

attacks.name: Repeat Lightning
attacks.text: Does 30 damage plus 10 more damage for each of your opponent's Benched Pokémon that has any damage counters on it.

name: Blastoise & Piplup-GX
rules: TAG TEAM rule: When your TAG TEAM is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Splash Maker
attacks.text: You may attach up to 3 Water Energy cards from your hand to your Pokémon in any way you like. If you do, heal 50 damage from those Pokémon for each card you attached to them in this way.

attacks.name: Bubble Launcher-GX
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed. If this Pokémon has at least 3 extra Water Energy attached to it (in addition to this attack's cost), this attack does 150 more damage. (You can't use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)

name: Purugly

attacks.name: High Hat
attacks.text: Choose 1 card from your opponent's hand without looking and discard it.

attacks.name: Body Slam
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: It is a brazen brute that barges its way into another Pokémon's nest and claims it as its own.

abilities.name: Temperamental Weather
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may search your deck for Sunny Castform, Rain Castform, or Snow-cloud Castform and switch it with Castform. (Any cards attached to Castform, damage counters, Special Conditions, and effects on it are now on the new Pokémon.) Shuffle Castform back into your deck. You can't use more than 1 Temperamental Weather Poké-Power each turn.

attacks.name: Holon Draw
attacks.text: Draw a card. If Castform has any Holon Energy cards attached to it, draw 2 more cards.

name: Pinsir

attacks.name: Charging Horn
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 10 damage plus 20 more damage.

flavorText: With its horns, it digs burrows to sleep in at night. In the morning, damp soil clings to its body.

name: Amaura
rules: Put this card onto your Bench only with the effect of Sail Fossil
flavorText: This ancient Pokémon was restored from part of its body that had been frozen in ice for over 100 million years.

name: Starmie

attacks.name: Deep Sea Swirl
attacks.text: Shuffle your hand into your deck. Then, draw 7 cards.

attacks.name: Balance Bind
attacks.text: If you and your opponent have the same number of Benched Pokémon, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: At the center of its body is a red core, which sends mysterious radio signals into the night sky.

name: Electrode

abilities.name: Magnetic Draw
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may draw cards until you have 4 cards in your hand.

flavorText: It is known to drift on winds if it is bloated to bursting with stored electricity.

name: Politoed

abilities.name: Frog Song
abilities.text: Whenever Politoed's attack damages the Defending Pokémon (after applying Weakness and Resistance), if there are more than 3 Poliwags, Poliwhirls, Poliwraths, and/or Politoeds in play (including your opponent's), that attack does 40 more damage (no matter how many heads you get). This power stops working while Politoed is Asleep, Confused, or Paralyzed.

attacks.name: Doubleslap
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 40 damage times the number of heads.

flavorText: Whenever three or more of these get together, they sing in a loud voice that sounds like bellowing.

name: Gyarados

attacks.name: Berserker Splash
attacks.text: This attack does 10 damage to each Benched Pokémon (both yours and your opponent's). (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

attacks.name: Aqua Tail
attacks.text: Flip a coin for each Water Energy attached to this Pokémon. This attack does 30 more damage for each heads.

flavorText: Rarely seen in the wild. Huge and vicious, it is capable of destroying entire cities in a rage.

abilities.name: Reactive Colors
abilities.text: If Kecleon has any React Energy cards attached to it, Kecleon is Grass, Fire, Water, Lightning, Psychic, and Fighting type.

attacks.name: Tongue Whip
attacks.text: Choose 1 of your opponent's Pokémon. This attack does 10 damage to that Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

attacks.name: Quick Attack
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 10 damage plus 20 more damage.

name: Avalugg

attacks.name: Frozen Ground
attacks.text: Your opponent can't play any Stadium cards from their hand during their next turn.

flavorText: The way several Bergmite huddle on its back makes it look like an aircraft carrier made of ice.

name: Buizel
flavorText: It inflates the flotation sac around its neck and pokes its head out of the water to see what is going on.

name: Vanilluxe

attacks.name: Hail
attacks.text: This attack does 20 damage to each of your opponent's Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

attacks.name: Frozen Breath
attacks.text: You may discard 2 Water Energy from this Pokémon. If you do, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: Each of its two heads has a brain, and when they are in agreement, it attacks its enemies by exhaling a violent blizzard.

name: Hitmonlee
flavorText: If it starts kicking repeatedly, both legs will stretch even longer to strike a fleeing foe.

name: Lapras

attacks.name: Seafaring
attacks.text: Flip 3 coins. For each heads, attach a Water Energy card from your discard pile to your Benched Pokémon in any way you like.

attacks.name: Hydro Pump
attacks.text: This attack does 20 more damage for each Water Energy attached to this Pokémon.

flavorText: They have gentle hearts. Because they rarely fight, many have been caught. Their number has dwindled.

attacks.name: Grind
attacks.text: Does 10 damage times the amount of Energy attached to Torkoal.

abilities.name: Snappy Move
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), if Aipom is on your Bench, you may draw a card. Then, discard all cards attached to Aipom and put Aipom on the bottom of your deck. You can't use more than 1 Snappy Move Poké-Power each turn.

attacks.name: Snap Tail
attacks.text: Choose 1 of your opponent's Pokémon. This attack does 10 damage to that Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

name: Alolan Golem

attacks.name: Electromagnetic Bomb
attacks.text: Move any number of Lightning Energy from your Benched Pokémon to this Pokémon. This attack does 20 damage for each Energy card you moved in this way.

attacks.name: Super Zap Cannon
attacks.text: Discard 2 Energy from this Pokémon.

flavorText: It's grumpy and stubborn. If you upset it, it discharges electricity from the surface of its body and growls with a voice like thunder.

attacks.name: Amnesia
attacks.text: Choose 1 of the Defending Pokémon's attacks. That Pokémon can't use that attack during your opponent's next turn.

name: Tyrogue

abilities.name: Sweet Sleeping Face
abilities.text: As long as Tyrogue is Asleep, prevent all damage done to Tyrogue by attacks.

attacks.name: Mischievous Punch
attacks.text: This attack's damage isn't affected by Weakness or Resistance. Tyrogue is now Asleep.

flavorText: Even though it is small, it can't be ignored because it will slug any handy target without warning.

attacks.name: Feint Attack
attacks.text: Choose 1 of your opponent's Pokémon. This attack does 20 damage to that Pokémon. This attack's damage isn't affected by Weakness, Resistance, Poké-Powers, Poké-Bodies, or any other effects on that Pokémon.

attacks.name: Flamethrower
attacks.text: Discard a Fire Energy card attached to Houndoom.

name: Unown [S]

abilities.name: Set
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), if you have Unown S, Unown E, and Unown T on your Bench, you may flip a coin. If heads, search your discard pile for an Energy card, show it to your opponent, and put it on top of your deck.

attacks.name: Hidden Power
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Confused.

flavorText: Shaped like ancient writing, it is a huge mystery whether language or UNOWN came first.

name: Azumarill

attacks.name: Deep Dive
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. For each heads, heal 40 damage from this Pokémon.

attacks.name: Aqua Sonic
attacks.text: This attack's damage isn't affected by Resistance.

flavorText: Its long ears are superb sensors. It can distinguish the movements of things in water and tell what they are.

name: Panpour
flavorText: It does not thrive in dry environments. It keeps itself damp by shooting water stored in its head tuft from its tail.

attacks.name: Singe
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Burned.

attacks.name: Dark Shock
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

name: Musharna

attacks.name: Precognitive Dream
attacks.text: Draw 3 cards. This Pokémon is now Asleep.

attacks.name: Psybeam
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon is now Confused.

flavorText: The dream mist coming from its forehead changes into many different colors depending on the dream that was eaten.

name: Team Aqua Grunt
rules: Discard a card from your hand. (If you can't discard a card, you can't play this card.) Draw 3 cards. If you discarded a Team Aqua Pokémon, draw 1 more card.
You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn (before your attack).

name: Vanillite

attacks.name: Snow Squall
attacks.text: This attack's damage isn't affected by Resistance.

flavorText: Theoretically, this Pokémon formed from icicles bathed in energy from the morning sun. Their breath is -58° F.

attacks.name: Turnaround Standby
attacks.text: During your next turn, any damage done by Mawile's Swallow or Bite to your opponent's Active Pokémon is increased by 40 (before applying Weakness and Resistance).

attacks.name: Swallow
attacks.text: Remove from Mawile the number of damage counters equal to the damage you did to the Defending Pokémon.

name: Clefairy

attacks.name: Doubleslap
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 10 damage times the number of heads.

attacks.name: Squaredance
attacks.text: Flip a number of coins equal to the total number of Pokémon in play. For each heads, you may search your deck for a basic Energy card, show it to your opponent, and put it into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.

flavorText: The moonlight that it stores in the wings on its back apparently gives it the ability to float in midair.

name: Marshtomp

attacks.name: Muddy Water
attacks.text: This attack does 20 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: Living on muddy ground that provides poor footing has made its legs sturdy.

attacks.name: Scary Face
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon can't attack or retreat during your opponent's next turn.

name: Toxicroak-EX
rules: Pokémon-EX rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Triple Poison
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Poisoned. Put 3 damage counters instead of 1 on that Pokémon between turns.

attacks.name: Smash Uppercut
attacks.text: This attack's damage isn't affected by Resistance.

attacks.name: Super Hypno Wave
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon is now Asleep.

abilities.name: Soak Up
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may take up to 2 Grass Energy cards attached to your other Pokémon and attach them to Erika's Bellsprout. This power can't be used if Erika's Bellsprout is Asleep, Confused, or Paralyzed.

attacks.name: Stretch Vine
attacks.text: Choose 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon, and this attack does 10 damage to it. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

name: Snorlax

abilities.name: Thick Skinned
abilities.text: Snorlax can't become Asleep, Confused, Paralyzed, or Poisoned. This power can't be used if Snorlax is already Asleep, Confused, or Paralyzed.

attacks.name: Body Slam
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: Very lazy. Just eats and sleeps. As its rotund bulk builds, it becomes steadily more slothful.

attacks.name: Double Lariat
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 30 damage times the number of heads.

attacks.name: Rock Tumble
attacks.text: This attack's damage isn't affected by Resistance.

name: Graveler

attacks.name: Earthquake
attacks.text: Does 10 damage to each of your own Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

attacks.name: Rock Tumble
attacks.text: Don't apply Resistance.

flavorText: With a free and uncaring nature, it doesn't mind if pieces break off while it rolls down mountains.

attacks.name: Triple Smash
attacks.text: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 20 damage plus 20 more damage for each heads.

name: Fletchinder

attacks.name: Searing Flame
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Burned.

flavorText: Its speed right after takeoff already puts it in the top speed class of all bird Pokémon.

name: Scizor

attacks.name: Special Blow
attacks.text: If the Defending Pokémon has any Special Energy cards attached to it, this attack does 30 damage plus 50 more damage.

attacks.name: X-Scissor
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 50 damage plus 40 more damage.

flavorText: It has a steel-hard body. It intimidates foes by upraising its eye-patterned pincers.

name: Graveler

attacks.name: Double-edge
attacks.text: Graveler does 10 damage to itself.

flavorText: It rolls on mountain paths to move. Once it builds momentum, no Pokémon can stop it without difficulty.

name: Minun

attacks.name: Call for Family
attacks.text: Search your deck for up to 2 Basic Pokémon and put them onto your Bench. Shuffle your deck afterward.

attacks.name: Tag Team Boost
attacks.text: If Plusle is on your Bench, this attack does 10 damage plus 20 more damage.

flavorText: Exposure to electricity from Minun and Plusle promotes blood circulation and relaxes muscles.

name: Simipour

attacks.name: Scald
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon is now Burned.

attacks.name: Fury Swipes
attacks.text: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 40 damage times the number of heads.

flavorText: The high-pressure water expelled from its tail is so powerful, it can destroy a concrete wall.

name: Alomomola

attacks.name: Borne Ashore
attacks.text: Put a Basic Pokémon from either player's discard pile onto its owner's Bench.

flavorText: It uses its special mucus to close the wounds of injured Pokémon. The reason for this behavior remains unknown.

name: Weezing

abilities.name: Toxic Virus
abilities.text: At any time between turns, each player puts 1 more damage counter on his or her Poisoned Pokémon.

attacks.name: Offensive Gas
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Confused and Poisoned.

flavorText: It grows by feeding on gases released by garbage. Though very rare, triplets have been found.

name: Smeargle

attacks.name: Paint
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, choose a type (other than Colorless) and put a Coloring counter on the Defending Pokémon. That Pokémon is now the type you chose. If it already had a Coloring counter, remove the old one. If tails, this attack does nothing.

flavorText: Once it becomes an adult, it has a tendency to let its comrades plant footprints on its back.

name: Lampent

attacks.name: Fireworks
attacks.text: Discard an Energy from this Pokémon.

flavorText: The spirits it absorbs fuel its baleful fire. It hangs around hospitals waiting for people to pass on.

name: Magnezone

abilities.name: Magnetic Circuit
abilities.text: As often as you like during your turn (before your attack), you may attach a Metal Energy card from your hand to 1 of your Pokémon.

attacks.name: Zap Cannon
attacks.text: This Pokémon can't use Zap Cannon during your next turn.

flavorText: As it zooms through the sky, this Pokémon seems to be receiving signals of unknown origin, while transmitting signals of unknown purpose.

abilities.name: Duplicate
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may search your deck for another Ditto and switch it with Ditto. (Any cards attached to Ditto, damage counters, Special Conditions, and effects on it are now on the new Pokémon.) If you do, put Ditto on top of your deck. Shuffle your deck afterward. You can't use more than 1 Duplicate Poké-Power each turn.

attacks.name: Energy Link
attacks.text: Search your discard pile for an Energy card and attach it to Ditto.

abilities.name: Super Dynamo
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), if Electrode is your Active Pokémon, you may flip a coin. If heads, choose a Lightning Energy card from your discard pile and attach it to 1 of your Pokémon. This power can't be used if Electrode is affected by a Special Condition.

attacks.name: Swift
attacks.text: This attack's damage isn't affected by Weakness, Resistance, Poké-Powers, Poké-Bodies, or any other effects on the Defending Pokémon.

name: Ninetales

attacks.name: Flame Bash
attacks.text: Flip a coin until you get tails. Search your deck for a number of basic Fire Energy cards up to the number of heads and attach them to any of your Pokémon in any way you like. Shuffle your deck afterward.

attacks.name: Mysterious Flames
attacks.text: If you have more Energy in play than your opponent, the Defending Pokémon is now Burned and Confused.

flavorText: Its nine tails are said to be imbued with a mystic power. It can live for a thousand years.

attacks.name: Child's Punch
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.

attacks.name: One-Two Punch
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 30 damage plus 10 more damage; if tails, this attack does 30 damage.

name: Delibird

attacks.name: Present
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, search your deck for a card and put it into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.

attacks.name: Icy Wind
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon is now Asleep.

flavorText: It carries food all day long. When someone is lost in the mountains, it shares that food.

abilities.name: Intimidating Fang
abilities.text: As long as Arbok is your Active Pokémon, any damage done to your Pokémon by an opponent's attack is reduced by 10 (before applying Weakness and Resistance).

attacks.name: Toxic
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned. Put 2 damage counters instead of 1 on the Defending Pokémon between turns.

abilities.name: Moonglow
abilities.text: The Retreat Cost for each Solrock you have in play is 0.

attacks.name: Foresight
attacks.text: Look at the top 5 cards of either player's deck and put them back on top of that player's deck in any order.

attacks.name: Target Beam
attacks.text: Does 20 damage plus 10 more damage for each Solrock you have in play.

name: Jellicent

abilities.name: Cursed Body
abilities.text: If this Pokémon is your Active Pokémon and is damaged by an opponent's attack (even if this Pokémon is Knocked Out), the Attacking Pokémon is now Confused.

attacks.name: Hydro Pump
attacks.text: Does 20 more damage for each Water Energy attached to this Pokémon.

flavorText: The fate of ships and crew that wander into Jellicent's habitats: all sunken, all lost, all vanished.

name: Volcanion ◇
rules: ◇ (Prism Star) Rule: You can't have more than 1 ◇ card with the same name in your deck. If a ◇ card would go to the discard pile, put it in the Lost Zone instead.

abilities.name: Jet Geyser
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may discard a Water Energy card from your hand. If you do, your opponent switches their Active Pokémon with 1 of their Benched Pokémon.

attacks.name: Sauna Blast
attacks.text: This attack does 20 damage to each of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: It expels its internal steam from the arms on its back. It has enough power to blow away a mountain.

name: Swampert

ancientTrait.name: α Growth
ancientTrait.text: When you attach an Energy card from your hand to this Pokémon (except with an attack, Ability, or Trainer card), you may attach 2 Energy cards.

abilities.name: Diving Search
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may search your deck for a card. Shuffle your deck, then put that card on top of it.

attacks.name: Hydro Pump
attacks.text: This attack does 30 more damage for each Water Energy attached to this Pokémon.

flavorText: It can swim while towing a large ship. It bashes down foes with a swing of its thick arms.

name: Dratini
flavorText: After a 10-hour struggle, a fisherman was able to pull one up and confirm its existence.

name: Cubone

attacks.name: Sharpshooting
attacks.text: This attack does 20 damage to 1 of your opponent's Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: The skull it wears on its head is that of its dead mother. According to some, it will evolve when it comes to terms with the pain of her death.

name: Kingler

attacks.name: Flail
attacks.text: Does 10 damage times the number of damage counters on Kingler.

flavorText: The large pincer has 10,000 horsepower of crushing power. However, its huge size makes it unwieldy to use.

abilities.name: Ancient Wind
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), if Aerodactyl is your Active Pokémon, you may ignore all Poké-Bodies until the end of your turn. This power can't be used if Aerodactyl is affected by a Special Condition.

attacks.name: Rising Lunge
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 damage plus 20 more damage.

name: Mantine

abilities.name: Jumbo Fin
abilities.text: If Mantyke is anywhere under Mantine, the Retreat Cost for each of your Water Pokémon is ColorlessColorless less.

attacks.name: Giant Wave
attacks.text: Mantine can't use Giant Wave during your next turn.

flavorText: When the waves are calm, one may encounter a swarm of Mantine swimming as if they are in flight.

name: Avalugg

attacks.name: Crunch
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, discard an Energy attached to your opponent's Active Pokémon.

attacks.name: Melting Floe
attacks.text: Discard the top 3 cards of your deck. For each Water Energy card you discarded in this way, discard the top 3 cards of your opponent's deck.

flavorText: The way several Bergmite huddle on its back make it look like an aircraft carrier made of ice.

name: Raichu

attacks.name: Thundershock
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

attacks.name: Slam
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 80 damage times the number of heads.

flavorText: Its tail discharges electricity into the ground, protecting it from getting shocked.

name: Lapras-GX
rules: Pokémon-GX rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Collect
attacks.text: Draw 3 cards.

attacks.name: Blizzard Burn
attacks.text: This Pokémon can't attack during your next turn.

attacks.name: Ice Beam-GX
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed. (You can't use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)

name: Bidoof
flavorText: With nerves of steel, nothing can perturb it. It is more agile and active than it appears.

name: Magmortar

attacks.name: Burst Punch
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Burned.

attacks.name: Ground Burn
attacks.text: Each player discards the top card of their deck. This attack does 100 more damage for each Energy card discarded in this way.

flavorText: Magmortar takes down its enemies by shooting fireballs, which burn them to a blackened crisp. It avoids this method when hunting prey.

name: Shining Volcanion

attacks.name: Dual Pump
attacks.text: This attack does 50 damage to 2 of your opponent's Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

attacks.name: Quad Smash
attacks.text: Flip 4 coins. This attack does 50 damage for each heads.

flavorText: It lets out billows of steam and disappears into the dense fog. It's said to live in mountains where humans do not tread.

name: Donphan

attacks.name: Rock Hurl
attacks.text: This attack's damage isn't affected by Resistance.

attacks.name: Double Spin
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 70 damage times the number of heads.

flavorText: It has sharp, hard tusks and a rugged hide. Its tackle is strong enough to knock down a house.

attacks.name: Soothing Scent
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon is now Asleep.

name: Vikavolt

abilities.name: Strong Charge
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may search your deck for a Grass Energy card and a Lightning Energy card and attach them to your Pokémon in any way you like. Then, shuffle your deck.

attacks.name: Electro Cannon
attacks.text: Discard 3 Energy from this Pokémon.

flavorText: It produces electricity via an electrical organ in its abdomen. It overwhelms bird Pokémon with shocking beams of electrical energy.

name: Magmar

attacks.name: Flamethrower
attacks.text: Discard 1 Fire Energy card attached to Magmar in order to use this attack.

flavorText: Its body always burns with an orange glow that enables it to hide perfectly among flames.

attacks.name: Poisonpowder
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned.

name: Hoopa-EX
rules: Pokémon-EX rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

abilities.name: Scoundrel Ring
abilities.text: When you play this Pokémon from your hand onto your Bench, you may search your deck for up to 3 Pokémon-EX (except for Hoopa-EX), reveal them, and put them into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.

attacks.name: Hyperspace Fury
attacks.text: Discard 2 Energy attached to this Pokémon. This attack does 100 damage to 1 of your opponent's Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

name: Samurott

abilities.name: Shell Armor
abilities.text: Any damage done to this Pokémon by attacks is reduced by 20 (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

attacks.name: Hydro Pump
attacks.text: Does 10 more damage for each Water Energy attached to this Pokémon.

flavorText: Part of the armor on its anterior legs becomes a giant sword. Its cry alone is enough to intimidate most enemies.

name: Floatzel

attacks.name: Agility
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, prevent all effects of attacks, including damage, done to this Pokémon during your opponent's next turn.

attacks.name: Aqua Blast
attacks.text: Discard a Water Energy from this Pokémon.

flavorText: Its flotation sac developed as a result of pursuing aquatic prey. It can double as a rubber raft.

abilities.name: Magnetic Call
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may flip a coin. If heads, search your deck for a Metal Basic Pokémon and put it onto your Bench. Shuffle your deck afterward. This power can't be used if Beldum is affected by a Special Condition.

attacks.name: Metal Charge
attacks.text: Put 1 damage counter on Beldum.

name: Alolan Vulpix

abilities.name: Snowed In
abilities.text: As long as this Pokémon is on your Bench, prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks (both yours and your opponent's).

flavorText: If you carelessly approach it because it's cute, the boss of the pack, Ninetales, will appear and freeze you.

name: Lickilicky

attacks.name: Licking Shot
attacks.text: Choose 1 of your opponent's Pokémon. This attack does 10 damage to that Pokémon for each Energy attached to Lickilicky. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

attacks.name: Stick and Absorb
attacks.text: Remove 2 damage counters from Lickilicky. The Defending Pokémon can't retreat during your opponent's next turn.

flavorText: It has space in its throat to store saliva. It can also roll up its tongue and store it in the same spot.

name: Galarian Obstagoon

abilities.name: Wicked Ruler
abilities.text: Once during your turn, you may have your opponent discard cards from their hand until they have 4 cards in their hand.

attacks.name: Knuckle Impact
attacks.text: During your next turn, this Pokémon can't attack.

flavorText: Its voice is staggering in volume. Obstagoon has a tendency to take on a threatening posture and shout—this move is known as Obstruct.

name: Cubchoo
flavorText: Its nose is always running. It sniffs the snot back up because the mucus provides the raw material for its moves.

name: M Gyarados-EX
rules: Mega Evolution rule: When 1 of your Pokémon becomes a Mega Evolution Pokémon, your turn ends.
Pokémon-EX rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Blast Geyser
attacks.text: You may do 20 more damage for each Water Energy attached to this Pokémon. If you do, discard the top 2 cards of your deck.

name: Skiddo

attacks.name: Collect
attacks.text: Draw a card.

flavorText: If it has sunshine and water, it doesn't need to eat, because it can generate energy from the leaves on its back.

attacks.name: Hypnosis
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon is now Asleep.

attacks.name: Ambush
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 10 damage plus 10 more damage.

name: Dark Dragonair

abilities.name: Evolutionary Light
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may search your deck for an Evolution card. Show it to your opponent and put it into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward. This power can't be used if Dark Dragonair is Asleep, Confused, or Paralyzed.

attacks.name: Tail Strike
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 damage plus 20 more damage; if tails, this attack does 20 damage.

flavorText: As is fitting for a legendary Pokémon, it has many strange powers, including the ability to make rain or snow fall.

name: Aurorus

abilities.name: Ice Shield
abilities.text: Any damage done by an opponent's attack to each of your Water Pokémon that has any Water Energy attached to it is reduced by 20 (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

attacks.name: Icy Wind
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Asleep.

flavorText: The diamond-shaped crystals on its body expel air as cold as -240 degrees Fahrenheit, surrounding its enemies and encasing them in ice.

name: Nidoking

attacks.name: Thrash
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 30 damage plus 10 more damage; if tails, this attack does 30 damage and Nidoking does 10 damage to itself.

attacks.name: Toxic
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned. It now takes 20 Poison damage instead of 10 after each player's turn (even if it was already Poisoned).

flavorText: Uses its powerful tail in battle to smash, constrict, then break its prey's bones.

name: Snorlax

attacks.name: Block
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon can't retreat during your opponent's next turn.

attacks.name: Ease Up
attacks.text: Snorlax is now Asleep. If Snorlax is evolved from Munchlax, this attack does 40 damage plus 30 more damage.

flavorText: Its stomach can digest any kind of food, even if it happens to be moldy or rotten.

name: Dewgong

attacks.name: Super Deep Dive
attacks.text: Heal 40 damage from this Pokémon. Switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

attacks.name: Take Down
attacks.text: This Pokémon does 20 damage to itself.

flavorText: Its body is covered with a pure white fur. The colder the weather, the more active it becomes.

name: Weavile

abilities.name: Dark Engage
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may use this power. Each of your Active Pokémon's type is Darkness until the end of your turn. If that Pokémon is no longer your Active Pokémon, this effect ends.

attacks.name: Shadow Charge
attacks.text: Search your deck for up to 2 Darkness Energy cards and attach them to any of your Pokémon in any way you like. Shuffle your deck afterward.

attacks.name: Chip Off
attacks.text: If your opponent has 6 or more cards in his or her hand, discard a number of cards without looking until your opponent has 5 cards left in his or her hand.

flavorText: They live in cold regions, forming groups of four or five that hunt prey with impressive coordination.

name: Reuniclus

attacks.name: Link Fusion
attacks.text: If Solosis is on your Bench, this attack does 30 more damage. If Duosion is on your Bench, this attack does 60 more damage. If Reuniclus is on your Bench, this attack does 90 more damage.

flavorText: When Reuniclus shake hands, a network forms between their brains, increasing their psychic power.

name: Regice

abilities.name: Iceberg Shield
abilities.text: If you have Regirock in play, prevent all effects of attacks, including damage, done to this Pokémon by your opponent's Stage 2 Pokémon.

flavorText: It is said to have slept in a glacier for thousands of years. Its body can't be melted, even by magma.

name: Empoleon

abilities.name: Diving Draw
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may discard 1 card from your hand. If you do, draw 2 cards.

attacks.name: Attack Command
attacks.text: Does 10 damage times the number of Pokémon in play (both yours and your opponent's).

flavorText: If anyone were to hurt its pride, it would slash them with wings that can cleave through an ice floe.

abilities.name: Protect Wing
abilities.text: As long as Staraptor is your Active Pokémon, any damage done by attacks from your opponent's Stage 2 Evolved Pokémon is reduced by 20 (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

attacks.name: Strong Breeze
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, put 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon and all cards attached to it on top of your opponent's deck. Your opponent shuffles his or her deck afterward.

attacks.name: Clutch
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon can't retreat during your opponent's next turn.

name: Champions Festival
rules: Once during each player's turn, if that player has 6 Pokémon in play, he or she may heal 10 damage from each of his or her Pokémon.
This card stays in play when you play it. Discard this card if another Stadium card comes into play. If another card with the same name is in play, you can't play this card.

name: Noctowl

attacks.name: Carry Off
attacks.text: Choose 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. They shuffle that Pokémon and all attached cards into their deck. Then, shuffle this Pokémon and all attached cards into your deck.

flavorText: When it needs to think, it rotates its head 180 degrees to sharpen its intellectual power.

name: Dark Golbat
rules: This Pokémon is both Grass Darkness type.

attacks.name: Night Ambush
attacks.text: Does 30 damage to 1 of your opponent's Pokémon. Dark Golbat can't attack during your next turn. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

name: Jolteon

attacks.name: Pin Missile
attacks.text: Flip 4 coins. This attack does 20 damage times the number of heads.

attacks.name: Electri-Defuse
attacks.text: If the Defending Pokémon is a Pokémon-EX, that Pokémon can't attack during your opponent's next turn.

flavorText: By storing electricity in its body, it can shoot its bristlelike fur like a barrage of missiles.

name: Magikarp

attacks.name: Splashing Dodge
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, prevent all effects of attacks, including damage, done to this Pokémon during your opponent's next turn.

flavorText: Although weak and helpless, this Pokémon is incredibly fertile. They exist in such multitudes, you'll soon grow tired of seeing them.

name: Poliwrath

attacks.name: Submission
attacks.text: Poliwrath does 20 damage to itself.

flavorText: Although an energetic, skilled swimmer that uses all of its muscles, it lives on dry land.

name: Dark Forretress

attacks.name: Armor Up
attacks.text: Until the end of your next turn, if Dark Forretress would be Knocked Out by damage from an attack, flip a coin. If heads, Dark Forretress is not Knocked Out and its remaining HP become 10 instead.

attacks.name: Explosion
attacks.text: Dark Forretress does 60 damage to itself.

flavorText: It stays motionless in trees, driving away with flying armored shards from its hard shell any who come close.

attacks.name: Sleep Powder
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon is now Asleep.

name: Raichu

attacks.name: Spark
attacks.text: Does 20 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: When its electricity builds, its muscles are stimulated, and it becomes more aggressive than usual.

name: Flaaffy

attacks.name: Cotton Guard
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, any damage done to this Pokémon by attacks is reduced by 20 (after applying weakness and resistance).

flavorText: If its coat becomes fully charged with electricity, its tail lights up. It fires hair that zaps on impact.

name: Groudon

attacks.name: Drought
attacks.text: Choose up to 2 basic Fighting Energy cards from your hand and attach them to 1 of your Pokémon.

attacks.name: Major Earthquake
attacks.text: Discard 2 Fighting Energy attached to Groudon and this attack does 10 damage to each of your Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: It had been asleep in underground magma ever since it fiercely fought Kyogre long ago.

name: Corsola

attacks.name: Refresh
attacks.text: Heal 30 damage and remove all Special Conditions from this Pokémon.

attacks.name: Spiny Rush
attacks.text: Flip a coin until you get tails. This attack does 20 more damage for each heads.

flavorText: It continuously sheds and grows. The tip of its head is prized as a treasure because of its beauty.

name: Gallade-EX
rules: Pokémon-EX rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Swift Lunge
attacks.text: You may have your opponent switch his or her Active Pokémon with 1 of his or her Benched Pokémon.

attacks.name: Piercing Prizes
attacks.text: This attack does 20 more damage for each of your remaining Prize cards.

attacks.name: Fury Cutter
attacks.text: Flip 3 coins. If 1 of them is heads, this attack does 10 damage plus 10 more damage. If 2 of them are heads, this attack does 10 damage plus 20 more damage. If all of them are heads, this attack does 10 damage plus 40 more damage.

name: Vaporeon-EX
rules: Pokémon-EX rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Bubble Drain
attacks.text: Heal 30 damage from this Pokémon.

attacks.name: Deep Squall
attacks.text: This attack does 130 damage minus 10 damage for each damage counter on this Pokémon.

name: Cinderace

attacks.name: Flame Cloak
attacks.text: Attach a Fire Energy card from your discard pile to this Pokémon.

attacks.name: Bright Flame
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If tails, discard 2 Energy from this Pokémon.

flavorText: It juggles a pebble with its feet, turning it into a burning soccer ball. Its shots strike opponents hard and leave them scorched.

name: Oshawott
flavorText: It fights using the scalchop on its stomach. In response to an attack, it retaliates immediately by slashing.

attacks.name: Steel Beak
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 damage plus 10 more damage.

attacks.name: Air Cutter
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.

name: Voltorb
flavorText: It was discovered when Poké Balls were introduced. It is said that there is some connection.

attacks.name: Land Sonic
attacks.text: If the Defending Pokémon already has any damage counters on it, the Defending Pokémon is now Confused.

attacks.name: Rest
attacks.text: Remove all Special Conditions and 5 damage counters (all if there are less than 5) from Lairon. Lairon is now Asleep.

attacks.name: Body Slam
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

name: Remoraid

attacks.name: Wild River
attacks.text: Switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

flavorText: Using its dorsal fin as a suction pad, it clings to a Mantine's underside to scavenge for leftovers.

name: Tropical Tidal Wave
rules: Flip a coin. If heads, discard all Trainer cards your opponent has in play. If tails, discard all Trainer (excluding Supporter cards) you have in play.

abilities.name: Energy Jump
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may move an Energy card from 1 of your Pokémon to another of your Pokémon. This power can't be used if Alakazam is affected by a Special Condition.

attacks.name: Psychic
attacks.text: This attack does 30 damage plus 10 more damage for each Energy card attached to the Defending Pokémon.

name: Machamp-EX
rules: Pokémon-EX rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Steaming Mad
attacks.text: This attack does 20 damage times the number of damage counters on this Pokémon. This Pokémon is now Confused.

attacks.name: Crazy Hammer
attacks.text: If this Pokémon is affected by a Special Condition, this attack does 80 more damage. Then, remove all Special Conditions from this Pokémon.

name: Ampharos

abilities.name: Electromagnetic Wall
abilities.text: As long as this Pokémon is your Active Pokémon, whenever your opponent attaches an Energy from his or her hand to 1 of his or her Pokémon, put 3 damage counters on that Pokémon.

attacks.name: Electrobullet
attacks.text: Does 20 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: The tip of its tail shines brightly. In the olden days, people sent signals using the tail's light.

abilities.name: Duplicate
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may search your deck for another Ditto and switch it with Ditto. (Any cards attached to Ditto, damage counters, Special Conditions, and effects on it are now on the new Pokémon.) If you do, put Ditto on top of your deck. Shuffle your deck afterward. You can't use more than 1 Duplicate Poké-Power each turn.

attacks.name: Toxic
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned. Put 2 damage counters instead of 1 on the Defending Pokémon between turns.

name: Swampert

abilities.name: Power Draw
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may discard a card from your hand. If you do, draw 3 cards.

attacks.name: Hydro Pump
attacks.text: This attack does 20 more damage times the amount of Water Energy attached to this Pokémon.

flavorText: It can swim while towing a large ship. It bashes down foes with a swing of its thick arms.

name: Dewott

attacks.name: Aqua Wash
attacks.text: You may put an Energy attached to your opponent's Active Pokémon into their hand.

flavorText: As a result of strict training, each Dewott learns different forms for using the scalchops.

name: Magmar

attacks.name: Smokescreen
attacks.text: If the Defending Pokémon tries to attack during your opponent's next turn, your opponent flips a coin. If tails, that attack does nothing.

attacks.name: Smog
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned.

flavorText: Found at the mouths of volcanoes and extremely hard to spot. There are very few instances of capturing this Pokémon.

name: Steelix

attacks.name: Slam
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 30 damage times the number of heads.

attacks.name: Metal Slash
attacks.text: Steelix can't attack during your next turn.

flavorText: Tempered underground under high pressure and heat, its body is harder than any metal.

name: Vanillish
flavorText: They cool down the surrounding air and create ice particles, which they use to freeze their foes.

attacks.name: Firebreathing
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 10 damage plus 10 more damage; if tails, this attack does 10 damage.

attacks.name: Lava Burst
attacks.text: Discard the top 5 cards of your deck. (If there are fewer than 5 cards in your deck, discard all of them.) This attack does 20 damage for each Fire Energy card you discarded in this way.

name: Gyarados

attacks.name: Venting Anger
attacks.text: This attack does 50 damage for each Magikarp in your discard pile.

attacks.name: Splash Burn
attacks.text: This attack does 30 damage to each of your Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: A tale is told of a town that angered Gyarados. Before the sun rose the next day, flames utterly consumed the town, leaving not a trace behind.

name: Steelix

attacks.name: Outbreak Power
attacks.text: Choose a number of your opponent's Pokémon up to the amount of Energy attached to Steelix. This attack does 20 damage to each of them. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

attacks.name: Iron Tail
attacks.text: Flip a coin until you get tails. This attack does 100 damage times the number of heads.

flavorText: Tempered underground under high pressure and heat, its body is harder than any metal.

name: Sigilyph

abilities.name: Toolbox
abilities.text: This Pokémon may have up to 4 Pokémon Tool cards attached to it. (If this Pokémon loses this Ability, discard Pokémon Tool cards attached to this Pokémon until only 1 Pokémon Tool card remains.)

flavorText: The guardians of an ancient city, they always fly the same route while keeping watch for invaders.

name: Jynx

attacks.name: Freezing Kiss Goodnight
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon is now Asleep.

attacks.name: Strange Dance
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 damage plus 20 more damage and the Defending Pokémon is now Confused. If tails, this attack does 20 damage.

flavorText: It rocks its body rhythmically. It appears to alter the rhythm depending on how it is feeling.

name: Remoraid

attacks.name: Ion Pool
attacks.text: Discard any Stadium card in play.

flavorText: Using its dorsal fin as a suction pad, it clings to a Mantine's underside to scavenge for leftovers.

name: Tympole
flavorText: By vibrating its cheeks, it emits sound waves imperceptible to humans. It uses the rhythm of these sounds to talk.

name: Torterra

attacks.name: Earthquake
attacks.text: Does 10 damage to each of your Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

attacks.name: Frenzy Plant
attacks.text: Torterra can't use Frenzy Plant during your next turn.

flavorText: Groups of this Pokémon migrating in search of water have been mistaken for "moving forests."

abilities.name: Heat Up
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may count the total number of Energy cards attached to all of your Pokémon and all of your opponent's Pokémon. If your opponent has more total Energy cards attached, you may search your deck for 1 Fire Energy card and attach it to 1 of your Benched Pokémon, if any. Shuffle your deck afterward. This power can't be used if Typhlosion is affected by a Special Condition.

attacks.name: Super Singe
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Burned.

abilities.name: Bind Eye
abilities.text: As long as Gliscor is your Active Pokémon, your opponent can't remove any Special Conditions by evolving or devolving his or her Pokémon. (This also includes putting a Pokémon Level-Up card onto that Pokémon.)

attacks.name: Cutting Turn
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, put damage counters on the Defending Pokémon until it is 10HP away from being Knocked Out. If you do, shuffle Gliscor and all cards attached to it back into your deck.

attacks.name: Friction Heat
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon is now Burned.

name: Chandelure

attacks.name: Spirit Burner
attacks.text: Discard the top 5 cards of your deck. This attack does 60 more damage for each Pokémon you discarded in this way. Then, put any number of Fire Pokémon you discarded in this way onto your Bench.

flavorText: Being consumed in Chandelure's flame burns up the spirit, leaving the body behind.

attacks.name: Burning Ball
attacks.text: If Magcargo has at least 2 Fire Energy attached to it, the Defending Pokémon is now Burned.

name: Pikachu

attacks.name: Iron Tail
attacks.text: Flip a coin until you get tails. This attack does 20 damage for each heads.

flavorText: A plan was recently announced to gather many Pikachu and make an electric power plant.

name: Dunsparce

attacks.name: Glare
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

attacks.name: Fade Out
attacks.text: Return Dunsparce and all cards attached to it to your hand.

flavorText: When spotted, this Pokémon escapes backward by furiously boring into the ground with its tail.

name: Luvdisc

attacks.name: Rendezvous Draw
attacks.text: Each player draws and reveals the top card of his or her deck.

attacks.name: Distorted Wave
attacks.text: Before doing damage, remove 1 damage counter from the Defending Pokémon.

flavorText: Its heart-shaped body makes it popular. In some places, you would give a Luvdisc to someone you love.

name: Mr. Mime

abilities.name: Airy Wall
abilities.text: If your opponent's Pokémon that has 2 or less Energy cards attached to it attacks Mr. Mime, prevent all damage done to Mr. Mime from that attack. If Mime Jr. is anywhere under Mr. Mime, prevent all effects of that attack, including damage, done to Mr. Mime.

attacks.name: Trick Play
attacks.text: Put a coin next to your Active Pokémon without showing your opponent and cover it with your hand. Your opponent guesses if the coin is heads or tails. If he or she is wrong, this attack does 50 damage to the Defending Pokémon. If he or she is right, Mr. Mime does 20 damage to itself, and this attack's damage isn't affected by Weakness or Resistance.

flavorText: It is a pantomime expert that can create invisible but solid walls using miming gestures.

name: Psyduck

attacks.name: Confusion Wave
attacks.text: Both Active Pokémon are now Confused.

flavorText: Using psychokinesis gives it a headache, so it normally passes the time spacing out and doing as little as possible.

name: Beldum

attacks.name: Core Beam
attacks.text: Discard a Metal Energy from this Pokémon.

flavorText: Its cells are all magnets. It uses magnetism to communicate with others of its kind.

name: Grovyle

attacks.name: Detect
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, prevent all effects of an attack, including damage, done to Grovyle during your opponent's next turn.

flavorText: It lives in dense jungles. While closing in on its prey, it leaps from branch to branch.

name: Articuno

attacks.name: Find Ice
attacks.text: Search your deck for up to 3 Water Energy cards, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.

flavorText: A legendary bird Pokémon that is said to appear to doomed people who are lost in icy mountains.

name: Incineroar

abilities.name: Strong Cheer
abilities.text: Your Pokémon's attacks do 30 more damage to your opponent's Active Pokémon (before applying Weakness and Resistance). You can't apply more than 1 Strong Cheer Ability at a time.

attacks.name: Flamethrower
attacks.text: Discard an Energy from this Pokémon.

flavorText: Although it's rough mannered and egotistical, it finds beating down unworthy opponents boring. It gets motivated for stronger opponents.

name: Absol
flavorText: The elderly call it the disaster Pokémon and detest it, but interest in its power to predict disasters is on the rise.

name: Nidorino

attacks.name: Double Kick
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 30 damage times the number of heads.

flavorText: An aggressive Pokémon that is quick to attack. The horn on its head secretes a powerful venom.

name: Pikachu

attacks.name: Thunder Shock
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: It raises its tail to check its surroundings. The tail is sometimes struck by lightning in this pose.

name: Chinchou
flavorText: It discharges positive and negative electricity from its antenna tips to shock its foes.

name: Zapdos

attacks.name: Random Spark
attacks.text: This attack does 50 damage to 1 of your opponent's Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

attacks.name: Thundering Hurricane
attacks.text: Flip 4 coins. This attack does 50 damage times the number of heads.

flavorText: A legendary Pokémon that is said to live in thunderclouds. It freely controls lightning bolts.

name: Scraggy

attacks.name: Paralyzing Gaze
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: It immediately headbutts anyone that makes eye contact with it. Its skull is massively thick.

abilities.name: Natural Cure
abilities.text: When you attach a Fire Energy card from your hand to Combusken, remove all Special Conditions from Combusken.

attacks.name: Lunge
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.

name: Snover

attacks.name: Ice Shard
attacks.text: If your opponent's Active Pokémon is a Fighting Pokémon, this attack does 40 more damage.

flavorText: In the spring, it grows berries with the texture of frozen treats around its belly.

name: Wormadam Plant Cloak

abilities.name: Plant Cloak
abilities.text: If Wormadam Plant Cloak has 2 or more Grass Energy attached to it, Wormadam Plant Cloak gets +40 HP.

attacks.name: Leaf Hurricane
attacks.text: If the Defending Pokémon has fewer remaining HP than Wormadam Plant Cloak, the Defending Pokémon is now Confused.

flavorText: When BURMY evolved, its cloak became a part of this Pokémon's body. The cloak is never shed.

name: Croconaw

attacks.name: Screech
attacks.text: Until the end of your next turn, if an attack damages the Defending Pokémon (after applying Weakness and Resistance), that attack does 20 more damage to the Defending Pokémon

attacks.name: Jaw Clamp
attacks.text: Until the end of your opponent's next turn, as long as Croconaw is your Active Pokémon, the Defending Pokémon can't retreat, and if the effect of an attack, Pokémon Power, or Trainer card would change that player's Active Pokémon, that part of the effect does nothing.

flavorText: If it loses a fang, a new one grows back in its place. There are always 48 fangs lining its mouth.

name: Sizzlipede
flavorText: It stores flammable gas in its body and uses it to generate heat. The yellow sections on its belly get particularly hot.

abilities.name: Electro-guard
abilities.text: As long Minun has any Lightning Energy attached to it, Minun has no Weakness.

attacks.name: Quick Attack
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 damage plus 20 more damage.

attacks.name: Drizzle
attacks.text: If you have Water Energy cards in your hand, attach as many Water Energy cards as you like to any of your Active Pokémon.

attacks.name: Max Bubbles
attacks.text: Flip a coin for each Energy attached to all of your Active Pokémon. This attack does 30 damage times the number of heads.

name: Palkia

attacks.name: Tsunami
attacks.text: Does 10 damage to each of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

attacks.name: Water Pulse
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Asleep.

flavorText: A legendary Pokémon of Sinnoh. It is said that space becomes more stable with PALKIA's every breath.

name: Duraludon

attacks.name: Steel Beam
attacks.text: This Pokémon also does 40 damage to itself.

flavorText: Its body resembles polished metal, and it's both lightweight and strong. The only drawback is that it rusts easily.

name: Dewpider

attacks.name: Spider Web
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon can't retreat during your opponent's next turn.

flavorText: When it comes across enemies or potential prey, this Pokémon smashes its water-bubble-covered head into them.

name: Lapras

attacks.name: Errand-Running
attacks.text: Search your deck for a Trainer card, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.

attacks.name: Water Splash
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 more damage.

flavorText: Its high intelligence enables it to understand human speech. It likes to ferry people on its back.

name: Numel

attacks.name: Flare Bonus
attacks.text: Discard a Fire Energy card from your hand. Then, draw 3 cards.

flavorText: The flaming magma it stores in the hump on its back is the source of its tremendous power.

name: Glaceon

attacks.name: Quick Attack
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 30 more damage.

attacks.name: Reflect Energy
attacks.text: Move an Energy from this Pokémon to 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

flavorText: By controlling its body heat, it can freeze the atmosphere around it to make a diamond-dust flurry.

name: Medicham

attacks.name: Channeling
attacks.text: Your opponent draws 2 cards.

attacks.name: Chakra Points
attacks.text: Does 10 damage plus 10 more damage for each card in your opponent's hand.

flavorText: It gains the ability to see the aura of its opponents by honing its mind through starvation.

name: Wigglytuff

abilities.name: Good Night Melody
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may use this power. Each Active Pokémon (both yours and your opponent's) is now Asleep. This power can't be used if Wigglytuff is affected by a Special Condition.

attacks.name: Ballon Attack
attacks.text: If Igglybuff is anywhere under Wigglytuff, this attack does 40 damage plus 20 more damage.

flavorText: Its fine fur feels sublime to the touch. It can expand its body by inhaling air.

name: Scizor

attacks.name: Leer
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon can't attack Scizor during your opponent's next turn. (Benching either Pokémon ends this effect.)

attacks.name: Metal Pincer
attacks.text: Flip a coin until you get tails. This attack does 30 damage plus 10 more damage for each heads.

flavorText: Its wings are not used for flying. They are flapped at high speed to adjust its body temperature.

name: Ludicolo

ancientTrait.name: α Recovery
ancientTrait.text: When this Pokémon is healed, double the amount healed.

attacks.name: Astonish
attacks.text: Choose a random card from your opponent's hand. Your opponent reveals that card and shuffles it into his or her deck.

attacks.name: Splash Dance
attacks.text: During your next turn, this Pokémon's Splash Dance attack does 60 more damage (before applying Weakness and Resistance).

flavorText: The rhythm of bright, festive music activates Ludicolo's cells, making it more powerful.

name: Team Aqua's Great Ball
rules: Search your deck for a Basic Team Aqua Pokémon and a basic Water Energy card, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.
You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

name: Cryogonal

attacks.name: Icy Wind
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon is now Asleep.

attacks.name: Ice Shard
attacks.text: If the Defending Pokémon is a Fighting Pokémon, this attack does 40 more damage.

flavorText: They are born in snow clouds. They use chains made of ice crystals to capture prey.

name: Ursaring

attacks.name: Cross Chop
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 50 damage plus 30 more damage.

flavorText: With its ability to distinguish any aroma, it unfailing finds all food buried deep underground.

name: Championship Arena
rules: This card stays in play when you play it. Discard this card if another Stadium card comes into play. If another card with the same name is in play, you can't play this card.
At the end of each player's turn, if that player has 8 or more cards in his or her hand, that player discards a number of cards until that player has 7 cards left in his or her hand.

name: Dark Golduck
rules: This Pokémon is both Water Darkness type.

attacks.name: Darkness Shield
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, prevent all effects of an attack, including damage, done to each of your Active Pokémon during your opponent's next turn.

attacks.name: Cold Crush
attacks.text: You may discard an Energy card attached to Dark Golduck. If you do, discard an Energy attached to the Defending Pokémon.

name: Snorlax

attacks.name: Pick and Collect
attacks.text: Search your discard pile for up to 4 basic Energy cards, show them to your opponent, and put them into your hand.

attacks.name: Roll Over
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, both Snorlax and the Defending Pokémon are now Asleep. If tails, Snorlax is now Asleep.

flavorText: It stops eating only to sleep. It doesn't feel full unless it eats nearly 900 pounds a day.

name: Dark Electrode

attacks.name: Energy Bomb
attacks.text: Take all Energy cards attached to Dark Electrode and attach them to your Benched Pokémon (in any way you choose). If you have no Benched Pokémon, discard all Energy cards attached to Dark Electrode.

flavorText: Some researchers hypothesize that Electrode may actually be a form of energy instead of having an actual body.

name: Beartic

attacks.name: Sheer Cold
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon can't attack during your opponent's next turn.

attacks.name: Icicle Crash
attacks.text: This attack's damage isn't affected by Resistance.

flavorText: It can make its breath freeze at will. Very able in the water, it swims around in northern seas and catches prey.

name: Dragonair

attacks.name: Dragon Tail
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 60 damage for each heads.

flavorText: From time immemorial, it has been venerated by agricultural peoples as an entity able to control the weather.

name: Nidoqueen

attacks.name: Boyfriends
attacks.text: Does 20 damage plus 20 more damage for each Nidoking you have in play.

flavorText: Its hard scales provide strong protection. It uses its hefty bulk to execute powerful moves.

name: Electrode

abilities.name: Electro Shaker
abilities.text: When you play this Pokémon from your hand to evolve 1 of your Pokémon during your turn, you may move any number of Lightning Energy from your Pokémon to your other Pokémon in any way you like.

flavorText: It explodes in response to even minor stimuli. It is feared, with the nickname of "The Bomb Ball."

name: Raticate

attacks.name: Razor-Sharp Incisors
attacks.text: Does 10 damage times the number of damage counters on the Defending Pokémon.

attacks.name: Gnaw Up
attacks.text: Discard a Special Energy card attached to the Defending Pokémon.

flavorText: Its whiskers help it to maintain balance. Its fangs never stop growing, so it gnaws to pare them down.

name: Espurr

attacks.name: Fake Out
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: The organ that emits its intense psychic power is sheltered by its ears to keep power from leaking out.

name: Wishiwashi

abilities.name: Schooling
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may switch this Pokémon with a Wishiwashi-GX in your hand. Any attached cards, damage counters, Special Conditions, turns in play, and any other effects remain on the new Pokémon.

attacks.name: Sharpshooting
attacks.text: This attack does 10 damage to 1 of your opponent's Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: When it's in trouble, its eyes moisten and begin to shine. The shining light attracts its comrades, and they stand together against their enemies.

name: Venusaur-EX
rules: Pokémon-EX rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Poison Powder
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Poisoned.

attacks.name: Jungle Hammer
attacks.text: Heal 30 damage from this Pokémon.

name: Elekid
rules: If your Active Pokémon is a Baby Pokémon and your opponent announces an attack, your opponent flips a coin (before doing anything else). If tails, your opponent's turn ends.

attacks.name: Energy Kick
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, choose an Energy card attached to the Defending Pokémon. Your opponent moves that card to 1 of his or her other Pokémon. (If your opponent has no Benched Pokémon, this attack does nothing.)

attacks.name: Mud Splash
attacks.text: Does 10 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

name: Crobat

abilities.name: Surprise Bite
abilities.text: When you play this Pokémon from your hand to evolve 1 of your Pokémon, you may put 3 damage counters on 1 of your opponent's Pokémon.

attacks.name: Skill Dive
attacks.text: This attack does 30 damage to 1 of your opponent's Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: It flies so silently through the dark on its four wings that it may not be noticed even when nearby.

attacks.name: Double Kick
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 40 damage times the number of heads.

abilities.name: Temperamental Weather
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may search your deck for Sunny Castform, Rain Castform, or Snow-cloud Castform and switch it with Castform. (Any cards attached to Castform, damage counters, Special Conditions, and effects on it are now on the new Pokémon.) Shuffle Castform back into your deck. You can't use more than 1 Temperamental Weather Poké-Power each turn.

attacks.name: Forecast
attacks.text: Search your deck for a Stadium card and play it. Shuffle your deck afterward.

attacks.name: Extra Ball
attacks.text: If the Defending Pokémon is Pokémon-ex, this attack does 20 damage plus 20 more damage.

name: Oshawott
flavorText: It fights using the scalchop on its stomach. In response to an attack, it retaliates immediately by slashing.

name: Gogoat

attacks.name: Leaf Wallop
attacks.text: During your next turn, this Pokémon's Leaf Wallop attack does 50 more damage (before applying Weakness and Resistance).

flavorText: They inhabit mountainous regions. The leader of the herd is decided by a battle of clashing horns.

abilities.name: Paranoid
abilities.text: As long as Machoke is Confused, Machoke's attacks do 50 more damage to the Defending Pokémon (before applying Weakness and Resistance).

attacks.name: Split Kick
attacks.text: Does 20 damage to each Defending Pokémon.

name: Litwick

attacks.name: Scorch
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Burned.

flavorText: The flame on its head keeps its body slightly warm. This Pokémon takes lost children by the hand to guide them to the spirit world.

name: Clefairy

attacks.name: Lead
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, search your deck for a Supporter card, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

flavorText: They're popular, but they're rare. Trainers who show them off recklessly may be targeted by thieves.

name: Staryu

attacks.name: Quick Blow
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 10 more damage.

flavorText: It appears in large numbers by seashores. At night, its central core flashes with a red light.

attacks.name: Sleep Seed
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon is now Asleep.

attacks.name: Errand-Running
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, you may search your deck for a Trainer card. Show it to your opponent and put it into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.

name: Heatran

abilities.name: Smelt
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may move a Metal Energy attached to 1 of your Pokémon to Heatran. This power can't be used if Heatran is affected by a Special Condition.

attacks.name: Heavy Metal
attacks.text: Flip a coin for each Metal Energy attached to Heatran. This attack does 40 damage plus 20 more damage for each heads.

flavorText: It dwells in volcanic caves. It digs in with its cross-shaped feet to crawl on ceilings and walls.

abilities.name: Call for Help
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), if Team Magma's Mightyena is your Active Pokémon, you may search your deck for a Pokémon with Team Magma in its name, show it to your opponent, and put it into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward. This power can't be used if Team Magma's Mightyena is affected by a Special Condition.

attacks.name: Lunge
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.

name: Dark Haunter

attacks.name: Call Back
attacks.text: Put a Baby Pokémon or Basic Pokémon card from your opponent's discard pile onto his or her Bench. Put 1 damage counter on that Pokémon. (You can't use this attack if your Bench is full.)

attacks.name: Surround
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Asleep. If tails, the Defending Pokémon can't retreat during your opponent's next turn.

flavorText: It silently hunts its prey in dark rooms.

name: Mareep

attacks.name: Thunder Wave
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: It stores lots of air in its soft fur, allowing it to stay cool in summer and warm in winter.

name: Scizor

attacks.name: False Swipe
attacks.text: Does damage equal to half the Defending Pokémon's remaining HP (rounded down to the nearest 10).

attacks.name: Double Claw
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 20 damage times the number of heads.

flavorText: It swings its eye patterned pincers up to scare its foes. This makes it look like it has three heads.

name: M Gallade-EX
rules: Mega Evolution rule: When 1 of your Pokémon becomes a Mega Evolution Pokémon, your turn ends.
Pokémon-EX rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Unwavering Blade
attacks.text: This attack does 30 damage to each of your opponent's Benched Pokémon that has any damage counters on it. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

name: Talonflame

abilities.name: Scorching Feathers
abilities.text: If this Pokémon is in the Active Spot and is damaged by an attack from your opponent's Pokémon (even if this Pokémon is Knocked Out), the Attacking Pokémon is now Burned.

attacks.name: Mach Flight
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, the Defending Pokémon can't retreat.

flavorText: Bird Pokémon make up most of its diet. It approaches at high speeds and smacks them down to the ground with its powerful kick.

abilities.name: Primal Light
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may search your deck for a basic Energy card, show it to your opponent, and put it into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward. This power can't be used if Aerodactyl is affected by a Special Condition.

attacks.name: Granite Head
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, any damage done to Aerodactyl by attacks is reduced by 10 (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

attacks.name: Shining Fingers
