flavorText: There's a hole in its tail that allows it to draw in the air it needs to keep its fire burning. If the hole gets blocked, this Pokémon will fall ill.

abilities.name: Power of Evolution
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), if Electabuzz is an Evolved Pokémon, you may draw a card from the bottom of your deck. This power can't be used if Electabuzz is affected by a Special Condition.

attacks.name: Swift
attacks.text: This attack's damage isn't affected by Weakness, Resistance, Poké-Powers, Poké-Bodies, or any other effects on the Defending Pokémon.

name: Cloyster

attacks.name: Shell Grab
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: Its shell is extremely hard. It cannot be shattered, even with a bomb. The shell opens only when it is attacking.

attacks.name: Super Singe
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Burned.

name: Snorlax
flavorText: It is not satisfied unless it eats over 880 pounds of food every day. When it is done eating, it goes promptly to sleep.

name: Electrike

attacks.name: Quick Turn
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 20 damage times the number of heads.

flavorText: Using electricity stored in its fur, it stimulates its muscles to heighten its reaction speed.

name: Salazzle
flavorText: Salazzle lives deep in caves and forces the Salandit it has attracted with its pheromones to serve it.

name: Joltik
flavorText: Joltik that live in cities have learned a technique for sucking electricity from the outlets in houses.

name: Team Magma's Great Ball
rules: Search your deck for a Basic Team Magma Pokémon and a basic Fighting Energy card, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.
You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

name: Graveler

attacks.name: Double-Edge
attacks.text: This Pokémon does 20 damage to itself.

flavorText: With a free and uncaring nature, it doesn't mind if pieces break off while it rolls down mountains.

name: Zekrom

attacks.name: Mach Claw
attacks.text: This attack's damage isn't affected by Resistance.

attacks.name: Fusion Bolt
attacks.text: If Reshiram is on your Bench, this attack does 40 more damage.

flavorText: This legendary Pokémon can scorch the world with lightning. It assists those who want to build an ideal world.

name: Shaymin LV.X
rules: Put this card onto your Active Shaymin. Shaymin LV.X can use any attack, Poké-Power, or Poké-Body from its previous level.

abilities.name: Revenge Seed
abilities.text: If any of your Grass Pokémon were Knocked Out by damage from an opponent's attack during his or her last turn, each of Shaymin's attacks does 60 more damage to the Active Pokémon (before applying Weakness and Resistance).

attacks.name: Energy Flare
attacks.text: You may move any number of Energy cards attached to your Pokémon to your other Pokémon in any way you like.

name: Pupitar
flavorText: Even sealed in its shell, it can move freely. Hard and fast, it has outstanding destructive power.

attacks.name: Energy Call
attacks.text: Attach 1 Energy card from your discard pile to your Active Pokémon.

attacks.name: Cannonball
attacks.text: Does 30 damage plus 10 more damage for each Energy attached to Delcatty but not used to pay for this attack's Energy cost.

name: Combusken

attacks.name: Bulk Up
attacks.text: During your next turn, each of Combusken's attacks does 30 more damage to the Defending Pokémon (before applying Weakness and Resistance).

attacks.name: Double Kick
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 50 damage times the number of heads.

flavorText: Its kicking mastery lets it loose 10 kicks per second. It emits sharp cries to intimidate foes.

name: Kartana

attacks.name: Divine Paper
attacks.text: If your opponent has exactly 6 Prize cards remaining, this attack does 90 more damage.

flavorText: One of the Ultra Beast life-forms, it was observed cutting down a gigantic steel tower with one stroke of its blade.

name: Glaceon-GX
rules: Pokémon-GX rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

abilities.name: Freezing Gaze
abilities.text: As long as this Pokémon is your Active Pokémon, your opponent's Pokémon-GX and Pokémon-EX in play, in their hand, and in their discard pile have no Abilities, except for Freezing Gaze.

attacks.name: Frost Bullet
attacks.text: This attack does 30 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

attacks.name: Polar Spear-GX
attacks.text: This attack does 50 damage for each damage counter on your opponent's Active Pokémon. (You can't use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)

attacks.name: False Charity
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, look at the top card of your opponent's deck. If it's a Trainer card, put it in your opponent's discard pile; otherwise, put it into his or her hand.

attacks.name: Double Scratch
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 20 damage times the number of heads.

attacks.name: Rapid Spin
attacks.text: Your opponent switches the Defending Pokémon with 1 of his or her Benched Pokémon, if any. You switch Baltoy with 1 of your Benched Pokémon, if any.

name: Froakie

attacks.name: Bounce
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

flavorText: It secretes flexible bubbles from its chest and back. The bubbles reduce the damage it would otherwise take when attacked.

name: M Gengar-EX
rules: Mega Evolution rule: When 1 of your Pokémon becomes a Mega Evolution Pokémon, your turn ends.
Pokémon-EX rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Phantom Gate
attacks.text: Choose 1 of your opponent's Pokémon's attacks and use it as this attack.

name: Dark Magcargo
rules: This Pokémon is both Fire Darkness type.

attacks.name: Energy Press
attacks.text: Does 10 damage plus 10 more damage for each basic Energy card attached to all of your Active Pokémon.

attacks.name: Linear Attack
attacks.text: Choose 1 of your opponent's Pokémon. This attack does 40 damage to that Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

name: Cyndaquil

attacks.name: Smokescreen
attacks.text: If the Defending Pokémon tries to attack during your opponent's next turn, your opponent flips a coin. If tails, that attack doesn't happen.

flavorText: It has a timid nature. If it is startled, the flames on its back burn more vigorously.

name: Vaporeon

abilities.name: Vitality Cheer
abilities.text: Your Pokémon-GX in play that evolve from Eevee get +60 HP. You can't apply more than 1 Vitality Cheer Ability at a time.

attacks.name: Refreshing Rain
attacks.text: Heal 30 damage from each of your Pokémon.

flavorText: Clean, clear waters are its usual habitat. When it's about to be attacked by an invading enemy, it dives into the water to hide.

name: Galarian Darmanitan VMAX
rules: VMAX rule: When your Pokémon VMAX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Max Whiteout
attacks.text: This attack also does 30 damage to each of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

abilities.name: Power Circulation
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may search your discard pile for a basic Energy card, show it to your opponent, and put it on top of your deck. If you do, put 1 damage counter on Sealeo. This power can't be used if Sealeo is affected by a Special Condition.

name: Mr. Mime

abilities.name: Focus Wall
abilities.text: If Mr. Mime would be Knocked Out by damage from an attack that does 70 or more damage (after applying Weakness and Resistance), Mr. Mime is not Knocked Out and its remaining HP becomes 10 instead.

attacks.name: Desperate Slap
attacks.text: If Mr. Mime already has 5 or more damage counters on it, this attack does 20 damage plus 40 more damage.

flavorText: It shapes an invisible wall in midair by minutely vibrating its fingertips to stop molecules in the air.

name: Wailmer

attacks.name: Wave Swallower
attacks.text: Flip a coin until you get tails. For each heads, heal 50 damage from this Pokémon.

flavorText: It shows off by spraying jets of seawater from the nostrils above its eyes. It eats a solid ton of Wishiwashi every day.

name: Magikarp

attacks.name: Flail
attacks.text: This attack does 10 damage times the number of damage counters on this Pokémon.

flavorText: It is virtually worthless in terms of both power and speed. It is the most weak and pathetic Pokémon in the world.

name: Mesprit

abilities.name: Psychic Bind
abilities.text: Once during your turn, when you put Mesprit from your hand onto your Bench, you may use this power. Your opponent can't use any Poké-Powers on his or her Pokémon during your opponent's next turn.

attacks.name: Extrasensory
attacks.text: If you have the same number of cards in your hand as your opponent, this attack does 20 damage plus 50 more damage.

flavorText: Known as "The Being of Emotion." It taught humans the nobility of sorrow, pain, and joy.

name: Manaphy-EX
rules: Pokémon-EX rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

abilities.name: Aqua Tube
abilities.text: Each of your Pokémon that has any Water Energy attached to it has no Retreat Cost.

attacks.name: Mineral Pump
attacks.text: Heal 30 damage from each of your Benched Pokémon.

name: Mareanie

attacks.name: Bail Out
attacks.text: Put a Pokémon from your discard pile into your hand.

flavorText: It plunges the poison spike on its head into its prey. When the prey has weakened, Mareanie deals the finishing blow with its 10 tentacles.

name: Dark Golduck

attacks.name: Third Eye
attacks.text: Discard 1 Energy card attached to Dark Golduck in order to draw up to 3 cards.

flavorText: When Psyducks evolve, their headaches disappear and they find themselves in possession of formidable mental powers.

abilities.name: Duplicate
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may search your deck for another Ditto and switch it with Ditto. (Any cards attached to Ditto, damage counters, Special Conditions, and effects on it are now on the new Pokémon.) If you do, put Ditto on top of your deck. Shuffle your deck afterward. You can't use more than 1 Duplicate Poké-Power each turn.

attacks.name: Copy
attacks.text: Choose 1 of the Defending Pokémon's attacks. Copy copies that attack. This attack does nothing if Ditto doesn't have the Energy necessary to use that attack. (You must still do anything else required for that attack.) Ditto performs that attack.

name: Wormadam Trash Cloak

abilities.name: Trash Cloak
abilities.text: If Wormadam Trash Cloak has a Pokémon Tool card attached to it, any damage done to Wormadam Trash Cloak by attacks is reduced by 20 (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

attacks.name: Iron Tackle
attacks.text: Wormadam Trash Cloak does 20 damage to itself.

flavorText: When BURMY evolved, its cloak became a part of this Pokémon's body. The cloak is never shed.

name: Salazzle

abilities.name: Roast Reveal
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may discard a Fire Energy card from your hand. If you do, draw 3 cards.

flavorText: Salazzle lives deep in caves and forces the Salandit it has attracted with its pheromones to serve it.

name: Vaporeon

attacks.name: Cleanse Away
attacks.text: Remove 2 damage counters from each of your Benched Pokémon.

attacks.name: Hyper Whirlpool
attacks.text: Flip a coin until you get tails. For each heads, discard an Energy card attached to the Defending Pokémon.

flavorText: It has evolved to be suitable for aquatic life. It can invisibly melt away into water.

name: Wobbuffet

attacks.name: Countercharge
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, move all damage counters from Wobbuffet to the Defending Pokémon.

flavorText: It desperately tries to keep its black tail hidden. It is said to be proof the tail hides a secret.

name: Bayleef
flavorText: A spicy aroma emanates from around its neck. The aroma acts as a stimulant to restore health.

abilities.name: Anticipation
abilities.text: Prevent all effects of attacks, excluding damage, done to Toxicroak G.

attacks.name: Deep Poison
attacks.text: If the Defending Pokémon is Poisoned, this attack does 20 damage plus 40 more damage.

name: Zebstrika

attacks.name: Quick Attack
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 more damage.

attacks.name: Shock Bolt
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If tails, discard all Lightning Energy attached to this Pokémon.

flavorText: They have lightning-like movements. When Zebstrika run at full speed, the sound of thunder reverberates.

name: Mareep

attacks.name: Shock Bolt
attacks.text: Discard all Lightning Energy from this Pokémon.

flavorText: It stores lots of air in its soft fur, allowing it to stay cool in summer and warm in winter.

name: Hitmontop

attacks.name: Repeating Kick
attacks.text: Flip a coin until you get tails. This attack does 20 damage times the number of heads.

attacks.name: Rapid Spin
attacks.text: If your opponent has any Benched Pokémon, he or she chooses 1 of them and switches it with his or her Active Pokémon, then, if you have any Benched Pokémon, you switch 1 of them with your Active Pokémon. (Do the damage before switching the Pokémon.)

flavorText: It launches kicks while spinning. If it spins at high speed, it may bore its way into the ground.

abilities.name: Carry Off
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may flip a coin. If heads, look at your opponent's hand. If your opponent has any Baby Pokémon, Basic Pokémon, or Evolution cards there, choose 1 of them. Your opponent shuffles that card into his or her deck. This power can't be used if Crobat is affected by a Special Condition.

attacks.name: Double Cross
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 40 damage times the number of heads. If both of them are tails, the Defending Pokémon is now Confused and Poisoned.

name: Alolan Raichu

abilities.name: Surge Surfer
abilities.text: If there is any Stadium card in play, this Pokémon has no Retreat Cost.

attacks.name: Psychic
attacks.text: This attack does 20 more damage times the amount of Energy attached to your opponent's Active Pokémon.

flavorText: It only evolves to this form in the Alola region. According to researchers, its diet is one of the causes of this change.

abilities.name: Intimidating Pattern
abilities.text: As long as Masquerain is your Active Pokémon, any damage done by an opponent's attack is reduced by 20 (before applying Weakness and Resistance). You can't use more than 1 Intimidating Pattern Poké-Body each turn.

attacks.name: Stun Spore
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

name: Magneton

attacks.name: Tri Attack
attacks.text: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 20 damage times the number of heads.

flavorText: The Magnemite are united by a magnetism so powerful, it dries all moisture in its vicinity.

name: Dark Quilava

attacks.name: Incinerate
attacks.text: Show the top card of your opponent's deck to all players. If it's a Trainer card, discard it.

attacks.name: Rushing Magma
attacks.text: Discard the top 5 cards of your deck. (If there are fewer than 5 cards in your deck, discard all of them.) This attack does 20 damage for each Fire Energy card you discarded in this way.

flavorText: If it turns its back to an opponent, it is a sign that it is getting ready to attack.

name: Magneton

attacks.name: Zap Cannon
attacks.text: This Pokémon can't use Zap Cannon during your next turn.

flavorText: It has about three times the electrical power of Magnemite. For some reason, outbreaks of this Pokémon happen when lots of sunspots appear.

name: Regirock

attacks.name: Unyielding Rock
attacks.text: If your opponent's Active Pokémon is a Pokémon-EX, this attack does 60 more damage.

flavorText: The same rocks that form its body have been found in ground layers around the world.

name: Seadra

attacks.name: Water Gun
attacks.text: Does 20 damage plus 10 more damage for each Water Energy attached to Seadra but not used to pay for this attack's Energy cost. You can't add more than 20 damage in this way.

attacks.name: Agility
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, during your opponent's next turn, prevent all effects of attacks, including damage, done to Seadra.

flavorText: Capable of swimming backward by rapidly flapping its wing-like pectoral fins and stout tail.

name: Rhydon

attacks.name: Ram
attacks.text: Rhydon does 20 damage to itself. Your opponent switches the Defending Pokémon with 1 of his or her Benched Pokémon, if any. (Do the damage before switching the Pokémon. Switch the Pokémon even if Rhydon is Knocked Out.)

flavorText: Protected by an armor-like hide, it is capable of living in molten lava of 3600 degrees.

name: Scrafty

attacks.name: Corner
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, the Defending Pokémon can't retreat.

attacks.name: Bad Brawl
attacks.text: If you played Piers from your hand during this turn, this attack does 90 more damage.

flavorText: As halfhearted as this Pokémon's kicks may seem, they pack enough power to shatter Conkeldurr's concrete pillars.

name: Tapu Koko

attacks.name: Flying Flip
attacks.text: This attack does 20 damage to each of your opponent's Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: This guardian deity of Melemele is brimming with curiosity. It summons thunderclouds and stores their lightning inside its body.

attacks.name: Aqua Sonic
attacks.text: This attack's damage isn't affected by Resistance.

abilities.name: Poison Pollen
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned. This power can't be used if Vileplume is affected by a Special Condition.

attacks.name: Petal Dance
attacks.text: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 30 damage times the number of heads. Vileplume is now Confused.

name: Furfrou

attacks.name: Tight Jaw
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: Trimming its fluffy fur not only makes it more elegant but also increases the swiftness of its movements.

abilities.name: Fast Feet
abilities.text: Dodrio can retreat even when it is Asleep or Paralyzed.

attacks.name: Retaliate
attacks.text: Does 10 damage times the number of damage counters on Dodrio.

attacks.name: Slashing Strike
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If tails, Dodrio can't use Slashing Strike during your next turn.

name: Reuniclus

attacks.name: Barrier Attack
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, any damage done to this Pokémon by attacks is reduced by 30 (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

attacks.name: Telekinesis of Nobility
attacks.text: Switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

flavorText: They use psychic power to control their arms, which are made of a special liquid. They can crush boulders psychically.

name: Whiscash

attacks.name: Amnesia
attacks.text: Choose 1 of your opponent's Active Pokémon's attacks. That Pokémon can't use that attack during your opponent's next turn.

attacks.name: Rising Lunge
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 30 more damage.

flavorText: It makes its nest at the bottom of swamps. It will eat anything—if it is alive, Whiscash will eat it.

name: Glalie-EX
rules: Pokémon-EX rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Ice Breath
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

attacks.name: Instant Freeze
attacks.text: If you have the same number of cards in your hand as your opponent, this attack does 100 more damage.

name: Exeggutor

attacks.name: Teleport
attacks.text: Switch Exeggutor with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

attacks.name: Big Eggsplosion
attacks.text: Flip a number of coins equal to the number of Energy attached to Exeggutor. This attack does 20 damage times the number of heads.

flavorText: Legend has it that on rare occasions, one of its heads will drop off and continue on as an Exeggcute.

name: Vileplume

abilities.name: Heal
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may flip a coin. If heads, remove 1 damage counter from 1 of your Pokémon. This power can't be used if Vileplume is Asleep, Confused, or Paralyzed.

attacks.name: Petal Dance
attacks.text: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 40 damage times the number of heads. Vileplume is now Confused (after doing damage).

flavorText: The larger its petals, the more toxic pollen it contains. Its big head is heavy and hard to hold up.

name: Jolteon-EX
rules: Pokémon-EX rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Swift
attacks.text: This attack's damage isn't affected by Weakness, Resistance, or any other effects on your opponent's Active Pokémon.

attacks.name: Flash Ray
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks from Basic Pokémon.

name: Panpour
flavorText: The water stored inside the tuft on its head is full of nutrients. It waters plants with it using its tail.

name: Ducklett
flavorText: They are better at swimming than flying, and they happily eat their favorite food, peat moss, as they dive underwater.

name: Blastoise-EX
rules: Pokémon-EX rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Rapid Spin
attacks.text: Switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon. Then, your opponent switches his or her Active Pokémon with 1 of his or her Benched Pokémon.

attacks.name: Splash Bomb
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If tails, this Pokémon does 30 damage to itself.

attacks.name: Big Eggsplosion
attacks.text: Flip a number of coins equal to the amount of Energy attached to Exeggutor. This attack does 20 damage times the number of heads.

attacks.name: Lateral Eggsplosion
attacks.text: Flip a number of coins equal to the amount of Energy attached to all of of your Benched Pokémon. This attack does 30 damage plus 10 more damage for each heads.

name: Maractus

attacks.name: Mega Drain
attacks.text: Heal 20 damage from this Pokémon.

attacks.name: Pin Missile
attacks.text: Flip 4 coins. This attack does 20 damage times the number of heads.

flavorText: It uses an up-tempo song and dance to drive away the bird Pokémon that prey on its flower seeds.

name: Galvantula

attacks.name: Double Thread
attacks.text: This attack does 30 damage to 2 of your opponent's Pokémon. Also apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.

attacks.name: Electroweb
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon can't retreat during your opponent's next turn.

flavorText: When attacked, they create an electric barrier by spitting out many electrically charged threads.

name: Articuno ex
rules: When Pokémon-ex has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

abilities.name: Synchronized Lift
abilities.text: As long as you have Zapdos ex and Moltres ex in play, the Retreat Cost for Articuno ex is 0.

attacks.name: Ice Gift
attacks.text: You may move a Water Energy attached to Articuno ex to 1 of your Pokémon.

attacks.name: Freezing Wing
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Asleep.

name: Mantine

attacks.name: Group Swim
attacks.text: Search your deck for a Water Pokémon, show it to your opponent, and put it into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.

attacks.name: Aqua Slash
attacks.text: Mantine can't attack during your next turn.

flavorText: As it majestically swims, it doesn't care if Remoraid attach to it for scavenging its leftovers.

attacks.name: Doubleslap
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 10 damage times the number of heads.

attacks.name: Rest
attacks.text: Remove all Special Conditions and 3 damage counters from Jigglypuff (all if there are less than 3). Jigglypuff is now Asleep.

name: Cubchoo

attacks.name: Hail
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 10 damage to each of your opponent's Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: Their snot is a barometer of health. When healthy, their snot is sticky and the power of their ice moves increases.

name: Haunter

attacks.name: Shadow Bind
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon can't retreat during your opponent's next turn.

flavorText: It likes to lurk in the dark and tap shoulders with a gaseous hand. Its touch causes endless shuddering.

name: Jolteon-EX
rules: Pokémon-EX rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Swift
attacks.text: This attack's damage isn't affected by Weakness, Resistance, or any other effects on your opponent's Active Pokémon.

attacks.name: Flash Ray
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks from Basic Pokémon.

name: Luxio

attacks.name: Quick Turn
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 20 damage times the number of heads.

flavorText: Strong electricity courses through the tips of its sharp claws. A light scratch causes fainting in foes.

name: Frogadier

attacks.name: Lick
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: It can throw bubble-covered pebbles with precise control, hitting empty cans up to a hundred feet away.

name: Furfrou

attacks.name: Tight Jaw
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: Trimming its fluffy fur not only makes it more elegant but also increases the swiftness of its movements.

name: Croconaw

attacks.name: Big Bite
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon can't retreat during your opponent's next turn.

flavorText: If it loses a fang, a new one grows back in its place. There are always 48 fangs lining its mouth.

name: Slowpoke & Psyduck-GX
rules: TAG TEAM rule: When your TAG TEAM is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Ditch and Splash
attacks.text: Discard any number of Supporter cards from your hand. This attack does 40 damage for each card you discarded in this way.

attacks.name: Thrilling Times-GX
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 100 more damage. If this Pokémon has at least 6 extra Water Energy attached to it (in addition to this attack's cost), flip 10 coins instead, and this attack does 100 more damage for each heads. (You can't use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)

name: Cyndaquil
flavorText: It has a timid nature. If it is startled, the flames on its back burn more vigorously.

name: Simipour

attacks.name: Aqua Reflect
attacks.text: Move a Water Energy from this Pokémon to 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

flavorText: The high-pressure water expelled from its tail is so powerful, it can destroy a concrete wall.

name: Alomomola
flavorText: Floating in the open sea is how they live. When they find a wounded Pokémon, they embrace it and bring it to shore.

attacks.name: Confuse Ray
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Confused.

attacks.name: Double-edge
attacks.text: Volbeat does 10 damage to itself.

attacks.name: Agility
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, prevent all effects of an attack, including damage, done to Seadra during your opponent's next turn.

abilities.name: Duplicate
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may search your deck for another Ditto and switch it with Ditto. (Any cards attached to Ditto, damage counters, Special Conditions, and effects on it are now on the new Pokémon.) If you do, put Ditto on top of your deck. Shuffle your deck afterward. You can't use more than 1 Duplicate Poké-Power each turn.

attacks.name: Thunderbolt
attacks.text: Discard all Energy cards attached to Ditto.

name: Nidoran ♂

attacks.name: Come Along
attacks.text: Search your deck for Nidoran ♀ and put it onto your Bench. Shuffle your deck afterward.

flavorText: Its large ears are flapped like wings when it is listening to distant sounds. It extends toxic barbs when angered.

attacks.name: Charge
attacks.text: Attach a Lightning Energy card from your discard pile to Electrike.

attacks.name: Thunder Jolt
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If tails, Electrike does 10 damage to itself.

name: Wynaut

abilities.name: Baby Evolution
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may put Wobbuffet from your hand onto Wynaut (this counts as evolving Wynaut) and remove all damage counters from Wynaut.

attacks.name: Astonish
attacks.text: Choose 1 card from your opponent's hand without looking. Look at the card you chose, then have your opponent shuffle that card into his or her deck.

flavorText: It grows strong by pushing up against others en masse. It loves eating sweet fruit.

name: Alolan Geodude

attacks.name: Defense Curl
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks during your opponent's next turn.

flavorText: Its body is a magnetic stone. Iron sand attaches firmly to the portions of its body that are particularly magnetic.

name: Marowak

attacks.name: Bonemerang
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 60 damage times the number of heads.

attacks.name: Bone Impact
attacks.text: If there is any Stadium card in play, this attack does 20 damage plus 60 more damage. Discard that Stadium card.

flavorText: It has been seen pounding boulders with the bone it carries in order to tap out messages to others.

name: Raikou

attacks.name: Booming Thunder
attacks.text: Attach a Lightning Energy card from your discard pile to 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

flavorText: The rain clouds it carries let it fire thunderbolts at will. They say that it descended with lightning.

name: Dark Jolteon

attacks.name: Lightning Flash
attacks.text: If the Defending Pokémon tries to attack during your opponent's next turn, your opponent flips a coin. If tails, that attack does nothing.

attacks.name: Thunder Attack
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed. If tails, Dark Jolteon does 10 damage to itself.

flavorText: A dangerous Pokémon when angered. It likes to playfully shock people with the electricity it stores within its body.

attacks.name: Poison Sting Tackle
attacks.text: Giovanni's Nidorina does 20 damage to itself. Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned.

attacks.name: Body Slam
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

name: Victory Cup
rules: Flip a coin. If heads, search your deck for a Pokémon, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.
You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

name: Zacian

attacks.name: Crunch
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, discard an Energy from your opponent's Active Pokémon.

attacks.name: Metal Blade
attacks.text: Discard 3 Energy from this Pokémon.

flavorText: Now armed with a weapon it used in ancient times, this Pokémon needs only a single strike to fell even Gigantamax Pokémon.

name: Luxio

attacks.name: Discharge
attacks.text: Discard all Lightning Energy attached to Luxio. Flip a coin for each Energy card you discarded. This attack does 40 damage times the number of heads.

flavorText: Strong electricity courses through the tips of its sharp claws. A light scratch causes fainting in foes.

name: Golem

attacks.name: Stone Edge
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 30 more damage.

attacks.name: Explosion
attacks.text: This Pokémon does 100 damage to itself.

flavorText: It tumbles down mountains, leaving grooves from peak to base. Stay clear of these grooves.

name: Pelipper

attacks.name: Courier
attacks.text: Put 1 of your Benched Pokémon and all cards attached to it into your hand.

attacks.name: Fly
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing. If heads, prevent all effects of attacks, including damage, done to this Pokémon during your opponent's next turn.

flavorText: Gathering food is the work of young males. They store food in their capacious beaks and carry it back to others waiting in the nest.

name: Manectric

attacks.name: Energy Crush
attacks.text: Does 20 damage times the amount of Energy attached to all of your opponent's Pokémon.

attacks.name: Flash Impact
attacks.text: Does 20 damage to 1 of your Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: It discharges electricity from its mane. It creates a thundercloud overhead to drop lightning bolts.

name: Wartortle

attacks.name: Withdraw
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, prevent all damage done to Wartortle during your opponent's next turn. (Any other effects of attacks still happen.)

flavorText: Often hides in water to stalk unweary prey. When swimming quickly, it moves its ears to maintain balance.

name: Regigigas

attacks.name: Drag Off
attacks.text: Before doing damage, you may choose 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon and switch it with the Defending Pokémon.

attacks.name: Giga Hammer
attacks.text: Regigigas can't use Giga Hammer during your next turn.

flavorText: There is an enduring legend that states this Pokémon towed continents with ropes.

attacks.name: Thundershock
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

name: Metapod

abilities.name: Green Shield
abilities.text: Each of your Grass Pokémon has no Weakness.

flavorText: It prepares for evolution by hardening its shell as much as possible to protect its soft body.

name: Wobbuffet

abilities.name: Bide Barricade
abilities.text: If this Pokémon is your Active Pokémon, each Pokémon in play, in each player's hand, and in each player's discard pile has no Abilities (except for Psychic Pokémon).

attacks.name: Psychic Assault
attacks.text: This attack does 10 more damage for each damage counter on your opponent's Active Pokémon.

flavorText: It hates light and shock. If attacked, it inflates its body to build up its counterstrike.

name: Beartic

attacks.name: Powerful Rage
attacks.text: Does 20 damage times the number of damage counters on this Pokémon.

attacks.name: Glacier Drop
attacks.text: Discard the top card of your opponent's deck.

flavorText: They love the cold seas of the north. They create pathways around the ocean waters by freezing their own breath.

name: Wailord

attacks.name: Dwindling Wave
attacks.text: This attack does 40 less damage for each damage counter on this Pokémon.

flavorText: Its immense size is the reason for its popularity. Wailord watching is a favorite sightseeing activity in various parts of the world.

abilities.name: Ice Wall
abilities.text: Any damage done to Glalie by attacks from your opponent's Pokémon with any Special Energy cards attached to it is reduced by 40 (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

attacks.name: Heavy Blizzard
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, put 1 damage counter on each of your opponent's Benched Pokémon.

name: Team Magma's Secret Base
rules: Whenever any player puts a Basic Pokémon (except for Team Magma Pokémon) from his or her hand onto his or her Bench, put 2 damage counters on that Pokémon.
This card stays in play when you play it. Discard this card if another Stadium card comes into play. If another card with the same name is in play, you can't play this card.

name: Trubbish

attacks.name: Venoshock
attacks.text: If your opponent's Active Pokémon is Poisoned, this attack does 50 more damage.

flavorText: Its favorite places are unsanitary ones. If you leave trash lying around, you could even find one of these Pokémon living in your room.

abilities.name: Galactic Switch
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may move an Energy card attached to 1 of your Pokémon SP to another of your Pokémon. Then, put 2 damage counters on Bronzong G. This power can't be used if Bronzong G is affected by a Special Condition.

attacks.name: Psychic Pulse
attacks.text: Does 10 damage to each of your opponent's Benched Pokémon that has any damage counters on it. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

attacks.name: Lick
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

attacks.name: Tongue Whip
attacks.text: Choose 1 of your opponent's Pokémon. This attack does 20 damage to that Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

name: Electivire

attacks.name: Tag Team Spark
attacks.text: This attack does 20 more damage for each Energy attached to your Magmortar.

attacks.name: Gigavolt
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 30 more damage. If tails, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: As its electric charge amplifies, blue sparks begin to crackle between its horns.

name: Farfetch'd

attacks.name: Leek Slap
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing. Either way, you can't use this attack again as long as Farfetch'd stays in play (even putting Farfetch'd on the Bench won't let you use it again).

flavorText: The sprig of green onions it holds is its weapon. This sprig is used much like a metal sword.

name: Whiscash

ancientTrait.name: α Growth
ancientTrait.text: When you attach an Energy card from your hand to this Pokémon (except with an attack, Ability, or Trainer card), you may attach 2 Energy cards.

attacks.name: Earthquake
attacks.text: This attack does 20 damage to each of your Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: It makes its nest at the bottom of swamps. It will eat anything—if it is alive, Whiscash will eat it.

name: Emolga

attacks.name: Electrichain
attacks.text: Does 20 more damage if you have any Lightning Pokémon on your Bench.

flavorText: They live on treetops and glide using the inside of a cape-like membrane while discharging electricity.

attacks.name: Rock Hurl
attacks.text: This attack's damage isn't affected by Resistance.

attacks.name: Double Spin
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 50 damage times the number of heads.
